properties.title: Elevate your Career Leveraging The Specialist Pipeline
properties.description:
***Back by popular demand***
Advancing your career as a specialist is not a matter of getting that next certification or achieving a higher-level expertise in a particular field, there is more to it than that.

What does your career path look like? What are your options when it comes to developing your career? For many this means becoming a people manager. The question is how do you develop your career without transitioning into management? Leveraging the framework of The Specialist Pipeline, we will define four dimensions to bring value to organizations that you can hone to elevate your career.

Join us for this interactive workshop where you will self assess and define your path forward to grow your career.

properties.title: The Founder Signal: Stories & Content for Growth | Panel & Studio Sessions
properties.description:
You can now purchase a VIP ticket to guarantee a seat, or join the waitlist if space becomes available.
Limited time slots are also available for the founder interview recordings, which will be booked in advance (a link will be sent to those who register). See the schedule below.

Most content gets ignored — but raw stories, strong perspectives, and real signal helps founders and startups break through. Join a candid panel on founder-led marketing, plus sessions to record your own story.
The Founder Signal is a free event hosted by Clarity Content as part of Toronto Tech Week, where we'll discuss what actually makes founder-led marketing work now — not more content for content’s sake, but sharper stories, stronger perspective, and a clearer signal in a noisy market.
Join us for a panel discussion on founder stories, content, and growth, plus the opportunity to step in front of the camera yourself.
Founders will have access to free content sessions to record a short story or insight. Clarity Content will edit and publish the final videos as part of the experience (follow along on IG, LinkedIn, YouTube).
Schedule
3 pm — 5:30 pm | Content Sessions (Pre-Booked)
5 pm — 5:45 pm | Networking
6 pm — 7 pm | Panel Discussion and Q&A
On the Panel
Get a wide range of perspectives and insights from Sarah Bartnicka (Founder & Editor of Milk Bag), Ahmed Shafik (Co-Founder of Venn), Sarah Stockdale (Founder & CEO of Growclass), and Alaina Booth (Creative Lead @ Stan).


This event is perfect for founders, operators, and leaders who are tired of bland brand content, tired of sounding like everyone else, and ready to show the thinking behind what they’re building.
Those breaking through right now are not just louder, they are clearer, more human and more believable. They turn founder insight into trust, attention, and momentum.
Less posturing, less noise, and more signal. Content that compounds.
Clarity Content turns founder expertise into high-leverage content assets, helping founders and leaders to build trust, grow their presence, and attract opportunities.

properties.title: Anchored In Innovation - Agentic AI, Data Architecture & Governance
properties.description:
This ‘Anchored In Innovation Executive Session’ is designed for senior technology leaders responsible for enabling AI-driven transformation while maintaining operational stability, trust, and control.
As organizations move beyond experimentation into AI systems that can plan, decide, and act, technology executives sit at the center of a rapidly expanding mandate—balancing innovation velocity with security, governance, and enterprise risk.
This executive technology briefing session provides a practical, executive‑level forum to explore how Agentic AI, modern data architecture, and AI governance must evolve together to support scalable, defensible adoption.
This session is deliberately non‑vendor, non‑theoretical, and grounded in real operating environments.
This session is deliberately non vendor, non theoretical, and grounded in real operating environments.
Agentic AI in Practice
• What distinguishes Agentic AI from GenAI, RPA, and traditional automation
• Where enterprises are realistically deploying agents today
• Early signals that indicate whether an initiative can scale safely
Data Architecture as an Enabler (and Governor)
Technology executives will explore:
• Why agentic systems are only as reliable as their underlying data architecture
• Architectural controls for:
o Data quality and validation
o Lineage, traceability, and explainability
o Context management and grounding
• Patterns that allow innovation without sacrificing operability or auditability
Engineering Governance into the Stack
Moving governance “left” into solution design, including:
• Embedding guardrails into platforms rather than relying on downstream controls
• Integrating AI governance with existing SDLC, platform, and risk practices
• Defining clear ownership for agent behaviour, outcomes, and failures
• Designing for human in the loop and human on the loop oversight where it matters
Security, Resilience & Trust
• How agentic AI expands the attack surface across data pipelines and orchestration layers
• Risks introduced by tool calling agents and autonomous decision flows
• Practical controls to preserve trust without slowing teams down
Format
• Closed door, peer level executive session
• Facilitated discussion with shared problem solving
• Designed to surface patterns, pitfalls, and pragmatic solutions
The session prioritizes technology leader realities—budget pressure, constrained talent, legacy platforms, and rising expectations from boards and regulators.
Outcomes for Technology Executives
Participants will leave with:
• A clearer mental model of where Agentic AI belongs (and doesn’t)
• Reference architecture patterns for enabling autonomous systems safely
• Practical governance models aligned to engineering and platform teams
• Better questions to ask vendors, architects, and delivery teams
• Peer validated insights from leaders facing similar challenges
Why Anchoram Consulting / Harry Cheema
Anchoram Consulting works with organizations operating in regulated, security sensitive, and mission critical environments, specializing in:
• AI governance and responsible AI implementation
• Data architecture and digital strategy
• Cybersecurity and enterprise risk
• Complex transformation under operational constraints
Harpreet (Harry) Cheema brings hands on experience advising technology leaders where failure is not an option, translating governance and risk into architectural and operational decisions that teams can actually implement.
This session is particularly well suited for:
• CIOs, CTOs, and CDOs
• Heads of Engineering, Architecture, and Platforms
• CISOs and technology risk leaders
• Digital and transformation executives preparing for AI scale
Space is limited and there will be a waitlist for this highly anticipated event.

properties.title: Women Who Build: A Toronto Tech Week Fireside
properties.description:
A curated gathering of women founders, operators, and emerging leaders for a candid fireside with accomplished women in tech —career growth, leadership, and the realities of building in this ecosystem, told without the LinkedIn filter.

Then: structured networking that goes somewhere. Conversation prompts that skip the surface, easy drinks and small bites. Leave with one relationship or insight that wasn’t on your calendar this morning.

Fireside Guest:
We’re joined by Upasana Sharma, a leader at TiE (one of the world’s largest entrepreneurship networks) bringing a rare perspective on what it takes to build, scale, and lead in tech across borders and industries.
Expect an honest conversation, not a rehearsed one.

Who this is for:
Women building, leading, or operating in tech: early-stage founders, product and growth operators, engineers and designers stepping into leadership, and emerging leaders looking for the room they haven’t found yet. If you’re past the introductory networking circuit and want conversations with substance, you’re in the right place.

Why this one:
Toronto Tech Week is full of panels, pitches, and crowded mixers. This isn’t that. The room is small by design, the guest list is curated, and the format is built for real conversation rather than business card exchanges. You’ll hear stories that don’t get told on stage, meet women operating at your level or one step ahead, and leave with something useful —a sharper perspective, a new contact, or the answer to something you’ve been sitting with.

One evening. The right room. Conversations worth having.

properties.title: Beyond the Bubble: An Intimate Chat with Will Hsu and Alex Norman
properties.description:
The biggest seed-stage wins of the last decade didn't come from Sand Hill Road.
Honey - sold to PayPal for $4 billion - was built in LA. ServiceTitan - $9 billion Nasdaq IPO - was built in Glendale, CA. AuditBoard - acquired by Hg for $3 billion - was built in Cerritos, CA.
All three companies were backed in their first institutional rounds by William Hsu of Mucker Capital.
William and his co-founder, Erik Rannala, built Mucker on a clear thesis: great founders are everywhere, but most venture capital firms are only looking in one place. Today, Mucker operates from Los Angeles, Austin, Chicago, Toronto, and New York.
Before becoming an investor, he lived it. He founded BuildPoint (acquired by Roper Technologies) and ran product as CPO at AT&T Interactive. He's not a career VC. He's a founder who became one.
He also doesn't do the speaking circuit. When he sits down for a conversation, it's in rooms like this - curated and off the record.
Alex Norman, partner at N49P, leads the conversation on what Will looks for in founders, why geography is a feature not a limitation, and where he thinks the next breakout companies are coming from.
Who is this for:
This session is for high growth promising founders, operators and ecosystem innovators who are shaping what Toronto's tech community becomes.
Seats are limited to maintain the intimate feel of the event. Attendance is by application and approval only.

Thanks To Our Partners

Co-organized with the Schwartz Reisman Innovation Campus - U of T  Ecosystem team, connecting innovators and leaders shaping the future of tech.

TechTO is Canada’s largest community of tech builders - connecting 60,000+ founders, operators, and investors through year-round meetups, curated dinners, peer tables, and media storytelling. Interested in learning more? techto.org. For sponsorship inquiries, reach out to partnerships@techto.org.

properties.title: Clio Behind the Build: Ruby X AI Live Demos
properties.description:
Toronto's best AI builders are taking the stage and we want you in the room.
Apply to Demo — submissions close May 8th at 11:59 PM EST.
Clio is partnering with Toronto Ruby to showcase the cutting edge of the Ruby AI ecosystem. This is an event for the engineers, the builders, and the innovators who are shaping what development looks like in the age of AI.
As the global leader in legal AI, trusted by over 400,000 legal professionals worldwide, Clio built the Intelligent Legal Work Platform — a system of action powered by the world's largest legal data library, designed for a profession where accuracy and real outcomes aren't optional. That standard runs through everything we build. AI shapes how our engineers write, ship, and scale code every day.
We’re opening our doors to the engineers who are holding themselves to that same high bar of craftsmanship.
The Format: Pure Technical Showcase
We’re looking for 10 of the most innovative builders to take the stage for a live technical showcase.
Show the Stack: Show us your IDE, your terminal, or your architectural diagrams.
The Q&A: After each 5-minute demo, our Engineering Leadership will step in with a question—asking the deep, technical questions about scale, latency, and trade-offs that only a peer would ask.
The Run of Show
5:00 PM - Doors Open: Registration, food/beverages, and networking.
5:45 PM - Kickoff: Opening remarks from Clio’s CTO and a welcome from Toronto Ruby.
6:00 PM - Demos: Rapid-fire technical showcases with live Q&A from Clio’s Engineering Leadership.
7:00 PM - Open Floor: Connect directly with our Engineering and Product leadership, the Toronto Ruby community, and your fellow innovators.
Want to Demo?
Applications close May 8th. If you’ve built something that pushes the boundaries of Ruby and AI, whether it’s a custom LLM bridge, a unique agentic workflow, or a breakthrough in Ruby-based data processing, we want to see it. Apply to Demo
Want to attend? Space is limited so RSVP via Luma today, to secure your spot in the room where the real building happens.

properties.title: BCCC PowerTech Roundtable 2026
properties.description:
Event Overview
Canada and Brazil share strong innovation ecosystems and growing opportunities for collaboration across technology, research and entrepreneurship.
The BCCC PowerTech Roundtable 2026 brings together senior leaders from government, academia and industry to explore how the two countries can further strengthen bilateral ties in applied research, technology commercialization, talent integration and intellectual property governance.
Hosted during Toronto Tech Week at Toronto Metropolitan University, this executive-level dialogue will focus on practical pathways for cross-border expansion, nearshore innovation and strategic market diversification.
Attendees
This event welcomes professionals, executives, entrepreneurs, investors, academics, and institutions from the Technology and Education sectors, as well as organizations whose business activities connect with innovation, digital transformation, talent development, research, or international collaboration.
It is also an excellent opportunity for those interested in learning more about how the Brazil-Canada Chamber of Commerce (BCCC) is helping strengthen the bilateral innovation and education ecosystem between Brazil and Canada.
Organizer
Promoted by the Brazil-Canada Chamber of Commerce (BCCC), and supported by Toronto Metropolitan University, Science Discovery Zone, Canada Starthub and Prima IP, the roundtable is designed to foster meaningful executive dialogue and strengthen long-term bilateral alignment.

properties.title: 5K Run + Coffee in matched small groups by LYTT
properties.description:
Toronto Tech Week is a citywide celebration of the people building what’s next. From May 26–29, 2026, founders, investors, and builders come together for hundreds of community-led events across Toronto, connecting tens of thousands of people around Canadian tech. Torontotechweek.com.

A different way to meet people during Toronto Tech Week.
Heads up: this isn't a typical event. There's no single venue and no one big group. We'll match you into a small group of 6 to 8 based on what you're working on and who you want to meet. Each group gets its own meeting point for a 5K run and its own coffee spot after.
Run with people who are actually thinking about the same things you are.
How it works
When you RSVP, you'll get a short form (3 questions, 60 seconds): what you're working on, who you want to meet this week, and your rough pace.
We use your answers to match you into a group of 6 to 8. You'll get your group, your meeting point, your coffee spot, and quick intros for everyone, the night before.
The morning
8:30 AM: Meet your group at your assigned spot
8:35 AM: Intros, group photo, pace check
8:45 AM: 5K together. No one gets dropped, slowest pace sets the run.
9:30 AM: Coffee with your group at your assigned spot
10:00 AM: You're back to tech week
Pace
Conversational. We pace to the slowest person in the group and walk breaks are fine. If you want to race, there are other tech week runs for that. This one is for talking.
What to bring
Just yourself in something you can run in.
Capacity
RSVP doesn't guarantee a spot. We confirm by email 48h before so we can match thoughtfully.
About LYTT
LYTT is a platform for curated group fitness socials. We organize plans like this every week (5K Run + Coffee, Bouldering + Lunch, Beginner Jiu-Jitsu + Brunch) for people who want to meet others over real shared experiences. This event is what LYTT does, run live for tech week.

properties.title: People Making Pasta
properties.description:
What does making fresh pasta have to do with hiring and retaining great teams?

More than you think.

At People Making Pasta, we’re bringing together senior leaders from the people and talent community for a hands-on pasta-making experience. While we mix, roll, and shape fresh pasta together, we’ll explore the parallels between building a great dish and building a great team.

Hiring is part art, part craft.
So is pasta.

Both require:
The right ingredients
The right timing
Patience and precision
A clear standard for quality
And a process that actually works
This is for senior People leaders, Talent leaders, and hiring operators who care deeply about building strong, durable teams. Especially those working inside high-growth environments where retention, alignment, and quality truly matter.

Expect:
A guided pasta-making experience
Real conversations about hiring and retention
A room full of thoughtful operators
No panels. No slides. Just substance

properties.title: Toronto Tech Week x Loop Tech Talk 1: Shipping Quality at Speed - How We Built a Safety Net for Fintech Releases
properties.description:
Join us for our Toronto Tech Week x Loop Tech series!

What we'll cover: How Loop Financial transformed from "move fast and break things" to "move fast with confidence." Covering the engineering culture shift, tooling, and processes that let a small team ship rapidly to production while maintaining SOC2 compliance and avoiding customer-facing incidents.

Key topics:
Trunk-based development with strict branch protection
CI/CD pipeline design: SAST, SCA, container scanning, and approval gates
Just-In-Time production access with Icebreak (15-minute auto-expiring access)
Ownership culture: what it really means to "own quality" of a domain service
Blameless post-mortems and continuous improvement loops
Meet the presenter:
Yan Matagne is an Engineering Leader and passionate about making product processes smoother and customers happier, while helping engineering teams work smarter. He loves to take complicated tech problems and make it work for real people. Another one of his passions is building systems that make developers' lives easier and customers smile more. When he is not automating complex problems, you will find him sharing stories about what actually works in the real world of engineering leadership. Yan is the Head of Engineering at Loop Financial and has over 7 years experience in the FinTech industry in Canada.
Whether you’re scaling a fintech platform, modernizing your DevOps practices, or trying to reconcile velocity with reliability, this talk offers practical lessons you can apply immediately. Come learn how to move fast—with confidence.

properties.title: Hackathon: ARI.Software & Claude Code
properties.description:
ARI.Software is an AI-native, open-source, self-hosted hyper-personal workspace for managing tasks, docs, notes, contacts, knowledge, and workflows in one place. ARI's magic is that it also lets you build your own custom modules in minutes with no code, giving you a unified system so you can do your best work and live your best life.
ARI is deeply personal and flexible. Using AI tools like Claude Code you can quickly create modules tailored to your exact needs, whether that is a CRM, fitness tracker, shipment dashboard, financial manager, invoice generator, prompt library, voice notes tool, content planner, or something entirely unique. Every module is editable, remixable, and shareable, so your software can adapt to how you think and operate.
We're excited to invite you to the live ARI Hackathon on May 27 from 3:30 PM to 7:30 PM EST. This is your chance to build your own custom ARI module, collaborate with other builders, experiment with the platform, and bring your ideas to life. Whether you are a developer, founder, or simply curious about what's possible, you'll get hands-on experience creating something real.
You can build any module you want such as a personal financial manager, fitness coach, invoice generator, OpenClaw orchestrator, personal relationship manager, nutritional meal planner, health metrics dashboard, prompt library, voice notes tracker, content planner, brainstorming tool etc.
We'll be awarding $2,500 to the winning project! So come ready to build, learn fast, and ship something great!

FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS:

What is Toronto Tech Week?
The ARI.Software Hackathon is an official tech parter of Toronto Tech Week. Toronto Tech Week is a citywide celebration of the people building what's next. From May 26–29, 2026, founders, investors, and builders come together for hundreds of community-led events across Toronto, connecting tens of thousands of people around Canadian tech. Torontotechweek.com.

Where is the venue?
We've secured a great venue downtown Toronto on King street west near Bathurst. Fast WiFi (1gbps mesh network), good music, freshly brewed on-site coffee, a selection of self-service teas, still and sparkling water on tap.
What experience do I need? 

Any level is welcome, but experience with code-generating AI tools Claude Code is recommended. Non-devs can still be valuable on teams to help with planning, design, UI/UX etc.

Claude Code is used to create ARI.Software modules and a paid Claude Code account is required. https://claude.com/download

What is the prize?

The winning team will win $2,500!
Do I need a team?
You can come solo or with a team (up to 3 people). If you arrive solo, we'll help match you with a team.
What should I build?

ARI comes with dozens of productivity modules built in like task manager, knowledge manager, fitness tracker, trip planner etc. You can custom any new module which solves a real problem or adds clear value. It can be professional/fun/creative/unique.. as long as it is useful!
What should I bring?

A laptop, charger, and anything you need to code. Optional: headphones, snacks, and ideas you want to explore.
Can I use AI tools and existing libraries?

Yes. AI tools, open-source libraries, and frameworks are allowed. Disclose any pre-built code.
Do I need to finish a complete product?
The submission should be a working module.
How are projects judged?
 Based on usefulness, creativity, execution, integration with ARI, and design.
What do I need to submit?

Submit a Github link with the code with a README file. A demo video is encouraged.
For rules, judging criteria and more visit: https://ari.software/hackathon

properties.title: Your Office is a Growth Bet. Here is How To Make Sure It Pays Off.
properties.description:
This is not one company. It is a pattern that repeats across the Toronto tech ecosystem.
A company raises a Series B. They sign a lease for space built for 280 people. Eighteen months later they reduce their workforce to 160. The lease has no break clause. The space cannot be sublet because it was designed as one open floor. Every Monday morning 160 people walk into an office built for 280 and sit in the daily reminder of what happened. The rent runs over a million dollars a year. The lease has seven years left.
The conversation that would have prevented this takes 45 minutes.
This session is for founders, operators, and investors who are either approaching a real estate decision or watching one play out across their portfolio. We will cover three things:
•       The governance gap that makes real estate the most expensive decision nobody owns in a growth-stage company
•       The specific window in the Series A to Series B journey where the mistake gets locked in
•       The three questions every operator should answer before signing anything
This is the only session at Toronto Tech Week focused on the real estate and workplace decisions that growth-stage companies make during their most critical funding windows.
Remote-first companies: this applies to you too. Remote-first does not mean real estate free. It means you are making your real estate decisions without admitting you are making them. Those are the most expensive kind.
Hosted by Syed Rashid Masood Rizvi, Founder of AM Masons Advisory, with 18 years of global workplace and real estate leadership at Procter and Gamble across 180 sites and 20 markets.

This is the only Corporate Real Estate & Workplace event on the agenda so far!
Space is limited to 35 attendees. Register to secure your spot.

___

About the Hosts
MaRS Investment Accelerator Fund (IAF) is a leading seed stage fund investing in high-potential IT, health and cleantech companies in Ontario. IAF has over a 20-year history of successfully providing innovative firms with the capital, mentorship, and networks needed to validate ideas and bring products to market. Its mission is to keep Ontario’s talent and technology local while helping its companies go global.
YSpace is York University’s pan-university entrepreneurship and innovation hub supporting startups and entrepreneurs from a variety of sectors and communities. Our programming spans across scaling innovative technologies through customer and investor capital to scaling agri-food businesses into mass retail to diversity-focused initiatives like **ELLA powered by Desjardins** for women-led businesses and Black Entrepreneurship Alliance (BEA) for Black-led businesses. Additionally with our Start-Up Visa program, we are building a robust ecosystem, driving positive change and empowering entrepreneurs to shape the future. We are also home to two physical hubs in the City of Markham and the Town of Georgina.
TD Innovation Partners (TDIP) is a division of The Toronto-Dominion Bank. TDIP is a full-service team providing bespoke, high-touch banking and financing solutions in support of Technology and innovation companies at all stages. TDIP offers a broad suite of services to address the unique challenges and opportunities faced by technology entrepreneurs from banking and lending products to principal investments and private banking solutions.

properties.title: Bring One Thing, Automate It With Claude — A Live Hands-On Hour
properties.description:
About the event
AI tools have changed how work gets done — but most people are still using Claude like a smarter search bar. What does it look like to actually get leverage from it?
This interactive session brings together professionals, builders, and the AI-curious to learn how to use Claude effectively in real work. This is not a product pitch or a passive demo — it is a hands-on, task-driven hour focused on automating something you actually do.
Show and Share
The session opens with a live walkthrough of the different ways to use Claude — conversational chat, projects, and Claude Code — and where each one earns its keep in real workflows.
Learn by Doing
Participants will then work on their own task, with guidance, to explore:
How to break a real task into a prompt Claude can act on
When to use chat vs. projects vs. agentic modes
Prompting habits that separate "neat trick" from "saves me three hours a week"
Where Claude falls short and how to design around its limits
Each participant brings one task from their real work — a recurring email, a messy spreadsheet, a research summary, a meeting prep ritual, anything you'd rather not do again — and automates it live with us in the room.
The session will conclude with a brief recap of patterns that worked across the group, followed by open networking for those who want to continue the conversation.
What to bring: A laptop and one task you do every week that you wish you didn't.

properties.title: Finnovation Day @ Toronto Tech Week – The Ultimate Startup & Investor Experience!
properties.description:
Hey Toronto, get ready for open pitch competitions, panels and networking happening alongside Tech Week!

"Finnovation Day: Real-Time Payments, Real-Time Fraud. Can Risk Keep Up?"
The next wave of fintech innovation is powered by real-time infrastructure and accelerated by AI. Payments are becoming instant, borders are dissolving, and new financial products are being launched faster than ever.
But with this speed comes a new reality: fraud is evolving just as fast, and a little bit faster.
AI is no longer just a tool for financial institutions. It’s being used on both sides:
to optimize underwriting, detection, and customer experience
and to simulate behavior, bypass controls, and scale fraud attacks
This creates a new kind of challenge for the industry:
- How do you enable real-time innovation without opening the door to real-time fraud?
- How do you build guardrails that protect without slowing growth?
At this Toronto Tech Week gathering, we bring together the people operating at that exact intersection. Expect builders, risk leaders, and fintech operators exploring what it really takes to keep up.
What to expect:
🎤 Expert Panel Discussion
A candid conversation with representatives from Canada’s leading financial institutions and fintechs on:
the trade-offs between speed, security, and user experience
how fraud is changing in the age of AI
what actually works in modern risk infrastructure
🚀 Startup Showcase & Competition
Founders building the next generation of fintech innovation, including fraud prevention, risk and compliance tools will present their solutions, get feedback from industry experts and compete for an award.
! Only FinTech and related startups are eligible to participate.
⭐️ At the closing Prize Ceremony, one award will be presented:
Pitch Competition Winner
🏆 Main Prize - Winner will receive $25,000 in AWS credit from Spendbase, our official Pitch Competition Prize Partner.
Note: The full amount is guaranteed for winners who haven't previously redeemed $25k+ in AWS credits. Others may receive an adjusted amount.
🤝 Curated Networking
A room designed for meaningful connections between fintech builders, risk executives, and operators shaping the future of payments.
Join us to explore how the industry can move faster without losing control!
Spots are limited — apply now to be part of it!

Pre-seed to Series A B2B founders, especially those who identify as BIPOC, Women+, disabled, and/or LGBTQIA.

About the Hosts
Forum Ventures® is the leading early-stage fund, accelerator and venture studio for B2B startups. Founded in 2014 and based in New York, San Francisco, and Toronto, we’re on a mission to make the B2B SaaS journey easier, more accessible, and successful for early-stage founders.
Forum is investing in 60+ companies this year.  Pitch Us here.

 YSpace is York University’s pan-university entrepreneurship and innovation hub supporting startups and entrepreneurs from a variety of sectors and communities. Our programming spans across scaling innovative technology ventures through customer and investor capital to scaling agri-food businesses into mass retail, to diversity-focused initiatives like ELLA for women-led businesses and BEA for Black-led businesses.
Black Entrepreneurship Alliance (BEA) is a community-based, university-backed initiative created by Black Creek Community Health Centre (BCCHC) in partnership with York University’s YSpace, in response to the inefficient access to capital, knowledge, and support experienced by Black founders within the entrepreneurial ecosystem. BEA comprises of a suite of agri-food, tech, and business-acumen programs for Black founders to scale their companies.

properties.title: Women In Tech Meetup - A CodePub Workshop
properties.description:
What is Code Pub?
The Code Pub is an international network with events for women and gender diverse individuals who want to learn more about tech. Our goal is empowering every individual in the tech industry by creating a platform where one can be inspired and inspire others.
The Code Pub was created by Netlight in 2013 with the purpose of increasing the number of women and gender diverse individuals in the tech industry. By lowering the threshold for getting started with programming and highlighting female role models within tech, we believe that we can make a difference.
Join us on May 28 for a friendly hands-on session: Build and Program Your Own Plant Moisture Sensor!
Ever wondered how to make a plant "talk" to your software? In this workshop, you’ll work with an ESP32 microcontroller to build a soil moisture sensor, dip your toes into embedded programming, and stream real-time readings into a shared dashboard.
No technical experience necessary- just curiosity and enthusiasm, as well, please bring your own computer! Whether you’re a beginner or a seasoned pro, you’ll leave with new skills and new friends.
What is Netlight:
Netlight is an international digital consultancy, helping leading companies to succeed in the digital landscape, from advice to implementation.

Our service contains the collective intelligence of 2,200 consultants offering a comprehensive range of digital services, from strategy to technology. We support industries that are facing new challenges and opportunities based on new technology, to make better business.
Netlight has been awarded several times for profitable growth and management, as a top employer, and for our engagement in Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion. Located in Stockholm, Gothenburg, Oslo, Helsinki, Copenhagen, Munich, Hamburg, Berlin, Frankfurt, Zurich, Cologne, Amsterdam, Madrid, Toronto and London. Co-creating the future today, since 1999.

properties.title: How Great Teams Win in the Age of AI
properties.description:
AI is accelerating but team performance is not.
As organizations adopt new tools and technologies, many are running into the same challenges: unclear ownership, slower decision-making, and growing disconnects between strategy and execution.
This session brings together experienced leaders to unpack what it actually takes for teams to perform in the age of AI.  This is not a passive panel, it is a guided, interactive conversation.
In this session, we will explore:  Why teams still matter in an AI-driven world The core attributes of high-performing teams The most common breakdowns teams experience today How leaders can improve decision-making, alignment, and trust.  The discussion will go beyond surface-level insights.
Panelists will challenge assumptions, share real experiences, and work through practical scenarios that leaders are facing today.
Attendees will leave with clearer thinking, sharper questions, and a better understanding of how to strengthen their teams in an AI-driven environment.  

Part of the “How Great Teams Win in the Age of AI” Roadshow
This session is one stop in a multi-city experience bringing together leaders across North America to rethink how teams perform in an AI-driven world.
Join us in a format that works best for you:
Boston (Virtual): May 28 at 2:00 PM
New York City (In-Person): June 3
SF: TBA
LA: TBA
Each session offers a slightly different experience, but all are grounded in the same core idea:  The next competitive advantage isn’t AI alone,  it’s how your team performs with it.
We’d love to see you at one (or more) of the stops.

properties.title: Why Brand Authority is your Startup's AI Strategy - Workshop
properties.description:
Your brand isn’t your logo anymore - it’s what AI says about you (or doesn't) when making recommendations.
When buyers ask ChatGPT, Perplexity, or Gemini for the “best tools” in your category, only a few companies appear. Those companies have built brand authority signals long before buyers ever visit their website.
This session shows founders how to design those signals intentionally so AI, media, analysts, and customers recognize their company as a trusted leader.
Takeaways for startups:
• The difference between branding and brand authority
• How AI search decides which companies get recommended
• Five authority signals every startup should build early
• Practical ways to become the company AI recommends.

properties.title: People First, Product Second: A Consumer Deep Dive with Maveron
properties.description:
The best consumer companies don't start with a product. They start with a behaviour. A shift in how people discover, decide, and buy. The founders who see those shifts before they're obvious build the companies that last.
Maveron has operated on this belief since 1998. Co-founded by Howard Schultz (former CEO of Starbucks) and Dan Levitan, Maveron invests in businesses that transform consumer lives across a wide variety of industries including ecommerce, marketplaces, wellness, personal finance, workforce empowerment and the future of play. For 28 years, they've backed non-normal founders who look beyond convention. Their portfolio tells the story: eBay, Zulily ($2.4B exit), Flywire (IPO), Course Hero (exited at unicorn valuation), Thirty Madison, Rebelstork, Trupanion, Lovevery and more.
Jason Stoffer, Chief Investment Officer & General Partner at Maveron, sits down with Alex Norman for a deep-dive fireside. How Maveron identifies behavior change before consensus. Why they invest in how customers feel, not what they buy. What Canadian founders should know before pitching a US consumer investor.

The session also features Canadian consumer founders on stage. Spotlights on what they're building, who they're building for, and the behavior shifts they're betting on.

Featured Speaker

Jason Stoffer has spent 18 years investing in consumer companies from seed through IPO and acquisition. As CIO and General Partner at Maveron, he helps lead investment strategy across the firm's $225M fund, focusing on companies where a shift in consumer behaviour or a technology shift (AI!) creates an opening.
Jason's current portfolio includes Toronto-based Rebel, Lovevery, Remedy Meds, Unvault, and Necessaire. Jason writes about consumer business models and public market dynamics at Ringing the Bell on Substack.
Jason holds an MBA from The Wharton School and a BA in Economics (Phi Beta Kappa) from the University of Michigan.
More Speakers to be announced!

Thanks To Our Partner

Co-organized with the Schwartz Reisman Innovation Campus - U of T Ecosystem team, connecting innovators and leaders shaping the future of tech.

properties.description:
Join us at Questrade’s new downtown Toronto office for an exclusive evening of conversation, connection, and behind-the-scenes insight into one of Canada’s most established fintech companies.
Designed for senior developers, engineers, product managers and technical leaders, this event offers a candid look at what we’re building, how we’re scaling, and why this is such a meaningful moment to be part of Questrade’s next chapter.
You’ll hear directly from Hwan Kim, Chief Product Officer, and Salim Naran, President, Growth Portfolio, on Questrade’s growth, product vision, and the opportunities ahead. We’ll also bring together leaders across Product Technology, Customer Experience, and Banking to share how we’re building a multi-brand financial ecosystem that’s redefining how Canadians invest, bank, and build wealth.
This is an opportunity to experience Questrade up close—to meet the people behind the work, get a feel for our culture, and connect with leaders who are shaping the future of Canadian fintech.
If you care about solving meaningful engineering problems, building with strong people, and doing work that matters at scale, we’d love to meet you!

Agenda
4:30pm: Doors open - Check in, mingle, and settle in
5:30pm: Programming Content + Interactive Q&A
6:30pm: Networking Reception - open bar & passed canapés
9:00pm: End of evening

About Questrade

For 27 years, we’ve remained independent and 100% Canadian-owned. We are a 13x winner of Canada’s Best Managed Companies and a 2x winner of Canada’s Top 100 Employers. Our ecosystem includes Questrade, Questbank, Questrade Wealth Management, Community Trust, Zolo, and Flexiti - all working together to help Canadians become financially secure through cutting-edge technology.
We're building something worth being part of. Come see for yourself.
And if you’d like to learn more about building at Questrade, explore our current openings here.

TechTO is Canada’s largest community of tech builders - connecting 60,000+ founders, operators, and investors through year-round meetups, curated dinners, peer tables, and media storytelling. Interested in learning more? techto.org. For sponsorship inquiries, reach out to partnerships@techto.org.

properties.title: Market Mindfulness and Finliti's Game of Gains
properties.description:
Market Mindfulness is a beginner-friendly board game about investing, designed to make the stock market fun, social, and easy to understand.
At the Finliti Game of Gains Fintech Tournament, players step into the role of investors and learn how emotions like fear, confidence and regret can shape financial decisions.
Co-hosted by Jennifer Schell, fintech founder of Finliti and Highline Beta, this interactive event blends financial literacy, strategy, and friendly competition. Inspired by poker, blackjack, and Dungeons & Dragons, the game takes players through bull markets, downturns and behavioral traps that mirror real investing scenarios.
Whether you're a founder, builder, student, or simply curious about the markets, this is a welcoming chance to learn, play, and compete for prizes in an unforgettable experience.
About Highline Beta:
Highline Beta is a venture studio and venture capital firm working with corporate partners and family offices in the United States, Canada and globally to build and fund new ventures and launch venture studios. We validate unsolved problems, access untapped markets and leverage under-utilized assets, to drive growth, new revenue streams and innovation.
About Finliti:
Finliti builds behavioural finance software for the investment industry — helping advisors and investors better understand money decisions, risk, and personality. Our Game of Gains brings that mission to life through a fun investing game, first played on the floor of the New York Stock Exchange.
About Toronto Tech Week:
Toronto Tech Week is a citywide celebration of the people building what’s next. From May 26–29, 2026, founders, investors, and builders come together for hundreds of community-led events across Toronto, connecting tens of thousands of people around Canadian tech. Torontotechweek.com

properties.title: Co-Founder Connect
properties.description:
Build with the right people. Grow with intention.
Join us for Co-Founder Connect, presented by the Black Entrepreneurship Alliance in partnership with YSpace York University, an evening designed to help tech founders, operators, and builders find the right partners to form or strengthen their startup teams.
Co-Founder Connect is a curated networking experience that creates intentional space for meaningful conversations, genuine connections, and long-term collaboration. Whether you are looking for technical expertise, business strategy support, or a complementary co-founder to help take your startup to the next level, this is your chance to pitch, connect, and build robust partnerships in a community-driven environment.
Building the right team early matters. Research consistently shows that diverse and complementary teams outperform by making better decisions, innovating faster, and achieving stronger business outcomes.

Toronto Tech Week is a citywide celebration of the people building what’s next. From May 25–29, 2026, founders, investors, and builders come together for hundreds of community-led events across Toronto, connecting tens of thousands of people around Canadian tech. Torontotechweek.com

Fireside panel on finding the perfect co-founder & team
Quickfire 60-second founder and venture pitches
Match Making Zone (Curated co-founder/team matching)
Speed Matching Zone
Professional Headshots
Interactive Zones

Technical co-founders and software engineers
Product designers

Operators across finance, marketing, HR, and operations
Builders looking to join or support early-stage teams

How it works
Thoughtfully designed connections don’t happen by accident.
More to come...


Get Involved
If you have any additional questions or are Interested in partnering, mentoring, or sponsoring future BEA programs? Reach out to us at thebea@yorku.ca.
We gratefully acknowledge the support of the Government of Canada through the Federal Economic Development Agency for Southern Ontario (FedDev Ontario) and the City of Toronto.


About Black Entrepreneurship Alliance (BEA):
The Black Entrepreneurship Alliance (BEA) is a Black-led initiative that exists to amplify and advance the next generation of Black entrepreneurs. BEA provides fully funded programs, partnerships, and access to help Black entrepreneurs, professionals, and youth launch and grow successful ventures and careers. Learn more at www.thebea.co.
About YSpace, York University
YSpace is York University’s pan-university entrepreneurship and innovation hub supporting startups and entrepreneurs from a variety of sectors and communities. Our programming spans across scaling innovative technologies through customer and investor capital to scaling agri-food businesses into mass retail to diversity-focused initiatives like ELLA for women-led businesses and in partnership with Black Entrepreneurship Alliance (BEA) for Black-led businesses. We are also home to two physical hubs in the City of Markham and the Town of Georgina.
https://www.yorku.ca/yspace/
About Idea Mississauga
IDEA Mississauga has the essential building blocks that entrepreneurs and innovators need to get started and grow. Through our IDEA Square One Entrepreneurship Hub, you can access a network of mentorship, partners, talent and connections to test, scale and commercialize solutions to global challenges.
https://www.ideamississauga.ca/
About Futurpreneur:
Since 1996, Futurpreneur has fostered the entrepreneurial passions of Canada’s diverse startup founders ages 18-39. We are the only national non-profit organization that provides young aspiring entrepreneurs across Canada with the loan financing, mentorship and resources they need to start or buy a business.
https://futurpreneur.ca/en/
About SmartTo:
SmartTO (Technologies of Ontario) is YSpace, York University's provincially funded mobility hub and part of the York Region's Regional Technology Development Site (RTDS), funded by the Ontario Vehicle Innovation Network (OVIN).
Smart Mobility Applied Research and Testing-Technologies of Ontario (SmartTO) empowers startups and SMEs to develop, test and prototype advanced automotive technologies and smart mobility solutions through accessing our state of the-art equipment, innovative labs and research centres, world-renowned expertise, and concierge-style business advisory and technical services. We focus on Smart Materials and Light-weighting along with Smart Mobility, Connectivity and Reliability.
https://www.yorku.ca/yspace/programs-streams/smartto/


Code of Conduct
BEA is committed to creating a safe, respectful, and inclusive environment for everyone. By registering for this event, you agree to abide by our Code of Conduct. Please take a moment to review it before attending.

BEA & YSpace Attendee Media & Photo Release Notice
By attending this event, you acknowledge that the Black Entrepreneurship Alliance (BEA) and YSpace may capture photos, video, and audio for storytelling, promotional, and archival use. If you prefer not to be included in any recordings or photos, please notify a BEA or YSpace team member upon arrival so accommodations can be made.

All guests are expected to follow event guidelines and adhere to the direction of BEA and YSpace staff to support a safe, respectful, and welcoming environment. If you experience or witness behaviour that feels inappropriate, unsafe, or out of line with our community values, please report it immediately to a BEA or YSpace team member.
BEA reserves the right to remove or deny access to anyone who does not uphold these expectations.

properties.title: Building Our Future in AI: Power, Inclusion, and What Comes Next
properties.description:
Artificial intelligence is rapidly transforming how we work, learn, create, and participate in society. But many communities are still being asked to adapt to systems they did not help design.
Building Our Future in AI: Power, Inclusion, and What Comes Next is an Alianza Latina event during Toronto Tech Week 2026 that brings together community members, technologists, founders, students, researchers, nonprofit leaders, and ecosystem builders for an evening focused on inclusion, responsible AI, and community empowerment.
This event is rooted in a simple but urgent question: how do we ensure the future of AI is not shaped without us?

Through dialogue, reflection, and project visibility, we will explore both how AI is already affecting our lives through shifts in work, access, power, and inequality, and how our communities can become more active participants in shaping what comes next.
The evening will also build on Alianza Latina’s recent momentum in AI community-building by creating space to spotlight projects and ideas emerging from earlier community activations and hackathon efforts. Rather than treating inclusion as a side conversation, this event places representation, belonging, and community power at the center of the future of AI.
What to expect
A featured conversation or panel on responsible AI, inclusion, and the future of technology
Reflections on how AI is affecting communities, work, and opportunity
A curated showcase of community-rooted projects and ideas
Networking with leaders across tech, civil society, education, and community spaces
Space to connect around mentorship, collaboration, and future possibilities
Themes
Responsible AI and public understanding
Inclusion, access, and belonging
The future of work and social impact
Community-built innovation
From visibility to opportunity
Who should attend
This event is for:
Latino professionals, students, founders, and community members interested in AI and tech
Allies and cross-sector partners
Civil society and nonprofit leaders
Technologists and ecosystem builders
Researchers, educators, and public-interest leaders
Mentors, funders, accelerators, and industry representatives interested in more inclusive innovation
Why this event matters
Toronto Tech Week is one of the city’s most important convening spaces for innovation, startups, investment, and emerging technology. This event creates space for a broader and more grounded conversation about who gets to participate in shaping the future of AI, whose realities are reflected in the systems being built, and what more inclusive pathways to innovation can look like in practice.
Agenda
6:00 PM — Registration & Networking
6:30 PM — Welcome & Opening Remarks
6:45 PM — Land Acknowledgement
6:50 PM — Fireside Chat with Chenayi Mutambasere
7:25 PM — Panel: Building Inclusive AI Futures
8:10 PM — Community Project Showcase: Mini “Dragon’s Den”
8:45 PM — Community Reflections & Connections
8:55 PM — Closing Remarks & Group Photo
9:00 PM — Event Close
Hosted by Alianza Latina
Alianza Latina is a collaborative initiative working to build unity, visibility, and data power for Latino communities in Canada. Through community-centered programming, partnerships, and shared infrastructure, Alianza Latina is helping create stronger pathways for connection, representation, and collective action.

properties.title: Toronto Tech Week x Loop Tech Talk 2: The Proxy Pattern -How Next.js Let Us Migrate Without a Big Bang
properties.description:
Join us for our Toronto Tech Week x Loop Tech series! 

What we'll cover: How Loop uses Vercel's Next.js as an intelligent proxy layer to incrementally migrate from a legacy Netlify app — zero downtime, one route at a time. A practical playbook for teams stuck with legacy front ends who want to modernize without betting the business.

Key topics:
Next.js rewrites and middleware as a migration strategy
Intelligent routing: "route exists?" → Next.js, else → Netlify legacy
Handling authentication state (Cognito cookies) across two apps
Real gotchas: the non-ASCII cookie bug that broke production
GraphQL federation + Apollo Client as the unified data layer
Meet the presenter:
Ian Duke is a senior software engineer at Loop Financial, where he works on Next.js, React, and Apollo GraphQL. Before tech, he spent nine years in Flight Safety at a major airline, which is why he takes production incidents a little too seriously.
Come join us for a fun morning of learning, networking and starting the day strong.

properties.title: AI x CPG: The New Playbook for Retail Growth
properties.description:
Opener, the leading AI-powered wholesale engine, is hosting a Toronto Tech week event where Canada's cutting edge CPG & AI worlds collide. 

Unilever's Global AI lab is our feature keynote, discussing their newly released agentic whitepaper. 

Next, several of Canada's hottest emerging CPG brands are on a panel using real life examples of how they are using AI to grow their companies over 100% each year. 

The brands winning the next decade of retail aren't waiting for a distributor to make calls on their behalf. They're utilizing AI powered tools and building like it's 2026. Join 200 emerging CPG founders, CPG enterprise leaders, retail buyers, investors, and operators for an evening of hard-won insights, live product tasting, and real talk on how AI is changing the way emerging brands get to market and stay there. This is the room where the next wave of Canadian consumer brands gets built.

Hosted by Opener: CPG's AI powered wholesale growth engine. 

IS THIS FOR ME?
This event is for you if you want to know what's actually happening at the intersection of AI and CPG right now.
You'll leave knowing how the CPG industry is using AI right now and what outcomes they produce. What AI tools brands are actually using to grow. And what the next 6 to 12 months looks like for brands who get this right versus brands who don't.
You belong here if you're a CPG founder who needs to move faster. A retail buyer who wants to spot the next wave early. An investor or operator tracking where consumer is actually heading.
If you're still running your retail growth playbook from 2022, this room will change that.

properties.title: Scale Stage Founder Dinner
properties.description:
You've built something real. Now let's talk about what comes next, with founders who get it. Past the dreamer phase, past the point solution stage, and still owning the majority of what you've built. You shouldn't have to figure it out alone. Come find your people.
This dinner is intentionally small and curated for founders running scale-stage businesses at $15M+ ARR. There's no pitch at the end of this evening and no hidden agenda beyond good conversation over a meal. Just a room of operators who have grown past the hard early stages and are now navigating the messier, less-documented ones.
We're Mark and Catherine Graham, and since 2011 we've been growing commonsku from the inside out. We knew the promotional products industry firsthand, saw the need for a workflow platform designed specifically for it, and built it. Fifteen years later, we're proud to serve 900+ distributors and 100+ suppliers powering $2B in GMV. We know what this stage feels like, and how rare it is to find a room of founders who’ve lived it.
Honestly, this dinner is the one we wish had existed for us at this stage, and Toronto Tech Week felt like the right moment to bring this group together. Expect candid conversations on the things keeping you up at night: growing fast without losing ownership, going deep in a vertical versus broadening your market, finding your footing as a non-technical founder, and figuring out where AI fits into everything you're building. Our hope is that you'll leave with a small circle of founders who become your sounding board long after dinner ends, and at least one idea that changes how you think about what's next.
The Evening
6:30-7:30pm: Beverages and mingling
7:30-9:30pm: Dinner in a Jeffersonian style, where one conversation keeps the whole table connected throughout the evening
If you're compounding past $15M ARR, monetizing GMV, extending your product, or trying to grow without giving away the cap table, this dinner was designed for you.
Seats are limited. Apply to attend.
About commonsku
commonsku is a workflow collaboration platform designed exclusively for the promotional products industry. At the core, commonsku is a systems of record platform for promotional products distributors that enables them to manage their customer interactions, present ideas to their customers, create orders, manage production, invoice, and bill. With commonsku’s connected workflow, distributors find joy and growth in their businesses through powerful technology and unmatched community. commonsku.com

properties.title: From Idea to Clickable Prototype in Minutes: A Practical Miro AI Workshop
properties.description:
EVENT FULL. Please sign up for afternoon event. https://luma.com/0412yjqr

---
Most teams do not fail because they lack talent.
They fail because they start building before they are aligned.
Ideas move into development too early. Feedback arrives too late. Halfway through delivery everyone realises they had a different vision of the outcome.
The result is predictable: rework, delays, frustrated stakeholders, and wasted budget.
This hands-on workshop during Toronto Tech Week shows how modern teams solve that problem using Miro’s AI-powered prototyping capabilities.
Hosted by Generation Digital, a workplace AI consultancy working with organisations across the UK and Canada, this interactive session will demonstrate how teams can go from a rough concept to a working clickable prototype in minutes.
You will also hear from Michelle Johannessen, Group Product Marketing Manager at Miro, who will share insights into how teams are using Miro’s latest AI capabilities to accelerate product thinking, collaboration, and innovation.
Rather than theory, you will see the process happen live.
In this workshop you will learn:
• How AI-powered prototyping helps teams visualise ideas faster
• How early prototypes dramatically reduce costly development rework
• How product, marketing, and operations teams can collaborate around a shared visual concept
• How Miro’s AI capabilities accelerate innovation and improve decision making
By the end of the session you will see how quickly an idea can become something tangible that teams can test, critique, and refine before development begins.
Who should attend
This session is designed for leaders and teams responsible for:
• Product development
• Digital transformation
• Innovation and service design
• Operational improvement
If your organisation wants faster alignment, clearer communication, and more confident decision making before projects begin, this workshop will show how AI-powered prototyping helps teams move from idea to execution far more efficiently.
Seats are limited to keep the session practical and interactive.

properties.title: How Cybersecurity protects and grows Revenue (without buying an Arsenal of tools)
properties.description:
Description: How Cybersecurity protects Revenue Operations and steers revenue growth (without buying 20 tools)
Headlines
A practical and user-friendly lens to securing your Revenue operations
A short How to: Leveraging Cybersecurity as a Revenue Generator
Traditionally, cybersecurity has served as a digital guardian, protecting an organisation’s systems, data, and infrastructure from cybercriminals.
But in the age of AI, cyberattacks are multiplying faster than browser tabs during a late-night research rabbit hole. SMBs and enterprises alike are facing a constant stream of phishing attempts, credential theft, ransomware, and AI-powered social engineering.
The challenge today is not simply buying more security tools. That is infact counterproductive. The challenge is to design a cybersecurity posture that actually aligns with how your organisation operates and grows.
This session explores cybersecurity not just as a defensive function, but as a strategic lever for protecting AS WELL AS enabling revenue. (Approx Total: 90 -120 minutes)
The talk covers two key areas: (Approx 60-75 minutes)
A practical Cybersecurity Posture (for SMBs and growing teams; Enterprises also welcome) that ensures Revenue Protection:
Cybersecurity as a Revenue Enabler:
A well-designed security posture does more than prevent breaches. It improves uptime, protects brand trust, safeguards sales channels (like email and web infrastructure), and ultimately acts as a catalyst for revenue growth.
In a world overflowing with attack types and security products, how do you decide what actually matters?
Using the Pareto Principle, we’ll explore how organisations can focus on the relatively smaller but highly impactful bits of security controls that reduce real-world risks almost entirely while protecting their most important assets.
Workshop (Approx: 30 - 45 Minutes)
A live demo + self-implementation workshop of a lightweight, in-house tool that can:
Audit your organisation’s cybersecurity posture
Evaluate the domain and email security health
Assess personal cyber resilience habits
Think of it as a quick “cyber hygiene check-up” for both organisations and individuals.
No fear-mongering. No vendor jargon. Just practical insights, a bit of geek humour, and tools you can actually use.
The session draws on observations from SMB realities, and yet, Enterprise teams are likely to find some practical takeaways :)

Ambial Networks. Protecting Revenue. Powering Growth
Q&A Session, Workshop ( Appetisers + 1 Drink Included). 
Dinner available for Purchase

properties.title: Preparing for CMMC and CPCSC: What Defense Suppliers Need to Know
properties.description:
Canadian and U.S. defense contractors are facing a new compliance reality: CMMC in the United States and CPCSC in Canada. For small and mid-sized suppliers, the challenge is not just understanding the frameworks, but choosing an implementation approach that fits their business, customer base, and budget.

This interactive workshop will help Ontario-based defense contractors, subcontractors, and technology suppliers compare CMMC and CPCSC, understand where the frameworks overlap, and explore practical implementation options for smaller teams. Sponsored by CyberCare Pro and PreVeil the session will show how organizations can reduce complexity, protect controlled information, and build a compliance roadmap that supports both Canadian and cross-border opportunities.

Participants will leave with:
1. A clear comparison of CMMC and CPCSC requirements.
2. A framework for deciding whether to self-assess, outsource, or hybridize compliance work.
3. Practical implementation options for identity, device, document, and email protection.
4. Guidance on how to align cybersecurity controls with defense contracting goals.
5. A better understanding of funding and support options for Ontario-based companies pursuing certification.
Virtual Option will be available.
Hosted by CyberCare Pro and Preveil

properties.title: Mapping the Pulse of the Grid: Space, Time, and Physics. ⚡🌍
properties.description:
Mapping the Pulse of the Grid: Space, Time, and Physics. ⚡🌍
Join Kraftgene AI for a Toronto Tech Week deep-dive into the "Big Three" of Energy AI. See how we’re building the universal intelligence layer for a decentralized future using GNNs, LSTMs/Transformers, and Physics-Informed Learning.
As the global energy transition accelerates, our aging power grids are facing unprecedented stress. Kraftgene AI is at the forefront of this challenge, developing "EnergyEminence"—an AI-native platform designed as a comprehensive framework for orchestrating stability across the entire energy ecosystem.
In this intimate, high-level technical workshop, our founding team will pull back the curtain on:
Spatial Intelligence (GNNs): Why Graph Neural Networks are the superior architecture for mapping complex energy networks, from Microgrids to EV charging.
Temporal Forecasting (LSTMs/Transformers): Mastering the "Time" element—predicting the exact timing and propagation paths of cascade failures before they occur to enable proactive de-energization.
Physics-Informed Learning: Bridging the gap between "Black Box" AI and power engineering to ensure industrial-grade reliability.
Interactive Demo: A live, real-time look at our models simulating and mitigating grid risks.
Who should attend? Whether you are a utility professional, an AI researcher, or an investor interested in the "AI for Sustainability" movement, join us to see how we are leveraging the Toronto tech ecosystem to build a more resilient global energy infrastructure.

properties.title: What Kills Startups (Every Time)
properties.description:
Success is overhyped. Failure is patterned—and preventable.
Everyone teaches how to win. Almost no one studies how startups actually die.
And they don’t die randomly.
Most failures follow the same patterns—long before the market, product, or team gets blamed. This session breaks down what actually kills startups—and how to avoid it.
Hear from Albert Lin, Managing Partner, and a US venture capital GP, sharing lessons from the trenches on what separates companies that survive from those that don’t.
🎯 What you’ll walk away with
The most common (and preventable) startup failure patterns
How capital structure quietly determines outcomes
Where governance breaks—and how to fix it early
What experienced investors look for beyond upside
If you’re building or investing in a startup, this is the part no one teaches—and the part that matters most when things go wrong.

properties.title: Watts of Innovation: Toronto Tech Week Ride

Most Tech Week events start with coffee. This one starts at 25 km/h.
Watts of Innovation is a 20–25km road ride for experienced cyclists in tech. This is not a casual ride. Expect a steady pace, tight group riding, and a focused route.
We start early so you can ride hard and still make it back for Tech Week events.

Helmets required

Hold your line and ride predictably

Comfortable riding in a group at pace

It allows users to open local multi-currency accounts in the US, Canada, UK, and Europe, enabling them to receive payments and manage foreign exchange without traditional international wire fees. Additionally, the platform simplifies global operations by offering corporate Visa cards and integrating with tools like Stripe and Gusto to streamline team payments and payroll.


Midas Labs
Midas Labs is a forward-deployed AI engineering and product studio that accelerates zero-to-one product builds by acting as a technical co-founder rather than a traditional vendor. They utilize an AI-native delivery model where artificial intelligence handles the heavy lifting of coding, while their team of senior engineers provides the critical judgment, context, and decision-making. The company specializes in rapidly shipping AI-native infrastructure, data systems, and highly scalable platforms for heavily regulated markets such as banking and fintech.

properties.title: Your Best AI Strategy Has No UI
properties.description:
The most common AI mistake isn't picking the wrong tool. It's adding another one.
The executives building real AI advantage aren't expanding their tech stack; they're making their existing stack more intelligent. The future of AI for enterprise is a layer that sits across your existing business systems connecting your ERP, CRM, finance tools, and HR platforms into something that actually works together. Once that layer exists, something more powerful becomes possible: your people stop clicking through software and start talking to it.
Natural language is the future of how businesses interact with their systems. Not a chatbot bolted onto one platform, but a single intelligent layer that understands your business, follows your rules, and executes across every system you already run.
The leaders getting ahead aren't shopping for more tools. They're rethinking how their entire organization interacts with the ones they have.
You'll leave with:
A practical lens for evaluating where this strategy creates the least risk and the most value in your existing stack
A risk and governance framework that makes AI decisions easier to own
An understanding of AI as a connected layer across your business and not another software
A clear picture of how natural language is replacing the click-through workflows your teams navigate every day
Confidence to lead user adoption and team buy-in
The goal isn't to move fast. It's to move right. You’ll leave this session with a clearer path forward.

properties.title: Accelerating AI Startups in 2026: Insights from Founders, Operators, and Investors
properties.description:
Join us for this informative panel discussion, bringing together two founders from PRAKTIKAI’s KAI Accelerate program and an investor from our Toronto network for an honest, practical conversation about building and scaling AI startups. We’ll cover what’s actually working in early-stage AI, how teams are using data and model-driven workflows to accelerate product development, and what investors are looking for in 2026.The discussion will focus on the challenges of building startups in Canada's AI ecosystem, real founder experiences, how to prototype and build MVPs, and how to navigate early customer adoption in a market crowded with hype. Attendees will walk away with practical insights they can apply immediately, if they’re building, investing, or leading teams through AI adoption.
About the Speakers
Dr. Andrew Reaume, PhD, MBA, is the President, CEO, and Co-Founder of Melior Discovery, which he established in 2005, and the Founder of MeliorIQ. With over 25 years in the pharmaceutical industry, including leadership roles in drug discovery and business analytics at Pfizer and Cephalon, he has raised more than $15 million in investment capital and secured over $40 million in partnering deals. MeliorIQ leverages machine learning to address reproducibility challenges in pre-clinical pharmaceutical research, analyzing environmental variables to reduce industry costs and improve outcomes.
Nissrine Saidi is the Founder and CEO of Seniconnect, an AI-powered platform providing companionship and monitoring for seniors aging at home through voice interactions, health tracking, and family alerts. With a background in AI, IoT, and industrial systems from her education at École Polytechnique de Montréal, she currently serves as OT Security Manager at Deloitte in Toronto. Her work focuses on bridging human care with technology to reduce loneliness and detect early cognitive changes while prioritizing privacy.
Wendy Huang is an Investor at Comma Capital and the Founder of Maple Leaf Venture Club, a non-profit community platform connecting founders, investors, and operators through events like pitch nights, panels, and socials. A University of Toronto alumna, she leads initiatives like Comma Maple to support Canadian startups with early-stage funding, mentorship, and networks, emphasizing conviction in ambitious builders across borders.
PRAKTIKAI’s KAI Accelerate offering is a flexible technical partnership program designed for AI startups at any stage, offering hands-on mentorship, advisory support, collaborative building of core components like MVPs, go-to-market strategies, and investor introductions to help founders overcome common hurdles with pragmatic, experience-based guidance. Learn more at prktk.ai/accelerate

properties.title: Lunch & Learned: Building an Investor Mindset
properties.description:
Lunch & Learn: Building an Investor Mindset is a structured, small-group series designed for experienced professionals evaluating exposure to private markets and early-stage opportunities. It is particularly relevant for advisors to family-owned enterprises exploring patient capital strategies, as well as operators and executives seeking a disciplined framework before deploying capital.
This is not a pitch event, demo day, or fundraising session. The in-person working lunch in Toronto establishes a practical capital allocation framework — how the venture model works in practice, how investors assess asymmetric risk and opportunity, and how capital is deployed at the pre-seed and seed stages. The session is intentionally discussion-driven and limited in size to allow for meaningful exchange, not presentations.
Participants then continue with two live online sessions that deepen the framework, covering venture exits and return mechanics, portfolio construction and follow-on strategy, and early-stage valuation logic. Together, the three sessions form a coherent learning path — moving from understanding venture mechanics to evaluating outcomes and managing capital exposure over time.
This series is designed for professionals who want more than surface-level commentary. It offers a structured, practical lens for approaching early-stage investing within a broader long-term capital strategy.

properties.description:
Join the Legal Innovation Zone (LIZ) and Clio during Toronto Tech Week for an up-close conversation on building legal solutions in Canada.
As AI and tech continue rapidly transforming the world of law, what does it actually take to build, scale, and sustain game-changing legal tech solutions? Join us for an insightful and exclusive discussion with Clio's Chief Technology Officer, Jonathan Watson, about legal innovation, building for tomorrow and what the future holds.
What to Expect:
🔥Fireside Chat
Gain invaluable insights and a global leadership perspective from this candid conversation with Jonathan Watson, CTO of international legal tech leader Clio, moderated by TMU’s Legal Innovation Zone Director, Dr. Sasha Saunders. Drawing on Jonathan’s 20+ years of experience leading large-scale SaaS and engineering teams, the discussion will bridge the gap between technical strategy and industry-wide innovation.
Discussion topics will include:
➡️ Building in Canada: The unique opportunities and challenges of the Canadian legal tech ecosystem.
➡️ Successful Scaling: Lessons in product development, user experience and maintaining best-in-class customer retention.
➡️ Future Forecast and Takeaways: Where legal tech is headed and what today’s emerging innovators should be considering.
🎤 Audience Q&A
Have your voice heard! We’ll let the “campfire cool” with a dedicated Q&A session for the audience.
🤝Networking & Refreshments
Enjoy an afternoon pick-me-up of coffee, tea, and light snacks while connecting with fellow innovators, professionals and Toronto’s vibrant legal tech community.
Whether you are an innovator, legal professional, student, or law tech enthusiast, request your spot today!

Meet the Speakers:
Johnathan Watson
Chief Technology Officer, Clio 
Jonathan is the Chief Technology Officer at Clio. With over 20 years of leadership experience in AI transformation, large-scale SaaS, and live gaming solutions, Jonathan’s unique approach and passion for intertwining product development, design, user experience, and engineering has accelerated Clio’s product growth while earning Clio best-in-class NPS and customer retention rates. Outside of the office, he’s an endless tinkerer who is always exploring creative pursuits including 3D printing, cooking, and making his daughters laugh.
Dr. Sasha Saunders 
Director, Legal Innovation Zone
Sasha is the Director of the Legal Innovation Zone at Toronto Metropolitan University. She has over a decade of experience building innovation ecosystems in the UK and Canada, including creating, leading and growing a university-wide venture support service from the ground up. As a champion of the intersection of law, technology, and entrepreneurship, Sasha is passionate about empowering innovators to develop the skills and solutions to create meaningful impact. Outside of the office, you can find her on Lake Ontario in a sprint kayak—ideally right-side up.

properties.title: TMLS Agentic Hackathon
properties.description:
Presented by FGF Brands
For the past few years, "building an app" meant months of work, a full team, and a lot of luck.
That's changed.
Today, one person with the right AI tools can go from idea to working product in days. Cursor writes the code. Claude handles the logic. Bolt scaffolds the UI.
The bottleneck isn't skill anymore — it's imagination and execution speed.
The TMLS Agentic Hackathon is built around that reality. Five days. Real tools. Ship something that works.
No prescribed problem. No required stack. We want to see what you build when the only constraint is: make it real by Friday.
What does "agentic" mean?
It means your AI isn't just autocompleting lines — it's reasoning, taking actions, and building alongside you. Think less "chatbot," more "co-founder that never sleeps." Cursor, Claude Code, Bolt, Lovable, v0, Replit — use whatever gets you to shipped fastest.
Who should come?
Developers and ML engineers who want to actually ship something. Product people who have an idea and want to build it. First-timers who've been watching from the sidelines and are ready to jump in.
You don't need a team. Show up Monday and we'll find you one.
The week:
📅 Mon May 25 — Kickoff · CSI Spadina, 9 AM Pitches, team formation, project lock. Lunch provided.
💻 Tue–Thu — Build · Wherever you work best Async. Mentor support on Slack.
📬 Thu May 29 — Submit · 2 PM deadline
🏆 Fri May 30 — Expo + Awards · Wisedocs, 12–4 PM Science-fair format. Judges rotate. Winners announced. Lunch + drinks.
How projects are judged:
A panel of 5–7 judges from the TMLS community scores every project on five criteria:
Impact & usefulness — does it solve a real problem for a real person?
Technical execution — did you actually build it, or just mock it up?
Demo quality — can you show it working clearly and confidently?
Novelty & creativity — is there a fresh angle or clever approach?
Shipping mindset — does it feel like something that could go further?
Prizes:
🥇 Winner — tickets to TMLS 2026 (June 17–18, CIBC Square)
🥈 Runner-up — sponsor prize kit
🗳️ People's Choice — voted live by attendees
Offical Hackathon Rules
Questions? info@torontomachinelearning.com

Her work focuses on building safer, more efficient environments through operational excellence, analytical thinking, and client-centered solutions, while continuously exploring the intersection of security, innovation, and business growth.

Gharsa Amin is a Canadian entrepreneur, software engineer, and Founder & CEO of Kavodax — Canada's first stablecoin-powered cross-border payments platform, now live across 50+ countries. With a Master's in Public Administration specializing in financial crime management and experience across seven startups in Canada, the US, and Asia, she brings rare technical depth and a compliance-first mindset to global payments. Gharsa has worked with tier-one banks and Canada's largest exchanges, and today leads a female-led team redefining how money moves across borders. Named one of Spark Centre's Top 10 Women to Watch in 2026 and a national pitch competition champion, she is building the financial infrastructure the world runs on next — in five languages, and without taking no for an answer.
