UMMAHCON is a gathering for Muslim founders, builders, investors, operators, and professionals coming together during Toronto Tech Week in the heart of downtown Toronto.

As thousands across the city gather to build, invest, and shape what’s next in technology, this event creates a dedicated space for the Muslim tech and startup community to connect with intention. It brings together individuals across startups, venture capital, technology, and innovation who are building impactful companies, supporting founders, and contributing to the broader ecosystem.

Whether you're launching a startup, investing in the next generation of companies, working as an operator inside high-growth teams, or exploring the intersection of faith, entrepreneurship, and technology, the gathering is designed to bring together people who share both ambition and purpose.

Expect meaningful conversations, new connections, and an environment where Muslim talent across the tech ecosystem can meet, collaborate, and support one another. From founders and engineers to investors, designers, and students entering the space, the focus is on strengthening relationships and celebrating the growing presence of Muslims building within tech.

Join us during Toronto Tech Week for an intentional gathering of community, ideas, and people building what’s next.

Build to Last. Build with Purpose.

Hosted by UMMAH1

properties.title: Founder Pokébattle @ Toronto Tech Week
properties.description:
Cue the Battle Music
Forget pitch decks and cap tables — bring up type charts and move tables. We're going head to head in a literal Pokemon Showdown.
Patagonia vests are out.
Blue vests with all your gym badges are in.
When the dust settles, only 1 founder will hold the title of Toronto Tech Week's Ultimate Pokémon Master.
Your Ticket Includes: Appetizers & Drinks & A Pre-Formulated Pokemon Team Draft & Tournament


The Pokemon Format
We use a battling simulator called Pokemon Showdown. You can use it on your phone/laptop. No need to own a Switch or any actual Nintendo/Pokemon games.
We're going to battle using the ruelset for National Dex Monotype (Singles). This is like being a 'Gym Leader' in Pokemon, where all 6 of your Pokemon will share a type.
We will have 19 teams prepared for you - one for each Pokemon type, and at the start we'll each draft a type/team & an opponent (snake draft style). We will take care of ensuring all teams are viable & competitively balanced (EV spreads, items, movesets, etc.). You just show up!
Using your team, we'll do some round robins & a double elimination tournament to decide the Toronto Tech Week Pokemon League Champion!

properties.title: TfD Film Club: The End of The Internet
properties.description:
Join Technologists for Democracy as we watch Dylan Reibling’s THE END OF THE INTERNET at the Revue Cinema!
From Cold War military roots to Big Tech dominance, a journey through Berlin squats, Catalan churches, and Brazilian villages reveals movements fighting to decentralize the network beyond "the cloud.”
Before each screening, audiences are invited to join a secret server hidden inside the cinema — a private, offline network, completely disconnected from the internet. Inside, a parallel version of the film unfolds: raw footage, deleted scenes, research fragments, rough notes, live chat rooms, and hidden easter eggs — materials that exist nowhere else.
This ephemeral installation opens 30 minutes before the screening and disappears at the end of the night.
We’ll be meeting up at a the Bandit Brewery (2125 Dundas St W, Toronto ON M6R 1X1) after for casual drinks and conversation after the film and Q&A with director Dylan Reibling.
Schedule
6:00 PM - 6:30 PM - Doors open at the Revue Cinema / interactive installation
6:35 PM - 8:03 PM - Film screening (88 mins)
8:05 PM - 8:20 PM - Q&A
8:30 PM - Drinks at Bandit Brewery (5 min walk)
Film Trailer:
https://theendoftheinternetfilm.com/watch-the-trailer
Please note that tickets for the screening need to be purchased separately as well ($17.50): https://revuecinema.ca/films/revue-event-the-end-of-the-internet-director-in-attendance-hybrid-installation/
Presented by Technologists for Democracy and Game Theory Films

properties.title: AI at Lyft: From Data Foundations to Shipping Faster
properties.description:
Join Lyft Engineering during Toronto Tech Week 2026!
AI only works when the foundation does. In this fast-paced lightning session, two Lyft practitioners cover both ends of the spectrum—followed by a panel discussion on lessons learned, trade-offs, and what's next.

Part 1 — Unifying Data and Metadata for Reliable AI
Shankar Vedaraman, Head of Corporate Data & Analytics

Most AI failures aren't model failures—they're data failures. Shankar shares how Lyft is converging a fragmented data estate into a metadata-driven platform, the prerequisite for trustworthy AI at enterprise scale.

Part 2 — Expanding Lyft to Europe with Cursor and Claude
Arshiya Sayyed, Engineering

A continent-scale Lyft + FreeNow integration should have taken a year of ramp-up. It didn't. Arshiya's team is building Roaming—letting Lyft riders hail rides across Europe—and she'll share how Cursor powered by Claude transformed how they plan, write, and review code.

We'll close with a panel discussion, connecting data foundations to developer velocity and exploring what it really takes to ship AI-powered systems faster in production. We'll share our perspectives on transforming Lyft's engineering towards AI-first development.

This isn't a panel or a keynote. It's a candid, unscripted conversation with someone who's actually done it — built from zero to a $5B+ valuation without going public. Mike will get into the real inflection points: the decisions that compounded, the mistakes that cost time, and the mindset shifts that separate founders who scale from founders who plateau. Founders doing $1M+ in revenue who are past the survival stage and now navigating the harder questions — how to scale the team, how to hold culture, how to think about capital, and how to build something that lasts. If you're at that stage, this room was built for you.

properties.title: Tech Worries and Woes: Real Talk Working in the Age of AI
properties.description:
Rounds and rounds of layoffs. Forced AI mandates. Workloads piling up on smaller and smaller teams. Are you worried about increasing surveillance and control in the workplace? Does it feel like a job in tech is no longer the “good career” that it has been in the past? And– to top it all off– you can't say anything about it at work?
You are not alone.
Join fellow tech workers in a safe space to share your frustrations and uncertainties navigating today’s tech world. Connect with your peers, share your story, and build solidarity with fellow workers. All tech workers, whether engineering, product management, designers, analysts and/or sales are welcome.
About Tech Workers Coalition Canada
We are a coalition of workers in and around the tech industry, labor organizers, community organizers, and friends. We work in solidarity with existing movements towards social justice, workers' rights, and economic inclusion.

properties.title: PersonyFi Demo Night: Your Money Has a Story. Does Your App Know It?
properties.description:
You've got a solid income, multiple accounts, RSUs on the way, a mortgage, a lease. On paper, you're doing well. But ask your finance app "can I afford to buy a house?" and it stares blankly back at you because it only knows your transactions.
The rules of your financial life aren't in your bank feed. They're in PDFs. Contracts. Grant letters. Documents no app has ever been able to read, until now.
PersonyFi fuses structured bank data with unstructured financial documents to build a complete financial picture. Join us for an evening demo during Toronto Tech Week.

properties.title: The Rise of Agentic AI in Travel (Live Podcast Recording)
properties.description:
How Autonomous AI Will Transform Travel in 2026 and Beyond
What happens when AI agents start planning, booking, and managing travel on our behalf?
A new generation of agentic AI systems is emerging, capable of making decisions, executing complex tasks, and managing entire travel workflows. From trip planning and booking to real-time itinerary management, these autonomous systems could fundamentally reshape how travel is discovered, purchased, and experienced.
During Toronto Tech Week, join Travel Trends Podcast and Intrepid Travel for a special live podcast recording exploring one of the most important technological shifts facing the travel industry.
This interactive evening will feature an incredible panel of travel industry executives, startup entrepreneurs, and technology leaders discussing how agentic AI is already beginning to impact travel companies and what it means for the future of the industry in 2026 and beyond.
Unlike a traditional panel, this will be a live podcast experience, with opportunities for audience questions, interaction, and discussion during the recording.
What to Expect
Live recording of the Travel Trends Podcast
Expert panel discussion on Agentic AI and the future of travel
Audience interaction and Q&A
Startup demos and partner booths showcasing Toronto’s travel tech ecosystem
Networking party with founders, operators, investors, and travel innovators
Fun CPG swag by companies supporting your next travel experience (No Days Wasted, Zenergy, Greenhouse Juice Co, and more!)
After the recording, stay for a fantastic networking reception designed to help connect the people shaping the future of travel. Meet fellow builders, exchange ideas, and deepen relationships across the travel and technology ecosystem.
Whether you're a founder, travel executive, technologist, investor, or operator, this evening is an opportunity to learn, connect, and help shape how AI will transform our industry.
Come curious. Leave connected.
If you are interested in partnership & sponsorship opportunities for this event, please reach out to mellonie@traveltrendspodcast.com

🌍 Agenda-
5:30 PM – 6:15 PM - Registration, Networking & Live Podcast Recording
6:15 PM – 7:00 PM - Panel Discussion: The Rise of Agentic AI and the Future of Travel
7:15 PM – 9:00 PM - Networking & Drinks

🌍 Speakers
🎤 Dan Christian 
Founder & Host, Travel Trends Podcast
Dan Christian is a global travel industry executive, entrepreneur, and founder/host of the Travel Trends Podcast—the world’s #1 B2B travel podcast with listeners in over 125 countries. A seasoned leader, he previously served as Chief Digital Officer at The Travel Corporation, overseeing global digital transformation and marketing across 40 brands. Dan has also held senior roles at Lonely Planet and G Adventures bringing deep expertise across travel, media, and loyalty.
🎤 Christian Wolters 
President for Canada and GM of Marketing, Intrepid Travel
Christian Wolters is a travel executive with 20+ years growing brands like Intrepid Travel and TourRadar. Intrepid is the world’s largest adventure travel company and a B Corp, offering sustainable small group tours in 120+ countries. Christian drives growth in North America, focusing on revenue, partnerships, and innovation, and is passionate about using technology to enhance efficiency and personalization while preserving the human touch.
🎤 Brian Tossan
Chief Technology Officer, Toronto Pearson International Airport
Brian Tossan brings more than two decades of extensive experience to his role as the leader of Information Technology and Innovation at Toronto Pearson International Airport, serving as a driving force for technological advancement, pioneering new horizons in airport operations and enhancing passenger experiences through human-centric solutions.
🎤 Jeff Kischuk
Co-Founder & CEO, Tripian
Jeff Kischuk is Co-founder and CEO of Tripian, a B2B travel infrastructure company. He built the verified, structured data layer and orchestration engine that powers agentic AI across card networks, cruise operators, tourism boards, and global DMCs. An engineer and serial founder (four times over), Jeff has been running Tripian's Nested AI® platform, purpose-built for agentic execution, in production since 2016.
Additional panelists to be announced.

🌍 Who Should Attend
Founders, travel operators, product leaders, technologists, and travel industry experts curious about how AI will reshape the way we discover, plan, and experience travel.
Please note - we will review and approve registrations on an on-going basis and prioritizing executives, founders, operators and folks specifically working in the travel space. We apologize if we may not be able to accommodate all RSVPs.
See you there! ✈️

properties.title: The Showcase Room: AI / Built for Non-IT Founders.
properties.description:
The Showcase Room: AI / Built for Non-IT Founders.



🧠 A curated night for Non-IT businesses done with business card theatre.
You’ve been to the networking events.
You know how they go.
You walk in → collect cards → give cards → repeat the same conversation seven times → go home → follow up with two people → one replies.
Three weeks later… you’re not sure why you went.
This isn’t that.

🪑 Welcome to The Showcase Room
A curated event for Canadian businesses running at least 3+ years who are done with surface-level networking.
You don’t walk in as a name on a badge.
You walk in with:
Your business materials
A roll-up banner
A 1-minute stage pitch
…in front of a room that’s actually paying attention.

⚙️ Then we build
The second half of the night is the AI for SMBs Lab (SMB - Small Medium Business). This is a working session for business owners who want to walk out with a real solution, not a list of tools they’ll never use.
Bring:
The operational headache
The marketing bottleneck
The sales process held together with tape
We’ll work through it live.
👉 You leave with something working
👉 Not something theoretical
Bring a laptop. Bring a real problem.

🔄 And the conversation doesn’t stop
The 10 showcase tables stay up through the Lab.
So does the conversation.
This is the first pilot of a planned recurring series:
Village Hive × Segmentide × The AI Collective Canada x Toronto Tech Week 2026
The room is small on purpose.
The same businesses come back.
That’s how real collaboration compounds.

🗓️ Run of Show
3:00 PM — Showcase setup (showcase businesses only)
4:00 PM — Doors open, walk the room, meet the showcase
4:30 PM — Stage pitches (10 × 1-minute)
4:45 PM — AI for SMBs Lab begins
6:00 PM — Post-Lab networking (tables still live)
7:00 PM — Wrap

🎟️ Two Ways to Join
🧾 Business Showcase Table — $50
For Canadian businesses running 3+ years
Includes:
Table + chair
Space for one roll-up banner
1-minute stage pitch
Table stays live through Lab + networking
⚠️ 10 spots only — application required

💡 AI for SMBs Lab Attendee — $10
For founders and business owners who want to bring a real problem and walk out with a working AI-driven solution.
🎟️ 30 spots — first come, first served

Total capacity: 40

👥 Who This Is For
Non-IT founders.
Caterers. Retailers. Agency owners. Consultants. Accountants. Product + service businesses.
People with 3+ years on the clock and the scars to prove it.
People curious about AI, but tired of being talked at about it.

👩🏻‍🏫Facilated By

properties.title: Bet on Yourself: Women in Tech Poker Night
properties.description:
Poker is more than a game. It’s a masterclass in decision-making, risk, and reading the room.
Bet on Yourself is an intimate, hands-on experience designed for women in tech who want to build confidence in high-stakes situations, both at the table and in their careers.
In a space that has historically been male-dominated, this night is about learning how to take your seat, trust your instincts, and make bold, calculated moves.
No poker experience is required.
We’ll start by teaching the fundamentals of the game, then transition into live play where you’ll practice:
Making decisions with incomplete information
Assessing risk and reward in real time
Managing emotions under pressure
Reading people, not just the cards
This isn’t about becoming a professional poker player.
It’s about building the mindset and skills to navigate negotiations, career moves, and leadership moments with clarity and confidence.
You won’t just learn how to play the game.
You’ll learn how to play your hand.
Seats are intentionally limited to keep the experience personal, interactive, and high-impact.

properties.description:

This is a small, informal session designed for Toronto tech leaders who want a clear-eyed picture of their privacy obligations without the jargon. In 30 focused minutes, you'll walk away knowing what you're required to have, what you're probably missing, and where to focus first.

Before (or after) the talk (we'll split into groups), we'll move through a guided pilates session to match - because if you're going to strengthen your core, you might as well strengthen both of them!

Spots are limited. Comfortable clothes encouraged. Note that indemnity forms must be completed before attending the Pilates portion.

properties.title: AI & Defence Innovation Summit 2026: The Future of Digital Trust & Defence Innovation
properties.description:
AI & Defence 2026
Toronto Tech Week is a citywide celebration of the people building what’s next. From May 25–29, 2026, founders, investors, and builders come together for hundreds of community-led events across Toronto, connecting tens of thousands of people around Canadian tech. Torontotechweek.com

AI and Defence 2026: AI Trust and Defence Innovation is a premier 3.0‑hour executive summit taking place in Toronto, Canada, on May 26. This hybrid event brings together leading voices from defence, augmented and artificial intelligence, cybersecurity, aerospace, academia, and government.
Co‑hosted by James Castle, Chairperson of the Cyber Security Global Alliance, and the CSGA Cyber AI Research Institute, along with Master of Ceremony Samir Azizi, Co‑Partner of Toronto DAO, and Toronto Tech Week, this summit will explore the future of AI trust, AI weaponization, autonomous defence systems, quantum and AI‑ready security, next‑generation defence innovations, and the governance frameworks shaping global security over the decade ahead.
Designed for senior executives, policymakers, military leaders, defence innovators, advanced technology strategists, and Toronto Tech Week participants, this mini summit delivers a powerful combination of strategic insights, practical defence models, and an immersive crisis simulation that challenges participants to navigate real‑world national security threats.
Through expert dialogue and focused analysis, attendees will gain a strong understanding of the future of AI trust, secure AI systems, autonomous defence technologies, drone‑based threat response, and quantum‑era cybersecurity.
This event offers a rare opportunity to examine how trusted AI, cross‑border governance standards, and resilient defence capabilities are shaping North America's strategic advantage. It will help leaders prepare for the next era of rapidly accelerating technological conflict and innovation.
To see our list of in‑person and virtual speakers or to visit our sponsorship page, please go to the CSGA website and select the Events tab.
We Are Seeking Event Sponsors
Cyber Security Global Alliance and Toronto DAO are actively seeking sponsors for this event.

If you are interested in sponsoring our event or receiving more detailed information on our event, please visit us online: https://csga-global.org/events-and-community-partners
Hybrid Event Details
This event is hosted in person in downtown Toronto with an option for virtual attendance for guests outside the city. Several speakers will join via our main event screen and audio system, and all registered participants will have the opportunity to ask questions after each presentation to both in‑person and virtual presenters.
All attendees must be registered. At the registration table, please be prepared to show your assigned QR code.
Thank you.

properties.title: Screen Life: Finding Balance in a World Full of Screens
properties.description:
Overview
We're living in a world of technology designed to keep you hooked - and most of us are navigating the balance between on and offline completely alone.
This practical workshop is for founders, operators, parents, and partners seeking a more intentional relationship with technology at work and at home. Hear expert perspectives on focus, burnout, relationships, parenting, and AI - grounded in research and delivered without judgment.
You'll take a personal assessment, hear from practitioners working on the front lines, and leave with a concrete plan built for your actual life.

Who Should Attend
Founders and operators navigating constant digital demands
Parents thinking critically about technology use at home
Professionals looking to improve focus, boundaries, and overall well-being

Key Takeaways
You’ll leave with:
A clearer understanding of how technology is shaping focus, relationships, and daily life
Practical ways to recognize and manage burnout linked to constant connectivity
Insight into how screen use impacts parenting, partnerships, and work dynamics
A personal framework and plan to build a more intentional relationship with technology

properties.title: AI Build Lab @ Toronto Tech Week
properties.description:
From idea → prototype in 1 day. No more waiting.
Most people don’t lack ideas.
They lack the space, structure, and support to actually start.
During Toronto Tech Week, we’re changing that.
Why This Event Exists
We’ve seen this pattern across thousands of students, professionals, and founders:
“I’ve been thinking about this idea for months…”
“I want to build something but don’t know where to start…”
“I’m not technical, so I don’t think I can…”
The reality today?
👉 AI has changed the game.
👉 The barrier to building is lower than ever.
👉 But execution is still the biggest gap.
This event is designed to close that gap.
Not through another panel.
Not through theory.
But by getting you to actually build something, in the room.
What This Experience Is
The AI Build Lab is a 5–6 hour guided sprint + workshop + showcase designed to help you go from:
👉 idea → problem clarity → MVP → live demo
All in one day.
You’ll work in curated teams (with both technical + non-technical participants), supported by mentors, and move through a structured build process used by real product teams.
As outlined in the workshop structure, each team is supported by a dedicated mentor and follows a clear build framework focused on defining the user, problem, MVP, and execution path .
👀 Who This Is For
This is intentionally designed to be inclusive of all skill levels:
Developers & engineers
Product managers, designers, operators
Students & early-career professionals
Newcomers exploring opportunities in tech
Non-technical individuals curious about AI
Aspiring founders & side-project builders
👉 You do NOT need:
a full idea
a technical background
prior experience building
Just curiosity + willingness to try.
What You’ll Actually Do
This isn’t a sit-and-listen event.
You will:
Learn how AI tools can be used to build real products
Understand how to think like a product builder
Work in a team to define a real problem
Scope a realistic MVP (not overbuilt ideas)
Build a working prototype using AI tools
Get feedback from mentors
Share your work with the community
What You’ll Leave With
By the end of the day, you won’t just have “learned something.”
You’ll have:
✅ A real project or prototype
✅ Hands-on experience using AI tools
✅ A portfolio-ready build (huge for job seekers)
✅ New collaborators, mentors, and connections
✅ A repeatable framework to keep building
For many, this becomes:
your first project
your first proof of work
or the start of something much bigger
High-Level Agenda
🟢 11:00 – 11:30 | Arrival + Networking
🟢 11:30 – 12:00 | Welcome + Introduction
🟢 12:00 – 1:30 | Skill-Leveling + Live Demo
🟢 1:30 – 2:00 | Team Formation + Scoping
🟢 2:00 – 5:30 | 🚀 Build Sprint (With real-time mentor support throughout)
🟢 5:30 – 6:00 | Closing + Networking
About the Hosts
Re-Defined is a global community supporting 50,000+ international students, newcomers, and early-career professionals to bridge the gap between potential and opportunity through mentorship, events, and real-world experience.
Stan, built by Creators for Creators, Stan combines Stanley, an AI content partner for Instagram and LinkedIn, with Stan Store, an all‑in‑one hub. With over 80,000 active users and over $500M earned through the platform since 2021.

properties.title: DMZ x SHAKE SHACK: Burgers & Builders - Open House
properties.description:
Burgers & Builders Open House 🍔🍟
Ever wonder what goes on at a world-leading tech incubator?
Join us during Toronto Tech Week for our Burgers & Builders Open House. You’ll meet the founders behind some of the standout startups in our portfolio, and get a firsthand look at what the next generation of companies is building.
We’ll be serving Shake Shack alongside a high-calibre room of founders, investors and ecosystem leaders.
Capacity is limited. Attendance is by application only.
About DMZ
DMZ is a tech incubator and startup ecosystem that fuels entrepreneurship in Canada and beyond. Through its award-winning programs, DMZ empowers founders to scale high-impact ventures, helps students develop entrepreneurial mindsets and equips professionals to thrive in today’s fast-moving economy.
To date, DMZ has supported 2630+ startups in raising $3.09 billion in capital and has created over 25,400 jobs. Headquartered in Toronto, DMZ operates a global network of hubs spanning 15+ countries.
About Shake Shack Canada
Shake Shack serves elevated versions of American classics using only the best ingredients. Every burger is made to order using premium 100% Canadian Angus beef, served alongside the Chicken Shack™, featuring whole white-meat Canadian chicken raised cage-free and without antibiotics. Rounding out the menu are classic crinkle-cut fries and hand-spun shakes made with Canadian dairy.
Shake Shack's purpose is to Stand For Something Good®, from its premium ingredients and elevated hospitality to its deep community investment.

properties.title: LangChain Meetup: Building Deep Agents and Deploying to Production (Toronto)
properties.description:
Join us for an evening in Toronto with Haider Zaidi, Deployed Engineer @ LangChain, and Jasen Mackie, Senior Principal AI Engineer @ Questrade.
Most teams can build agents, but far fewer have the infrastructure or know-how to run them reliably in production. In this meetup, Haider Zaidi will walk through what it actually takes to deploy long-running agents and the runtime capabilities that make it possible. We'll cover how to:
Deploy agents with durable execution so runs survive crashes, deploys, and long waits for human input
Use checkpoints and memory stores
Add human-in-the-loop gates for consequential decisions
We'll also discuss how teams operationalize this in practice, from sandboxed code execution to scheduled runs and open integrations via MCP and A2A.
Then, see deep agents in practice with Jasen! Jasen will share his journey partnering with LangChain, and using LangSmith and deep agents at Questrade.
Featuring:
Haider Zaidi, Deployed Engineer @ LangChain
Jasen Mackie, Senior Principal AI Engineer @ Questrade
Agenda ⏰
5:15 PM: Doors Open + Refreshments + Pizza
6:00 PM: Presentation with Haider Zaidi (LangChain) - Building Deep Agents and Deploying to Production
6:20 PM: Presentation with Jasen Mackie (Questrade) -Deep Agents: See them in Practice!
6:40 PM: Q&A Session
6:55 PM: Networking + Pizza + Refreshments
8:30 PM: Event Ends
🎤 Please note this event is fully in-person in Toronto and will not be live-streamed online
📍 Location: Financial District – address provided upon approval.
🎟️ We can only admit guests with approved registrations. If you’re still on the waitlist or haven’t received a confirmation yet, please stay tuned for future events—but please sit this one out.

About LangChain:
LangChain provides the agent engineering platform used by millions of developers globally. LangSmith brings observability, evaluation, and deployment together in one platform so teams can iterate and improve production agents. LangSmith works with any stack, including our open source frameworks like Deep Agents and custom code.

LangSmith powers top AI teams, from startups to global enterprises -- including Cloudflare, Toyota, Rippling, and Clay. Learn more: https://www.langchain.com/

properties.title: Greensoil's 2026 Summit- Why the Physical World Wins the AI Era
properties.description:
Greensoil's one-day Summit is for the people building, funding, and operating the future of real assets.

This year, our Summit explores the theme of Proptech and AI in the Real World.  The noise around AI and PropTech has never been louder. The question for 2026 isn’t whether technology is transforming real estate, it’s which signals are worth following, and which structures are being built to last.

Physical AI is turning buildings and infrastructure from passive containers into intelligent, responsive assets. Energy is the connective tissue - as on-site generation, storage, and AI-driven demand management converge, the building and the energy asset are becoming one.  For the firms paying attention, decarbonization isn’t a mandate. It’s a margin driver.

This Summit brings together limited partners, real estate developers, energy innovators, and portfolio company leaders for a full day of candid conversation, live product demonstrations, and collaborative problem-solving.  Practitioners-deploying capital, building products, operating assets-working through the questions that actually matter in 2026.

properties.title: AI for the Rest of Us: A Roundtable for Small Business Owners
properties.description:
AI is everywhere, but if you run a small business, what actually works?
Join a room full of business owners for an honest conversation about the AI tools that are saving them time, the ones that flopped, and the problems nobody's solved yet.
This isn't a keynote. It's not a "listening" event. It's a roundtable where real business owners share what they're actually using day to day: from handling customer calls and messages, to scheduling, marketing, follow-ups, and the admin that eats your week.
What we'll cover:
Keeping up with customer calls and messages when you're busy
Scheduling and admin that takes hours out of your day
Marketing without a marketing team
Following up with leads before they go cold
What's worth paying for vs. what's free and good enough
By the end, we'll compile a shared list of every AI tool the room recommends, sourced from the people who actually use them, not a sponsored listicle.
Who this is for:
Small business owners, solopreneurs, freelancers, and operators across any industry. No technical background needed. If you run a business and you're curious about AI, you belong in this room.
Seats are limited to keep the conversation real.

properties.title: What Kills Startups (Every Time) - Encore!
properties.description:
Success is overhyped. Failure is patterned—and preventable.
Everyone teaches how to win. Almost no one studies how startups actually die.
And they don’t die randomly.
Most failures follow the same patterns—long before the market, product, or team gets blamed. This session breaks down what actually kills startups—and how to avoid it.
Hear from Albert Lin, Managing Partner, and a US venture capital GP, sharing lessons from the trenches on what separates companies that survive from those that don’t.
🎯 What you’ll walk away with
The most common (and preventable) startup failure patterns
How capital structure quietly determines outcomes
Where governance breaks—and how to fix it early
What experienced investors look for beyond upside
If you’re building or investing in a startup, this is the part no one teaches—and the part that matters most when things go wrong.
If you missed our Monday session, this is encore!

properties.description:
About the Event
THE SOVEREIGN GALA: GENESIS is not your typical Toronto networking event. This will be the most prestigious event of Toronto Tech Week! Prepare for an evening of elegance, mystery, and powerful connections with entrepreneurs, investors, founders, and influential leaders.
We are Proud to be an Official Partner of Toronto Tech Week.
Set in a luxurious black-tie atmosphere, this masquerade blends elegance with connection. A selection of Gourmet food, and an Open Bar to share conversations. Behind the masks, guests  you will meet include some of the most ambitious minds across Tech, AI, Real Estate, Venture Capital, and Startups. Expect a refined social environment where meaningful conversations, partnerships, and new ideas can emerge naturally.

What To Expect
🍽️ Food Experience
Enjoy elevated food stations throughout the night:
• Oven-Baked Custom Pizza Station – Fresh pizzas made to order
• Chef-Curated Gourmet Bites served throughout the evening
• Artisan Gelato Station with premium flavors
• Delicious dinner plates perfect for mingling and socializing

🍸 Drinks & Bar
A premium bar experience designed for the night:
• Signature Masquerade Cocktails crafted exclusively for the event
• Open Bar with premium spirits, wine, and beer
• Drinks curated by professional mixologists

🎭 Black & Gold Masquerade
Enter a stunning black-and-gold themed venue where masks and black-tie attire create an atmosphere of elegance and intrigue.
The masquerade setting adds mystery while making conversations more relaxed and memorable.
(If you dont have a Masquerade Mask, they will be provided)

💎 GEMSTONE FINANCE PRESENTS

Browse and bid in a silent gemstone auction. featuring a limited collection of unique certified precious gems from the Gemstone Finance vaults. Timeless beauty and value presented in a curated gemstone browsing experience, and at the end of the night, they can be yours forever. We will be announcing more specifics as we draw closer to the Gala.

Every attendee will also be entered into a draw to win a Certified Precious Gemstone with an appraised value of over $1000! Details to be announced.

Expect a vibrant environment with great music by DJ Talmor, great people, and meaningful conversations, all designed to spark new ideas, partnerships, and opportunities.

Itinerary

Guests arrive, check in, and step into the black and gold masquerade atmosphere.
Luxury Limo Shuttle/Party Bus Downtown Toronto Pickup Starts at 7:00pm (Details will be shared with approved guests)

Enjoy the best of Toronto Tech Week while enjoying music, food, the gemstones on display, the silent auction and open bar in an elegant setting.
11:00 PM – 1:00 AM | Final Connections & Closing Moments
Exchange contacts, continue conversations, and head out to the after parties! (Official Partner After Parties TBD)

Join Us on WhatsApp
Be part of our growing WhatsApp Community! Continue the conversations, share information, and connect with our growing community by clicking here.

An evening of elegance and mystery. Guests are expected to wear formal black tie attire paired with a masquerade mask.
Gentlemen are expected to wear a tuxedo or any color formal suit with bow tie.
Ladies are encouraged to wear evening gowns or elegant cocktail dresses.

Black, white, gold, metallics, and deep jewel/gemstone tones.

properties.title: Nervous System Regulation for Busy Techpreneurs
properties.description:
Hosted at Supernatural, Toronto’s premier longevity lifestyle destination, this immersive, guided experience blends sound bath, breathwork, and deep listening to help you reset and recalibrate your nervous system.
The experience takes place in The Portal: a one-of-a-kind somatic sound studio with therapeutic light. Through guided breath patterns, layered sound, and intentional listening, you’ll be invited to shift attention inward—experiencing sound not just through the ears, but through the body.
The Portal was created as an intimate space for experiential classes, storytelling, and community-led sessions—designed to deepen awareness, regulate the nervous system, and expand how we perceive and process sound.
THE FLOW:
5:30pm Arrival and check in
5:45pm Class begins
6:45pm Closing and discussion
Participants will also receive a complimentary RE:SET Frequency Therapy session: a proprietary sound, light, and vibration experience that is exclusive to Supernatural in Toronto. RE:SET uses scientifically calibrated frequencies delivered through sound and vibration to shift your brainwaves and body into a parasympathetic state, where rest, repair, and regulation occur. As you recline in a zero-gravity chair, custom audio and vibroacoustic feedback create a full-body resonance that synchronizes the mind and body, helping to downshift stress and recalibrate your emotional and energetic systems.
As a bonus to the experience, attendees will also receive 20% off inner optimization treatments at Supernatural (excluding custom IV drips, NAD+ therapies, and retail).
• • • • •
ABOUT SUPERNATURAL
Supernatural is Toronto’s premier longevity lifestyle destination—an integrated space for advanced recovery, regenerative aesthetics, and science-informed wellness. Designed to support how you feel and function every day, it brings inner and outer optimization together in one place—so you can feel good now, age later.

properties.title: Chill Chats: Tech & Curious
properties.description:
🍦 Chill Chats: Tech & Curious
Presented by The New Hire

Ready to scoop up some fresh ideas and make real connections? The New Hire is back with our next event, Chill Chats: Product Meet Up.

We’re bringing back Chill Chats after the incredible energy and turnout from last year — a laid-back evening designed to bring together aspiring and seasoned professionals across tech for a little inspiration, a lot of conversation, and some good vibes.

Whether you work in product, marketing, sales, customer success, operations — or you’re simply tech-curious — this night is for you.
Last year, we had people come out from companies like Mastercard, Interac, EcoVadis, and Uber to connect, learn more about different avenues within tech, and meet others navigating similar career questions and ambitions.
Come share what you’re building, exploring, dreaming about, or questioning in a casual, collaborative space where no one’s pitching, but everyone’s listening.
Scrap traditional networking. No panels. No pressure. Just good conversations with people who are curious about growth, careers, and the many paths within tech.

✨ Whether you’re:
Looking to pivot into tech
Curious about different roles or functions in the industry
Early in your career and wanting to grow your circle
Or simply looking to meet thoughtful, ambitious people
We are right with you.

 Event Details:
🗓️ Date: May 27
🕗 Time: 5:30-8:00 PM
📍 Location: On Third Thought (6 Markham St, Toronto, ON M6J 1E4)
What to expect:
🍨 A flight of artisanal gelato. As this event is free, we encourage you to purchase a scoop or drink in support of On Third Thought.
🍷 Curated wine pairings from around the globe
💬 Chill convos with product minds at every stage
✨ A zero-pressure space to ask questions, share insights, and connect
No slide decks. No stiff networking.
Spots are limited — grab yours and come chill with us.

 _ _

About The New Hire:

We’re on a mission to bridge the gap between education and the workplace, while enjoying life’s moments in between.
Every month our social networking club hosts events for us to connect. With a supportive group of professionals we lift one another up, while keeping each other accountable through:
💼 Networking and fostering relationships
💼 Career development and learning
💼 Mentorship and support

properties.title: Brand Health: Building Brands People Feel — Fireside + Sober Party at Canada's First Non-Alcoholic Bar
properties.description:
A fireside chat with Ivan Ho and Kyle Collier, moderated by Ryan Peerwani — followed by Toronto Tech Week's most unexpected after-party: a professional DJ, full sauna and cold plunge, and Canada's first fully non-alcoholic bar.
Run of show
7:00–7:30 — Welcome in the sauna. Get loose, meet the room, settle in.
7:30–8:00 — Fireside chat with Ivan Ho (NRG Haus) and Kyle Collier (Neobanc), moderated by Ryan Peerwani
8:00–10:30 — Sober party. Pro DJ, full mocktail bar, sauna and cold plunge open all night.
The founders
Ivan Ho is the founder of NRG Haus and the operator behind Fit Factory Fitness — recognised as Toronto's Best Boot Camp, Best Gym, and Best Fitness Club multiple times. Thirteen years of building a fitness brand people are genuinely devoted to, and a new venture (the one you're sitting in) that opened to a waiting community before it ever ran an ad.
Kyle Collier is the CEO and Co-founder of Neobanc — a Systems Design Engineer from UWaterloo, serial fintech entrepreneur with 15+ years of experience and multiple exits, who has grown past startups to over 80 million users globally and spoken at the United Nations on fintech innovation. Neobanc itself? 800% growth in five months on only half a million dollars of funding — the math only works when users bring users. That's not a funnel. That's a cult.
Different industries, same thesis: brands people feel aren't built by marketing. They're built by founders who understand what they're actually solving, then earn trust one user at a time. The brand is the relationship.
Notes
Bring swimwear for the sauna welcome — towels and robes provided
Phones go in lockers. Be present.
Functional mocktails all night — Lion's Mane, Ashwagandha, vitamins. Stay sharp.
About the space
NRG Haus is Toronto's first social wellness club — a 5,000 sq ft Liberty Village space combining guided contrast therapy, functional beverages, IV recovery, and intentional social design under one roof. Inside: a 55-person sauna, four immersive cold-plunge tubs each paired with a 16-foot LED screen running ocean waves and icefields, a tea lounge with a water-vapour fireplace, and a zero-proof bar pouring adaptogenic drinks built around Lion's Mane, Ashwagandha, and vitamins instead of alcohol. Phones go in lockers. Be fully present. You leave grounded, energized, and clear-headed — not depleted.

properties.title: AI in the Dev Trenches: A Roundtable on What's Actually Working
properties.description:
Every dev team is experimenting with AI, but most conversations about it happen on stage, not at the table. This roundtable flips that. Developers, engineering managers, and technical leaders sit together and share what's actually working in their day-to-day: the tools, the workflows, the wins, and the failures. No keynotes. No pitches. Just practitioners learning from each other.
This is a structured peer conversation, not a panel. Each participant gets the floor to share how their team is using AI across the software development lifecycle: from code generation and review to testing, debugging, documentation, and deployment. After each share, the table opens up for questions, pushback, and "we tried that too" moments. The goal is to leave with tactics you can bring back to your team on Monday.
Topics we expect to surface include
Where AI pair programming tools deliver real productivity gains versus where they create false confidence
How teams are integrating AI into code review, QA, and CI/CD pipelines
Managing the learning curve when rolling out AI tools across a dev team
The impact on junior developer growth when AI handles boilerplate
Security, IP, and compliance considerations engineering leaders are navigating
What's overhyped and what's quietly transforming how teams ship software
This roundtable is for you if you write code, manage people who write code, or make tooling decisions for a development team. Whether you're deep into AI-assisted development or just getting started, you'll find value in hearing what peers across Toronto's tech ecosystem have learned through real implementation.
Space is limited to keep the conversation intimate and high-signal. Come ready to share and learn.

properties.title: Anchored in Innovation: Australia Canada Case Study - Catalyzing Clinical AI
properties.description:
What happens when frontline clinical reality meets AI innovation, national leadership, and global collaboration?
Anchoram Canada is pleased to host an executive roundtable featuring Dr. Simon Kos, Global Chief Medical Officer at Heidi Health, and Dr. Rashaad Bhyat, Senior Clinical Leader at Canada Health Infoway, for a focused discussion on the future of AI in healthcare.
Bringing together perspectives from Australia and Canada, this session will explore how leading global practices can be translated into scalable impact within the Canadian healthcare system.
Dr. Kos shares the perspective of a physician leader who has practiced critical care medicine in Australia and led global digital health initiatives at Microsoft and other international organizations over the past two decades. 
Dr. Bhyat offers a national view, advancing digital health adoption across Canada and shaping interoperability, clinician engagement, and system-wide innovation.
Among our discussion topics, we’ll explore:
How AI is moving from pilot to real-world clinical impact
What it takes to drive clinician adoption at scale
Patient impact of AI
The role of interoperability, data quality, and workflow integration
How global lessons from Australia can be applied in Canada
Designed for senior clinical, technology and policy leaders, this executive roundtable focuses on moving beyond experimentation to measurable transformation.
Space is limited and there will be a waitlist for this highly anticipated event.
Expect a dynamic exchange of practical insights and peer perspectives, centered on one key question:
How do we turn global AI innovation into everyday clinical reality for clinicians and patients in Canada?

properties.title: The Founder’s Canvas: A Creative Strategy Workshop for Technical Founders Stuck on a Startup Problem
properties.description:
About the Event
Most founders don’t fail because of a technical problem. They fail because they get stuck in a logic loop, trying to solve new challenges with the same analytical thinking that created them.
When that happens, the real constraint isn’t technology or resources. It’s perspective.
If you’re a technical founder who tends to double down on analysis when a problem gets hard, this workshop will help you unlock new solution paths without abandoning structured thinking.
During the workshop, we’ll run a live startup strategy experiment.
Using real challenges from the startup world, we’ll map the problems and push them through structured creative frameworks to uncover solution paths most founders would never consider.
By the end of the session, you’ll walk away with a new way of reframing strategic problems and a set of creative thinking tools you can use whenever your startup hits a wall.

Inside the Founder’s Strategy Lab, we will:
• Break down hidden assumptions behind the challenge
• Reframe the problem using the Founder’s Canvas
• Generate unconventional solution paths
• Pressure-test ideas using systems thinking
This workshop is intentionally small and interactive, so spots are limited.
Meet Your Facilitator
Kerry McKenzie has helped over 100 founders break through strategic deadlocks. Working with accelerators and startup programs in Toronto and across the GTA to turn analytical paralysis into decisive action.
Light fare will be provided for workshop participants. Please provide any dietary restrictions upon registration.

properties.title: What Kills Startups (Every Time) - Finale!
properties.description:
Success is overhyped. Failure is patterned—and preventable.
Everyone teaches how to win. Almost no one studies how startups actually die.
And they don’t die randomly.
Most failures follow the same patterns—long before the market, product, or team gets blamed. This session breaks down what actually kills startups—and how to avoid it.
Hear from Albert Lin, Managing Partner, and a US venture capital GP, sharing lessons from the trenches on what separates companies that survive from those that don’t.
🎯 What you’ll walk away with
The most common (and preventable) startup failure patterns
How capital structure quietly determines outcomes
Where governance breaks—and how to fix it early
What experienced investors look for beyond upside
If you’re building or investing in a startup, this is the part no one teaches—and the part that matters most when things go wrong.
If you missed our Monday & Tuesday sessions, this is the Finale!

properties.title: THE PITCH ROOM: Live. Interactive. Built for Founders
properties.description:
You don't need a better idea. You need a stronger delivery.
Many founders lose the room in the first 60 seconds, not because of their deck, but because their nervous system takes over.
Under pressure, your voice and body language change, often without you realizing it. Those signals shape how you're perceived before your content has a chance to land. If you haven't trained for that, they quietly work against you.
The Pitch Room is a fully interactive session led by Dr. Sumeyra Ozturk, executive communication expert and founder of Speech & Mind, grounded in neuroscience and the psychology of persuasion.
Each round targets a specific element: how you project authority, signal confidence, and hold attention under pressure. You'll refine your delivery, identify your blind spots, and apply adjustments in real time, so your delivery becomes as strong as your ideas.
What you'll walk away with:
A clear framework for pitches that land
Techniques to communicate with authority and precision
Tools to stay steady and in control under pressure
What investors notice before your message even lands
Built for: Founders who are ready to understand exactly what's happening in the room; and how to shift it.

⚡ Due to the curated nature of this event, spots are limited. Register early to secure your place.
📩 For sponsorship inquiries, contact us at info@speechandmind.com
📸 Photos and videos may be taken for promotional use.

Speech & Mind helps leaders and ambitious professionals communicate with clarity, confidence, and presence in high-stakes moments. Founded by Dr. Sumeyra Ozturk, the firm combines behavioral research with practical strategy to elevate performance when it matters most.

properties.title: The Burnout Invoice: What It's Costing Your Tech Team And the Tools to Fix It
properties.description:
The tech industry moves fast. Deadlines don't wait, sprints don't slow down, and somewhere between the Slack notifications and the all-hands meetings, your team is running on fumes.
But here's what most companies aren't tracking: burnout isn't just a wellness problem. It's a performance problem, a retention problem, and a leadership problem and it's showing up on your bottom line whether you're measuring it or not.
The Burnout Invoice is a free, virtual webinar designed specifically for tech leaders, HR professionals, and people teams who want to understand what burnout is actually costing their organizations and walk away with real tools to do something about it.
This isn't a lecture about self-care. This is a practical, honest conversation about what's happening inside your teams, why high-performing people are burning out fastest, and what leaders can do right now to change the trajectory.
What You'll Learn:
In 60 focused minutes, you'll gain:
— A clear, clinical understanding of burnout: what it is, what it isn't, and why stress management advice alone isn't enough
— The hidden organizational costs of burnout including lost productivity, increased absenteeism, and high turnover — with context for what that means in the tech sector specifically
— How to spot burnout early in your team before it becomes a resignation letter or a breakdown
— Practical, evidence-based tools you can implement as a leader or HR professional without overhauling your entire culture overnight
— A downloadable resource to take back to your team, your manager, or your next planning cycle

Who This Is For:
This webinar is built for people who carry responsibility for others at work:
— Tech managers and team leads who want to support their people without burning out themselves
— HR professionals and people operations teams navigating mental health in high-pressure environments
— Founders and executives who are starting to notice the cracks in their team's performance and culture
— Anyone in the tech industry who suspects burnout is closer than they'd like to admit

Why This Conversation Matters Now:
Burnout in the tech sector is not new  but it is getting worse. The rise of remote work, always-on communication culture, layoffs, and relentless pressure to deliver have created a perfect storm for psychological exhaustion. And yet most organizations are still responding with ping pong tables and pizza Fridays.
The research is clear: burnout is a systemic issue, not a personal failing. It requires a systemic response. This webinar gives you the language, the data, and the tools to start building one.

About Your Host:
This webinar is hosted by Grey Couch Counselling, (www.greycouchcounselling.com) a practice dedicated to supporting the mental health and psychological wellbeing of individuals navigating high-pressure professional environments. Our work sits at the intersection of clinical expertise and real-world relevance  because good mental health support shouldn't feel disconnected from the life you're actually living.
We work with individuals, teams, and organizations to build sustainable approaches to performance, stress, and mental health at work.

Alongside Toronto Tech Week: 

Toronto Tech Week is a citywide celebration of the people building what’s next. From May 25–29, 2026, founders, investors, and builders come together for hundreds of community-led events across Toronto, connecting tens of thousands of people around Canadian tech. Torontotechweek.com.

What You'll Walk Away With:
-A framework for understanding and identifying burnout in yourself and your team 

- Practical tools you can use immediately, no lengthy rollout required 

-A downloadable resource guide to reference after the session 

- Clarity on when burnout requires professional support and how to have that conversation

- An open Q&A to get your specific questions answered live

Event Details:
Date: May 25 
Time: 12- 1 pm 
Format: Virtual — Zoom link provided upon registration  Investment: Your lunch hour 
Includes live Q&A

Reserve Your Spot:
Space is limited to ensure an intimate, high-quality experience. Register now to secure your place and the webinar link directly to your inbox.

Have questions about the webinar or interested in bringing this conversation to your organization? Contact us at hello@greycouchcounselling.com to discuss corporate sessions, team workshops, and speaking engagements.

properties.title: Build with AI: A hands-on lab for product, design, and innovation teams
properties.description:
Build with AI: A hands-on lab for product , design, and innovation teams
Toronto Tech Week · OCAD Waterfront Campus
AI is already changing how the best product and design teams work. Discovery is faster. Research synthesis that took days now takes minutes. Competitive intelligence runs in the background. Prototypes that used to take weeks ship in an afternoon. The practitioners who've figured this out have a real edge — and the gap is widening.
But speed without intention is just noise. The teams pulling ahead aren't just moving faster — they're asking better questions. Who is this designed for? What does responsibility look like when AI is doing the heavy lifting? What's the experience for the person on the other end?
That's what this workshop is about.
What this is
A hands-on practitioner lab — not a lecture. Momentum brings the real workflows. OCAD U CO brings the principled lens. Together, we're running a 3-hour session at OCAD's Waterfront Campus during Toronto Tech Week, designed for product managers, designers, innovation leads, and anyone bringing AI into their team's work with intention.
You'll see where AI is actually being used in product and design teams, which tools matter, where they break down — and then you'll try the workflows yourself with your own tools and accounts.
We cover the workflows that are already changing the job:
→ Discovery & insight synthesis — turn interviews, support tickets, and research notes into usable patterns in minutes. Human judgment picks the question; AI collapses the time.
→ Competitive intelligence — build AI-powered signal monitoring and translate insights directly into product and design strategy. A serious look at what changes when your team has this running.
→ Prototyping & vibe coding — go from idea to working prototype in one session. A designer's eye on who experiences what you build — and what that means for the choices you make along the way.
We'll also address what teams do when their org restricts certain tools, how to adopt AI safely, and how to choose between tools depending on the task.
And if you're actively interviewing? "What's your AI toolkit?" is now a first-15-minutes question from hiring managers. This workshop gives you a real answer.
Why this one is different
Practitioners and principles, in the same room. Most AI workshops give you tools without a framework, or frameworks without anything you can use on Monday. This one gives you both. Momentum's practitioner-tested workflows meet OCAD U CO's human-centered design lens — so you leave with a toolkit that's not just fast, but intentional.
Built for the people who ship. PMs, designers, innovation leads — this is for the people deciding what gets built and how. We've sat through the painful, time-consuming parts of the job, and we've rebuilt those workflows with AI.
Dedicated experts in the room. Momentum workshops have facilitators helping you during the session. You leave with something working.
In person for a reason. AI workflows click faster when you can ask questions live, experiment together, and troubleshoot in real time. And the space matters — OCAD U CO Waterfront Campus is a creative studio environment purpose-built for this kind of thinking.
First in a series. Future sessions go deeper into AI-powered prototyping, vibe coding, competitive intel agents, and AI-enabled product strategy.
All attendees get access to the Momentum Product Community — a space for product and design folks actively building with AI and sharing what works.

properties.title: The Story Behind the Sales: Scaling E-commerce Brand Growth
properties.description:
Every successful ecommerce brand begins with a strong story. The brands that break through scale are the ones that translate that story into infrastructure, systems, and revenue momentum.
This session explores what actually changes inside a company when growth becomes complex and technology starts to shape competitive advantage.
Hosted by JC+CO Growth Accelerants, a Shopify Plus studio working with scaling consumer brands across platform architecture, performance strategy, and AI informed growth systems, this conversation brings together operators who have navigated real inflection points on the path from traction to scale.
Rather than theory, we will focus on what founders and leadership teams experience behind the scenes as their brands move into the next stage of maturity.
From Story to Revenue Engine
How strong brand narratives evolve into structured acquisition, retention, and conversion systems that support long term growth.
Technology as a Growth Multiplier
What changes when Shopify infrastructure, integrations, and data begin driving commercial decisions instead of reacting to them.
The Operator’s Shift
How leadership priorities evolve when brands move beyond early traction and begin building durable market position.
Scaling Without Losing Identity
How high growth brands protect what makes them distinctive while expanding across channels, markets, and customer segments.

properties.title: Developers At Tech Week TO
properties.description:
Attention Devs & CTOs
Stop watching and start building. Join our BUILD with AI hands-on session led by an official Google Developer Expert Tanmay Bakshi.

Talk Title: From First Principles to Agentic AI: How Language Models Learn and Generalize
Join us for a hands-on workshop exploring the machine learning fundamentals behind modern agentic AI applications. We’ll start from first principles: how models learn from data, how training changes behavior, and why generalization matters when building systems that need to reason, adapt, and act reliably. Through a practical language-model experiment using JAX and Google Cloud TPUs, attendees will see how different model sizes respond to the same learning task and how well they transfer that learning to examples they have never seen before. By the end, participants will have a grounded understanding of the core ML ideas that underpin today’s AI-powered and agentic applications
To participate, you must bring your own laptop and power cable. The activities are intended for laptops and cannot be completed on a tablet or phone.

As AI reshapes how we access and organize knowledge, the most valuable ability may be the skill to direct your own learning. Join Diego Vera at OCAD University on May 27 for a practical session about designing a sustainable, effective personal learning system: choosing the right projects, evaluating and curating resources, setting measurable milestones, and using AI tools to accelerate understanding without losing depth.
Expect hands-on frameworks, quick demos of helpful tools and workflows, and time to build a short, actionable learning plan you can start immediately. Bring a learning goal or problem you’re working on and leave with concrete next steps, a reading/practice roadmap, and resources to keep advancing independently.

Note: Food and drinks will be available (included in your ticket)

Address: 115 McCaul St. – 3rd level

This event is supported by OCAD University’s Office of Research & Innovation. Attendees will also have the opportunity to learn more about OCAD’s “Research is Everything” initiative during the event.

properties.title: The New Foundations for High Performing Teams.
properties.description:
OVERVIEW
The playbook for high-performing teams has changed. While change is a constant for team leads across departments and industries - the pace of change has made it harder than ever before to establish strong processes, practices and systems of cooperation.
Most of us are adapting in real time. And most of us are doing it without a map.
So what does high performance actually look like right now? What still works, and what needs to be rethought? What will a great team look like six months from now?
That's what this evening is about.
We've designed an intentional evening, balanced between inspirational talks and intimate, industry specific breakout rooms to dive into these topics and establish some creative consensus. 

For Our Keynote - we're pleased to welcome:
Emile Studham, founder of Cooperation Works, author of the forthcoming book The Cooperation Code. Emile has lead cultural development for The Toronto Blue Jays, BMO, Hydro One and dozens of startups. Following Emile, we'll be inviting a curated panel of Team Leads and AI practitioners picks up the threads Emile will setup for us all. 

For Our Panel (more panelists to be announced soon)

We're going to have a down to earth conversation to capture a pulse about the most challenging collaboration issues & opportunities that various leaders within different industries are seeing & feeling at the moment.

Moderator: 
Jason Goodman | Product & AI Consultant for Brave Teams.

Panelists:
Markus Grupp | Senior Director of Product Design & UX Research at Loblaws Digital 

Holly Heglin | Partner, AI & Innovation at Jarvis Consulting Group

The Breakout Rooms
You'll be able to join one of 10 industry-specific breakout rooms — each consisting of five people and a dedicated facilitator.
After the event, you'll receive a customized report with the key takeaways and actionable recommendations from your session.
Food and drinks are on us throughout.

SCHEDULE
3:00 PM  — Doors open and refreshments & food served.
3:30 PM  — Keynote — Emile Studham, Cooperation Works 
3:45 PM  — Panel discussion 
4:15 PM   — Breakout sessions (you pick your room) 
4:55 PM  — Final thoughts + synthesis 
5:00 PM  — Networking & refreshments cont.

WHO SHOULD ATTEND
This one's for you if you're a Team Lead, people leader, or Founder who's actively thinking about how to build and sustain a high-performing team right now — not in theory, but in practice.
Team Leads across product, engineering, operations, marketing, and HR
Founders rethinking how their teams work in an AI-accelerated environment
People leaders and department heads who know culture is the work, not a side project
Attendance will be capped at around 60 people.

ABOUT VEN GARDEN
Ven Garden is a Fractional Venture Studio built on a new model of cooperation. Led by Jason Goodman, Fractional Product & AI Lead at Goodman & Co. (www.goodmanandcompany.io) Ven Garden brings together fractional talent who invest their expertise in exchange for revenue share — a different kind of alignment between builders and the ventures they grow together.
Ven Garden's first venture is Aretek (www.aretek.ca) — the innovation layer for the construction industry.
vengarden.com

ABOUT VISION TALENT
Vision Talent is a full-service tech talent partner for companies at every stage — from building junior pipelines through hire-train-deploy to finding the right senior leaders when it counts. Their whole approach is built around one thing: getting the right people in place so you can actually focus on the work.
visiontalent.io

properties.description:
The Vision
We’re bringing the OpenClaw and GOAT Network stack to Toronto for a one-day vibe coding sprint with the support of the MindFuel grant program, GOAT Network, ClawUP and Metis Foundation. Your mission: Build an AI agent that doesn’t just talk but owns its identity, manages its own wallet and transact with other agents. Leverage Bitcoin’s 99.9% uptime and BitVM2’s mathematical security (viat GOAT mainnet) to build agents that handle high-value transactions with zero human oversight.
🏆 The Stakes
We aren't giving out stickers and socks. We are giving away Mac Mini to the top-performing team or individual to power your future AI development. We also are looking for the bright minds to join our team and our partner projects as an interns to continue developing what they started at the hackathon. We also will be mentoring and helping those who would like to develop their hackathon projects further as their own startups.
If you are a company and would like to partner with the event by hiring the students as an interns or by providing a prize or mentorship please send your inquiries to stayintouch@cryptochicks.ca.
🚀 The Challenge
Deploy a "vibe-coded" autonomous agent using:
OpenClaw: The brain and framework.
ERC-8004: On-chain identity (Give your agent a soul).
x402: Agent-native payments (Let your agent get paid).
GOAT Network: The infrastructure for the agent economy.

📅 Event Details
When: May 26 2026 during the Toronto Tech Week
Where: Toronto Metropolitan University (Sheldon & Tracy Levy Student Learning Centre).

Go from zero to a live, AI-powered transaction on GOAT mainnet fully vibe-coded.

properties.title: Czech Tech Day
properties.description:
Are you curious about European tech, research, and innovation?
Join us for an afternoon exploring research and innovation cooperation between Canada and the Czech Republic — and discover what's possible when you connect with one of Central Europe's fastest-growing innovation ecosystems.
Organized in collaboration by the Czech Consulate General in Toronto, CzechInvest Agency, and the South Moravian Innovation Centre, this event offers:
Tangible opportunities for research cooperation under Horizon Europe and other funding instruments
Practical insights into the Czech innovation ecosystem
An overview of the thriving Czech Life Sciences and Biotech sector
WHO WILL YOU MEET?
Network over snacks and Czech beer with leading innovation and research institutions, including:
South Moravian Innovation Centre
The City of Brno
South Moravian Region
Prague.bio
Brno University of Technology
Masaryk University
University of Chemistry and Technology
University Hospital Brno
HealthMonk
WHO IS THIS FOR?
Professionals interested in Central Europe's innovation landscape, research collaboration opportunities, and growth in Life Sciences and Engineering.
Biotech: The biotech sector in the Czech Republic builds on a century-old industrial tradition, with pharmaceuticals being manufactured in Prague on an industrial scale for over 100 years, and combines that with a cutting-edge scientific inquiry supported by a robust network of over 400 companies and research institutes, a specialized talent pipeline of 12,000 students in life sciences, and a growing venture capital landscape.
What makes the Czech Republic particularly attractive is its concentration of talent and output, with more than 50% of top-quality publications produced in collaboration with partners in the United States, Germany, and the United Kingdom. The country generates a high density of high-impact scientific research relative to its size.
Advanced manufacturing: The Czech Republic is a European leader in advanced manufacturing, combining a long-standing engineering tradition with cutting-edge innovation. Manufacturing contributes to more than 25% of the country’s gross value added, one of the highest shares in Europe, and high-tech products already make up over 20% of Czech exports. The Czech Republic also ranks 8th globally in Harvard’s Economic Complexity Index, underlining the sophistication and diversity of its industrial base. That is supported with a rapid expansion of AI adoption in the Czech Republic, with Czech startups outpacing their European counterparts with AI innovation.
South Moravian Region: The South Moravian Region has emerged as one of the leading European innovation hubs. A global leader in electron microscopy, with one-third of the world's electron microscopes coming from Brno, Brno region excels also in the semiconductor, space, cybersecurity, and game development sectors. Leading the Czech Republic in knowledge intensity and high-tech employment, Brno region matches Europe's top regions. The share of expenditures on science and research relative to regional GDP, at 3.1%, is not only the highest in the Czech Republic over the long term but also reaches the level of knowledge intensity of leading European regions, especially in Germany, Austria, Belgium, or Sweden.

properties.title: Sunrise Paddleboard
properties.description:
Part of the Kolbe 15 Rain or Shine series.
The calm before the tech storm. Start your morning on the water as the sun rises, take a moment to be present, set the mood for the day.
We meet at Cherry Beach at 6am, get on the water, and paddle out as the sun comes up. No agenda, no pitch, no performance. Just you, the water, and the people around you.
Once we're out, we stop. For 20 minutes we close our eyes, put the week away, and just be present with the sound of the water, the air, the moment. No experience required. No instruction. Just space to breathe.
Then we have tea and coffee on the water, a small snack, and talk. The kind of conversation that happens when you're floating on a lake at sunrise with people who get it.
We paddle back by 8am. You're at your desk by 9am. But you'll feel different for the rest of the day.
Resilience Level: 1/10
Schedule

properties.title: From signal to system: what it takes to reach Series A
properties.description:
Introduction
Only 15% of seed-stage companies make it to Series A. By the time the numbers explain why, the break is already months old. Most companies don’t fail all at once. They drift, while everything still looks like it’s working.
The Gap
There’s a window between when the system breaks and when anyone realizes it. In that window, the metrics look fine, the team feels productive, and the story still holds. Until it doesn’t.
Quant vs. Qual Lenses
Jordan Hill, CEO, Growth Partners shows you what to look for and how to interpret the patterns before they before they become metrics. Marcelo Ceron, Founder, MISSING LINK shows you why those patterns exist and what made them inevitable.
One company. Two lenses.
Quant shows where to look.
Qual shows why it happens and how to fix.
Walking the Arc
We take you on a startup's journey from Seed to Series A. Key moments. Subtle shifts. What to notice and what to track. Naming the point where things go wrong. Seen through both lenses, in real time.
Where to Act
The companies that survive aren’t the ones that move the fastest. They’re the ones that see earlier, and know what to fix before it compounds. You won’t just see the gap. You’ll understand where to act before it closes.
Q&A
Join us for a lively discussion after the talk.
About Growth Partners
At Growth Partners, our team of startup finance experts has supported 75+ ambitious companies through growth. We specialize in startups with $1M–$10M in annual revenue.
Through strategic fractional CFO support, the right finance tech stack, and timely, relevant reporting, we help founders understand and trust their numbers.
Our approach combines proven playbooks, strong processes, and the right tools to keep your finances running smoothly, saving you time to focus on what matters most.
We empower your growth, and always lead with a human first approach.
About MISSING LINK
MISSING LINK helps businesses run like brands and turns brands into powerhouses.
We work at the intersection of brand, growth, and operations, across every stage, from founder-led teams to $6B enterprises. One pattern holds: companies don’t underperform because of bad decisions, they underperform because of inconsistent ones.
MISSING LINK turns strategy into a decision system: aligning what you believe with how you decide so it compounds in what you do.
Strategy sets direction. Results reflect outcomes. Decision systems determine everything in between.

properties.title: The Agentic Boardroom: Canada's AI Leadership Conversation
properties.description:
Most AI events are about tools.
This one is about leadership.
The question is no longer whether your team uses AI. They already do. The leadership question is whether your company has a clear operating model for AI: what it can access, where it can take action, who approves the work, and who owns the outcomes.
The Agentic Boardroom brings a curated group of CEOs, founders, operators, investors, and AI leaders together during Toronto Tech Week for one focused evening at Harbour Sixty.
We will open with a keynote from Aaqib Azeem, Global AI and ML Partner Lead at Google, on where enterprise AI is heading. From there, the conversation moves into practical leadership questions: adoption, governance, workflow ownership, risk, and the operating muscle required to turn AI from scattered experimentation into real company leverage.
Then the room goes to work.
Guests will be invited into the conversation through live Q&A, practitioner discussion, and facilitated boardroom roundtables.
This is a private working room for leaders being asked to shape:
- Where AI can safely take action inside a real company
- What should still require human judgment
- Who owns AI when it moves from experiment to operating system
- What a Chief Agent Officer, or equivalent AI owner, needs to control
- What Canadian AI leadership should look like as agentic systems enter the business mainstream
The evening is designed to give guests a clearer map of the agentic shift, a sharper view of where their organization sits, and a seat inside a founding conversation that should continue beyond the room.
