properties.title: Learn to Work with an AI Agent — Claude Cowork Workshop
properties.description:
A full-day, hands-on workshop for knowledge workers ready to go from chatting with AI to working alongside it.
Description
For 40 years, using a computer meant learning the computer's language: menus, buttons, keyboard shortcuts. Claude Cowork inverts that. The computer learns your language. It reads your files, connects to your tools, and takes actions on your behalf.
This workshop teaches you how to work with this new kind of computer.
One full day. Hands-on. You'll walk out with a working AI workflow you can run at your desk Monday morning.
Who this is for
You're a knowledge worker, with a technical or builder mindset. You work in operations, finance, marketing, sales, product, HR, or a similar role. Maybe you've used Claude before, maybe you haven't — what matters is that you're comfortable with technology and ready to learn something new.
You should be the kind of person who can install an app, manage files on your computer, and follow instructions without hand-holding. We'll teach you everything else.
You should apply if:
You want to learn how to work alongside an AI agent, not just chat with one
You have real workflows you'd like to automate or improve
You're evaluating Cowork for yourself or your team and want hands-on experience
You have (or are willing to get) a Claude Pro, Teams, Max, or Enterprise subscription
What you'll learn
Fundamentals of working with Claude: How Claude actually works — not theory for its own sake, but the practical knowledge that makes you a better user. You'll understand tokens, context, and system prompts so you can manage cost. You'll learn prompt engineering so you can get better results. And you'll learn the cost curve: when to pay for expensive intelligence and when to combine your own judgment with effective techniques and less expensive models. Instructor-led demos throughout.
Working from Chat to Agents: What changes when Claude goes from answering questions to doing work. The Cowork mental model: an AI agent running on your machine with access to your files, your calendar, your email. Live demo and hands on your first Cowork task.
Building blocks to automation: Connectors, skills, plugins, and scheduled tasks — the building blocks of Cowork. Two guided hands-on sessions where you connect your real tools, build a custom workflow, and set it up to run automatically. By the end of the afternoon, you'll have something running on your machine that wasn't there this morning.
What you'll walk away with
The knowledge to manage AI costs and get better results — understanding tokens, context, model selection, and prompt engineering across every Claude surface
Cowork connected to your real tools, a custom workflow you built and tested, and an automated task scheduled to run Monday morning
A foundation for designing how you work with your AI agent collaborator — and the ability to evaluate what's possible for your team
Requirements before the workshop
Bring: Your laptop (macOS or Windows), fully charged, with Claude Desktop installed. An active Claude subscription (Pro, Teams, Max, or Enterprise) with Cowork access. If you don't have one yet, sign up for Claude Pro before the event. No coding experience required. New to Claude? That's fine — we start from the foundations.
We provide:
Full-day instructor-led workshop
Catered lunch and coffee and snacks
Printed quick-reference materials
Post-workshop resources for continued learning.
Pricing
This is for Antfluent's Founding Cohort and we are offering introductory pricing for Toronto Tech Week.
Standard: $449 CAD
Testimonial Rate: $349 CAD — $100 off for helping us tell the story — a short on-camera testimonial and permission to share it as part of Antfluent's training portfolio.
One automated workflow that saves you 30 minutes a day pays for this ticket in under two weeks. That's less than $56 per hour of instruction from the person who launched Claude for enterprise at AWS.
About
Gabriel Velazquez Lopez — Founder & Principal, Antfluent
Gabriel has spent 20 years launching technology that matters — Canada's first 4G network at TELUS, a revamped channel partnership at Apple, the first core banking migration for a credit union in North America on AWS. Then Claude on AWS.
As Anthropic's first Applied AI & Partnership lead at AWS, Gabriel built the global GTM strategy behind Claude's growth into enterprise. He launched every Claude product that reached enterprise customers: Claude for Enterprise, Education, Financial Services, Life Sciences, and Claude Code — and designed AWS' Claude Code production guidance adopted by over 1,200 companies, now the third most deployed solution in the AWS Solutions Library.
Gabriel is an Anthropic Claude Certified Architect and AWS Certified Solutions Architect Professional.
LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/gvlz/
Antfluent
Antfluent is Canada's first Anthropic-specialized consulting, training, and advisory boutique.

Get ready for a 12-hour Hackathon from 9AM to 9PM designed for builders, dreamers, and doers!
This year’s theme: The Rise of Personal Assistants: AI to Organize Your Life.
For decades, software has made people do the work. Open the app. Learn the workflow. Manage the tools.
Now AI is starting to flip that model.
A new generation of personal assistants can understand context, manage tasks, and help people handle everyday life - from calendar chaos and life admin to groceries, travel planning, and family logistics.
At this hackathon, we want to see what builders create when AI can do more than answer prompts. We want products that help people actually get through the day.

Join us during Toronto Tech Week on Sunday, May 24 and spend 12 hours building alongside founders, operators, developers, and designers exploring one big question:
What becomes possible when AI helps organize your life?

Come solo or bring a team. You’ll get time to build, support from mentors, and the chance to demo your idea and win prizes from TechTO and our sponsors.
This is not just about writing code. It’s about building something people would actually use every day.


Agenda
9:00am: Doors open, check in and grab coffee
9:45am: Welcome speech and walkthrough of agenda of the day
10:00am - 5:00pm: Hackathon time! Lunch will be provided at 1:00pm
5:00pm - 5:30pm: Voting and Pizza time!
5:30pm - 6:30pm: The top 3 teams will be selected for the judge panel. Play video-demos and Q&A
6:30pm - 7:00pm: Judges discussion
7:00pm - 7:30pm: Awards & Celebration
7:30pm - 9pm: Social & End of the Event

Thank You To Our Sponsors


TechTO is Canada’s largest community of tech builders - connecting 60,000+ founders, operators, and investors through year-round meetups, curated dinners, peer tables, and media storytelling. Interested in learning more? techto.org. For sponsorship inquiries, reach out to partnerships@techto.org.

properties.title: Why Founders Waste Months on the Wrong Decisions and How to Avoid It
properties.description:
Founders do not just lose months by building too slowly.
They lose months when they commit too early to the wrong customer, product direction, sales channel, pricing model, or scaling decision.
This working session is for early-stage founders and startup operators who are making decisions around customers, positioning, pricing, sales channels, product direction, or growth.
The goal is to help founders make clearer decisions before spending too much time, money, and energy in the wrong direction.
It is especially relevant if you are asking:
Who is the right customer?
Is this interest strong enough to become revenue?
Which sales channel should be tested first?
What should be validated before building or scaling further?
What the session will break down
Real startup examples will be used to examine patterns that often look promising at first, but create problems later:
A product feels useful, but customers still do not pay
A product works technically, but breaks down in real-world use
An audience grows, but does not turn into business value
A team keeps building, even though the biggest risk was never tested
What you will leave with
You will leave with a practical decision framework to help you identify key assumptions, separate demand, product, business model, and growth risks, and decide which assumptions should be tested first.
The session will also cover how to design simple, low-cost experiments before building, selling, or scaling too far.
Who this is for
This is not a motivational startup talk.
It is a hands-on working session for founders, pre-seed and seed-stage teams, and startup operators who want to make better decisions before wasting months in the wrong direction.
Hosted by
Growth Partner AI helps founders make clearer growth, go-to-market, and business model decisions before wasting months in the wrong direction.
Selected by Scale Up Canada as one of Waterloo50 2026, Growth Partner AI provides AI-powered advice and decision-making support for founders navigating messy market signals, risky assumptions, and uncertainty.
Toronto Tech Week

properties.title: Building the Future of Community Health: A Roundtable on Modernization and Innovation
properties.description:
Ontario is investing $2.1B to connect 2 million people to primary care by 2029, with Community Health Centres (CHCs) positioned as a key delivery mechanism. This creates both unprecedented opportunity and pressure for organizations to scale operations, modernize systems, and innovate.
This session brings together CHC leaders, clinicians, and system stakeholders for a candid, practical conversation on the realities of delivering community-based care in this moment of transformation. Moving beyond high-level discussion, we’ll explore the structural challenges facing Ontario’s community health sector and identify actionable paths forward.
Featuring:
Callum Tyrrell, VP, Innovation, Improvement & Engagement, Scarborough Centre for Healthy Communities
Michelle Joseph, CEO, Unison Health and Community Services
Dr. Susan Joyce, Primary Care Lead, North Toronto Ontario Health Team
Drawing on insights from leaders across the province, the discussion will examine how organizations can modernize within constrained budgets, navigate complex funding environments, and build sustainable innovation capacity, without disrupting frontline care.

properties.description:
Overview
Join your fellow innovators, founders, and curious minds for our interactive viewing party at the Schwartz Reisman Innovation Campus! Come for the live viewing of the Desjardins Speaker Series: How to Build a $100B Company, Brick by Brick, featuring Databricks Co-founder Reynold Xin; stay for the great food, networking, and interactive activities.
The celebration will continue after with the University of Toronto Lawn Party. Meet Toronto’s community of tech innovators, discover startups at the edge of innovation, and enjoy snacks offered by U of T’s startup community.

About the Keynote
Join Reynold Xin, Co-founder of Databricks, a San Francsico-based data and AI infrastructure startup valued at over $130B and one of the fastest growing tech startups in history. Reynold is a proud U of T alumnus (BASc in Engineering Science) who will share his story of a founding team that turned a research project into a business with a global footprint, over 8,000 employees and thousands of enterprise customers.
This signature Desjardins Speakers Series celebrating Toronto Tech Week will discuss:
Origin story of the company and advice for young founders
Trends, opportunities, and threats in the field of big data and artificial intelligence
What Canadian business leaders, innovators, investors, and government need to do to compete
How to effectively commercialize research and what has changed since 2013 when Databricks was founded
Lessons learned from a life spent in Silicon Valley

Date: Tuesday, May 26

Location: Schwartz Reisman Innovation Campus

Spots are limited. Register now.

properties.title: The AI Efficiency Blueprint: Practical Execution Strategies for CRM, ERP Technology Ecosystem Transformation
properties.description:
This is not your average panel discussion. Specifically designed for Founders, CFOs and Revenue Operations professionals, this evening combines high-level networking with an informal, interactive panel focused on real-world results.
We’re bringing together three distinct perspectives on CRM and ERP technology ecosystem design, with panelists from Campfire.AI, Trajectory Group and Initus Technologies, to discuss how AI is being embedded into modern businesses, from financial system implementation and order-to-cash automation, to AI-native ERP systems.
You drive the conversation. The panel will focus on practical execution strategies based on the topics you select once you are approved to attend.
The Agenda
5:00 – 5:30 PM: Arrival, Cocktails & Networking
5:30 – 6:30 PM: Interactive Panel Discussion & QA
6:30 PM Onwards: Curated Cocktails & Heavy Hors D'oeuvres
Tell Us What You Want to Discuss
These are the topics you will be able to select from upon registration confirmation:
1. The AI-Native Revolution
ERP vs. AI-ERP: Understanding why a system built on AI outperforms legacy systems with AI add-ons.
The Autonomous Finance Era: How AI agents are actively transforming accounting workflows.
Predictive vs. Reactive Finance: Shifting from monthly reporting to real-time forecasting using ERP data.
2. The Clean Pipes Strategy
The Manual Data Entry Leak: Stopping the overspend on manual data entry between CRM, ERP, and third-party tools.
Intelligent Document Processing (IDP): Turning your inbox into an automated data feed for your ERP.
Solving Data Silos: Creating unified information flows so AI has accurate data to learn from.
3. Orchestrating the Technology Transformation
The AI Implementation Roadmap: How to pick projects that drive margin instead of just chasing shiny objects.
NetSuite & Salesforce Optimization: Modernizing your existing stack to integrate with emerging AI tools.
People + AI: Managing the cultural shift as teams move from doing the work to reviewing the AI’s work.


Meet The Panelists


Jack Arrix
Head of Partnerships
Campfire.ai
https://www.linkedin.com/in/jackarrix/
Jack brings 10 years of ERP experience (NetSuite, Sage) to lead strategic partnerships for the AI-native ERP, Campfire.AI.


Andrew Andreoli
Managing Partner
Trajectory Group
https://www.linkedin.com/in/andrewandreoli/
With 25 years in CRM, ERP architecture and cloud technology, Andrew leads Trajectory in delivering robust, value-driven solutions for complex challenges.


Heather Gordon
Director of Sales & Marketing
Initus Technologies
https://www.linkedin.com/in/heather-gordon-27701a2/
Heather has 12 years of systems implementation experience, helping businesses transition from legacy tools to modern, automated AI-enhanced workflows.

About the Hosts
Campfire.AI: The AI-native ERP that gives accounting teams superpowers by automating transaction categorization, reconciliations, and revenue recognition.
Trajectory Group: A digital transformation consultancy helping scaling companies and PE firms drive value through CRM/ERP optimization and AI accelerators.
Initus Technologies: A premier consultancy specializing in turnkey integration and AI-enhanced workflow automation solutions that unify fragmented operations.

properties.title: Is Your Startup One Announcement Away From Irrelevant?
properties.description:
OpenAI ships. Anthropic ships. Google ships. And sometimes — they ship your product.
This event is for anyone building with AI who's asked themselves: is what I'm building defensible, or am I one changelog away from being obsolete?
We'll give you a framework to answer it honestly — and together.
What we'll cover:
The difference between a feature and a company — and how to tell which one you're building
How to identify your actual moat: is it your data, your workflow, or your trust?
What "platform risk" really means and how to stress-test your idea against it
How to build for a world where the floor keeps moving
What to expect:
A practical framework you can apply to your own product or idea
Open discussion with founders, builders, and operators who are wrestling with the same questions
Networking with the community that's actually building
Admission: $10 Contribution
As a non-profit, your ticket purchase directly supports our mission to provide equitable access to AI education and resources for the entire community.

Agentic Payments Breakfast 
Qatom Launch
Your agent calls a premium API. The API returns a 402. Your agent doesn't have a wallet, can't sign a transaction, can't negotiate payment terms. So it fails, logs an error, and you handle it manually. That defeats the point of building an autonomous agent.
This is the problem Qatom was built to solve.
Qatom is the hosted MCP server that turns any MCP-compatible agent into a fully economic participant. One install. Your agent gets a wallet, discovers paywalled APIs in a live marketplace, and pays for what it calls in the same request. Zero fees. Settled in USD, in under a second. No blockchain, no card rail, no 30-cent floor on a 3-cent transaction.
TODAQ Micro has been running this infrastructure in production since 2023. This breakfast is the official public launch.
The gathering is small and curated. Attendees include MCP builders, agent founders, and investors active in the Agentic stack. We're bringing together the people who are shipping Agentic systems and thinking seriously about what autonomous payments actually requires at the infrastructure level.
Expect a live demo with agents calling, paying, and earning in real time. A conversation with Hassan Khan, CEO of TODAQ Micro, on what three years of production agent payment infrastructure actually looks like. And an honest discussion on the one layer of the Agentic stack where the existing solutions impose real structural limits.
The official MCP Dev Summit comes to Toronto in October. This is May. If you want to be in the room when the payment layer ships, this is the morning.
80% of enterprise applications shipping today embed at least one AI agent. MCP has crossed 9,400 public servers. The payment layer is the gap. If you're building on MCP, shipping autonomous workflows, or thinking seriously about how agents pay for things, this is your room.
Registered attendees get hands-on access to TODAQ Console and Twin provisioning, with the team present to onboard you directly.
Space is intentionally limited.
Install Qatom: clawhub install Qatom · qatom.ai Open source: github.com/todaqmicro/openclaw

properties.title: Ship an AI Integration in 2 Hours: React + Apollo + MCP Apps
properties.description:
You've built a React app. It has data, logic, and real functionality. Now imagine letting an AI agent actually use it, displaying your UI right there in the chat window for users to interact with!
In this 2-hour hands-on workshop, Apollo GraphQL shows frontend developers how to take an existing web app and wire it up to an AI agent end-to-end. No prior GraphQL experience required! You'll leave with working code and a pattern you can use to embed any app into any MCP Apps-compatible host (like ChatGPT, Claude, and others).
What you'll do:
Explore an existing React + GraphQL application
Expose your app's operations as AI-callable tools
Use MCPJam's widget emulator to test against any LLM in the playground
What you'll learn:
How to think about your existing React app as an AI-ready product with GraphQL & MCP
How Apollo Skills helps you build with confidence
Who this is for:
This is a frontend-first workshop. If you know React and have built or consumed a REST API before, you're ready!
Prerequisites:
Attendees should arrive with:
A laptop with Node.js v18+ and npm
A code editor
(Optional, if you want to add your app to ChatGPT without using the MCPJam emulator) A ChatGPT Plus account with OpenAI Apps SDK access
The workshop will be led by Michelle Mabuyo, Developer Educator at Apollo.
We'll send you a reminder email prior to the workshop!
Food and drinks will be provided.

properties.title: Meraki Showcase @Toronto Tech Week
properties.description:
Toronto's builder community, together.
Toronto is full of builders - artists, engineers, designers, founders, tinkerers...quietly working on things they care about.
Meraki exists to bring those people into the same room.
To celebrate an incredible year of building, we're hosting our annual Symposium and Demo Day during Toronto Tech Week - an evening where members of the community get to formally present the projects they've been pouring themselves into. It's our favourite night of the year.
Whether you're a builder, an investor, an operator, or just someone who loves being around people making things - you belong here :)

UPDATE: DEMOER INTEREST FORM NOW OPEN: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSfxsL5vne_s9OQoUfbDe3u5vtJmL1okvojQozZKW2EshuLprg/viewform

What’s happening:
A few members of the Meraki UofT community will be demoing and sharing their passion projects. You'll see small glimpses into what people are building, learning, and experimenting with right now.
You can expect:
Short demos from Meraki community members
Casual conversations around art, tech, and creative work
Food (yes, there will be food)
Space to meet people and trade ideas
Come curious! Come ready to talk about what you’re working on or what you want to work on.

A Note on the Meraki mindset
Meraki is built on the idea that creative work is better when it’s shared.
When you come, you’re not just an attendee, you’re a part of the space. We ask everyone to:
Be welcoming to newcomers
Ask thoughtful questions
Share generously, even if your work feels “unfinished”
Help make the room feel warm, curious, and alive
If you see someone standing alone...go say hi & make a new friend.

What is Meraki UofT?
Meraki UofT is a student-led builder community at the University of Toronto.

(we're friends of Socratica!!)
We bring together people across disciplines; engineering, art, design, business, research, and beyond! People who care about making things and learning in public.
If you like building, experimenting, or exploring new ideas, Meraki is for you.

Filming & media notice
This event will be photographed and filmed for Meraki UofT’s (and sponsors) promotional and archival use (social media, website, and future event materials).
By attending, you consent to being recorded. If you have any concerns or would prefer not to be filmed, please let a Meraki organizer know... we’re happy to accommodate.

Food will be provided.
RSVP required.
We’re excited to share what we've been working on.
See you soon 🤍

properties.title: Unscripted: How Bias Shapes Investor Decisions — and How We Change It (Panel discussion)
properties.description:
Women and Gender-Diverse founders are asked different questions — and it costs them millions in lost investment.
Join the Canadian Women's Chamber of Commerce for an unfiltered and deeply needed conversation during Toronto Tech Week as we bring together investors, founders, and ecosystem leaders to examine one of the most persistent barriers in entrepreneurship: bias in investment decision‑making.
Despite the innovation, resilience, and economic impact of women and gender‑diverse founders, funding gaps remain staggering. This panel goes beyond the statistics to explore the real dynamics at play — the assumptions, narratives, and unconscious biases that shape who gets funded, who gets overlooked, and what we can collectively do to change the system.
Unscripted means exactly that: an honest, unapologetic dialogue about the structural and cultural forces that influence investor behaviour. Our panelists will share their lived experiences navigating the investment landscape, from pitching to gatekeepers to challenging outdated norms about what a “successful entrepreneur” looks like. Investors will also speak candidly about the pressures, blind spots, and decision‑making patterns that shape their choices — and how the industry can evolve toward more equitable, evidence‑based practices.
Together, we’ll explore:
How bias shows up in pitch rooms, due diligence, and portfolio strategy
The myths and stereotypes that continue to shape investor perceptions
What founders can do — and shouldn’t have to do — to navigate biased systems
How investors can adopt more inclusive frameworks and accountability measures
What collective action across the ecosystem could look like
Designed for founders, investors, ecosystem partners, and anyone committed to building a more equitable and inclusive entrepreneurial economy, the conversation will be bold, practical, and grounded in real‑world experience, with space for meaningful connection, reflection, and action.
This event is part of Canadian Women's Chamber of Commerce’s Flip the Script Investment Readiness Program a self‑paced online learning experience that helps women‑identified and non‑binary entrepreneurs master a communication style proven to secure up to 60% more funding from investors through a licensed CanWCC AI‑powered training simulation.
For more visit : www.canwcc.ca /ttps://fts.canwcc.ca/
Moderator: Nancy Wilson , CPA, CGA, CEO & FOUNDER at CANWCC

Nancy is a Chartered Professional Accountant with over a decade of accounting and finance experience in the private sector working with start-ups, small businesses, and multinational corporations.  Nancy launched the Canadian Women’s Chamber of Commerce in January 2018. She has been working ever since to build a powerful national network and drive the policy changes needed to create lasting gender and economic equity in Canada. Nancy is also a monthly columnist withrabble.ca, where she writes about feminism, economic justice, and public policy
MEET OUR  PANELISTS
Diane Wolfenden, General Partner with Phoenix Fire https://www.archangelnetwork.ca/

Diane Wolfenden is an active angel investor. Since 2009, she’s been a charter member of the Golden Triangle Angel Network (GTAN) and has twice been on its board. In 2016, she received GTAN’s David Borges Community Builder of the Year Award. Formerly an investor advisor with RBC Dominion Securities, she also gives back as a community volunteer. She sits on the board of Stratford Summer Music, and chairs the Community Aid Fund for the Kitchener Conestoga Rotary Club. She’s a former chair of Equation Angels, the board of Governors of the Grand River Hospital Foundation and the Greater Kitchener-Waterloo Chamber of Commerce.

Laura Venier, Founder & CEO of Arche Biotechnologies www.archebiotech.ca (2026 Winter Cohort FTS Participant)

Laura Venier holds an Honours Bachelor of Health Science and a Master of Biomedical Innovation from McMaster University. She is Founder & CEO of Arche Biotechnologies, a medical device startup focused on improving clinical monitoring in acute care, where she has raised over $250k in dilutive investment and won over $100k in nondilutive grants. She is an author on multiple peer-reviewed publications and patents, including multiple filings where she is the sole inventor. Laura has successfully pitched at several competitive venues, including the McMaster Student Seed Fund, the American Urological Association Innovation Nexus Bootcamp, and the OBIO Investment Summit, and brings experience at the intersection of clinical research, technology development, early-stage venture creation, and fundraising.

Faith Oloruntoba, Founder/CEO, Cotriply (2026 Winter Cohort FTS Participant)

Faith Oloruntoba is a serial entrepreneur and travel industry expert with a global background spanning multiple markets. She is the Founder of Cotriply, an AI-powered platform transforming how organizations plan and coordinate group travel. Built from her experience managing complex group trips globally, Cotriply replaces the fragmented process of emails, spreadsheets, and manual coordination with a single AI system that brings structure, visibility, and efficiency to every stage of the group travel experience.

Janice Byrne, Associate Professor in Entrepreneurship at the Ivey Business School.

Janice is an educator, a researcher and scholar-activist. She has studied gender & entrepreneurship for the last 20 years – in a variety of different geographical locations (France, New Zealand, Canada) and contexts (family business; incubators and accelerators, the media).
Her work has been published in numerous international, peer reviewed journals. Her recent work has focused on gender dynamics in the entrepreneurial finance space – with a specific focus on women investors and investor support for women-led firms.   She is bi-lingual (English and French) and an ardent feminist.  Janice is the current holder of the Corus Entertainment Chair in Women in Management at Ivey Business School

properties.title: Sovereign 2026
properties.description:
Canurta Therapeutics, International Business University (IBU) and TrainingGround.AI are collaborating on a one-day flagship event — SOVEREIGN 2026 — designed to explore the intersection of human performance, decentralized finance, and artificial intelligence.
SOVEREIGN 2026 will convene high-performance founders, frontier technologists, institutional leaders, and students for a curated day of insight, capital strategy, and applied innovation. Hosted in partnership between industry and academia, the event will bridge research, venture building, and next-generation talent.
The theme of sovereignty — biological, financial, and digital — is aligned with Toronto Tech Week’s focus on global competitiveness and technological leadership. As founders navigate AI acceleration, capital constraints, and systemic uncertainty, conversations around resilience, ownership, and defensibility are more urgent and relevant than ever.

properties.title: The Shortlist Founder Showcase: Presented by Boardy (Toronto Edition)
properties.description:
The Shortlist (Toronto Edition)
We're coming to Toronto for Tech Week with our friends at Boardy!
The Shortlist is back for its founder monthly Founder Showcase — and the room keeps getting better.
This is a monthly curated, application-only evening connecting early-stage founders with exceptional operators across New York. No panels. No pitches to VCs. Just six founders building breakout companies, a room full of people who've built things themselves, and the kind of candid conversation you don't get at most events.
Each founder will share what they're building, why it matters, and the specific roles they're actively hiring for right now — followed by a mixer.
What to expect: Top founders presenting at the seed through Series B stage. Operators, builders, and executives who've seen what good looks like. An intentionally small room designed for real connection.
Who this is for: Exceptional operators — whether you're actively exploring your next role, casually curious, or simply want to stay close to what's being built in Toronto right now.

Boardy will be helping us curate the room and connect you with the right opportunities after the show. Hop on a quick call to be considered.
Founders will be announced in the days leading up to the event. 👀
Attendance is intentionally limited. Apply to be considered.

properties.title: PMs in the Age of AI: From Break-In to Breakthrough
properties.description:
Are you looking to break into, transition within, or thrive in Product Management in the age of AI? This event is for you.

Hosted by Iris Guo, a former AI PM at Zynga, Scale AI, Microsoft, and Google X, and a Career Coach for 4+ years, and Aleeza Ladhani, a PM at Sago Mini, a Harvard M.Ed., and a former chocolate company founder — generously sponsored by Sago Mini — this event blends learning with meaningful connection.

We’ll begin with a speaker panel exploring how AI is reshaping the PM landscape — from navigating career transitions into product to helping mid–senior PMs evolve their skillsets and stay competitive in an AI-first world.

Following the panel, we’ll move into a job search clinic hosted by Iris to offer practical guidance on positioning yourself for your next move — whether you're breaking in, pivoting, or levelling up.

The evening concludes with a happy hour networking session, where you can connect with peers across experience levels — from aspiring PMs to seasoned operators — in a relaxed, community-driven environment.

🗓 Agenda

5:00 – 5:30 PM | Check-in & Enjoy Refreshments
5:30 – 6:30 PM | Speaker Panel
6:30 – 7:30 PM | Job Search Clinic
6:30 – 8:30 PM | Networking & Happy Hour

Whether you're making a career transition, actively job searching, or looking to future-proof your PM skillset, this event is designed to give you the insights and connections to move forward with confidence in the AI era.

properties.title: The Business Case for Safe Autonomy: Efficiency, Accountability, and Remote Oversight
properties.description:
Autonomy can deliver real efficiency , lower operating costs, better utilization, and more consistent service , but only when deployments are designed with clear accountability and a credible safety approach. 

This virtual session is for founders and companies evaluating autonomy as an operational advantage. We will share a practical, non-proprietary framework to assess autonomy initiatives: where ROI is most achievable, how to define measurable efficiency outcomes, what “safe enough” means in day-to-day operations, and how remote oversight (human-in-the-loop support and escalation) helps maintain control, uptime, and responsible decision-making. 

We will also cover common reasons pilots fail, mis-scoped use cases, unclear success metrics, missing incident processes, and stakeholder misalignment and how to structure pilots that can progress toward scalable deployments. Attendees will leave with a buyer-ready checklist for evaluating autonomy solutions and designing pilots that satisfy both efficiency goals and safety requirements. Q&A included.
About Speaker
Omid Sadeghi, a Toronto-based product leader with 15 years of experience in bridging innovation and commercialization. Over the past 5 years in mobility product commercialization domain, I have built over 20 partnerships worth $3M+ through grants, pilots, and industry collaborations, aligning R&D with market goals. As an early-stage product developer and commercialization strategist, I help startups validate products faster, refine investor narratives, and turn complex tech into scalable solutions.

About Telebotics
Telebotics is developing cost-effective autonomy for urban and campus fleets, helping them operate more safely and efficiently.

properties.title: AI Won’t Fix Commerce — But This Might
properties.description:
AI is everywhere - but selling physical products online is still full of friction.
From unclear product visualization to endless content needs and high return rates, the core experience of buying physical goods hasn’t evolved as fast as the technology around it.
This is a small, curated roundtable for founders, operators, and product leaders to explore what’s actually changing—and what’s not.
Together, we’ll unpack:
• Where AI is creating real impact in commerce
• Why core problems like conversion and returns still persist
• What the next generation of product experience might look like
No panels. No presentations. Just an honest, high-signal conversation with people building and operating in the space.
Space is intentionally limited to keep the discussion thoughtful and engaging.

The AI in Retail (AiR) Meetup is a curated gathering of retail leaders, AI innovators, founders, and investors exploring how artificial intelligence is reshaping the future of commerce.
Presented by RETHINK Retail in partnership with the University of Toronto’s Schwartz Reisman Innovation Campus, the event takes place during Toronto Tech Week and connects the global retail industry with one of North America’s fastest growing technology ecosystems.



The program brings together leaders from retail, AI, venture, and enterprise technology for an evening of strategic content and high-intent networking designed to accelerate collaboration across the commerce and technology sectors.
The AiR Conference & Reception is part of a series of events presented by RETHINK Retail, a global community advancing practical AI adoption across the retail industry. The largest of its kind AI in Retail (AiR) Conference & Reception has been held annually for 5 years now in New York City during the Retail Big Show Event via a direct partnership with the National Retail Federation.

IS THIS FOR ME?

RETHINK Retail
RETHINK Retail is the global community and intelligence platform shaping the future of commerce. Tom Masterson is the Chief Operating Officer at Deep Genomics. Tom leverages his experience in health strategy, research, and medical devices to bring structure and continuity to the company. He oversees the Project Management Office, governance, and executive operations of the company.
Ricky Mehra is the General Partner of Continuum Health Ventures based in Toronto and the Bay Area. He invests in early stage deep healthtech and the longevity space. Rickly is also part of the team at NextMed.Health a global conference bringing together people connected to the future of healthcare.

This hands-on workshop will teach you how to ask for more and get it confidently!
But when it’s time to talk about salary, promotion, or offers?
Suddenly it feels… unclear.
This high-energy, hands-on workshop is built for all women who are ready to:
Negotiate a new offer
Ask for a raise
Position themselves for promotion
Transition into higher-impact roles
Stop underpricing your skills
If you’ve ever thought,
“I know I’m good at what I do… I just don’t know how to say it in a salary conversation.”
This room is for you.

✨ A guided, hands-on negotiation simulation

The 10-Minute Raise Strategy

Negotiating in Tech

Live Practice Rounds

properties.title: Cash Only, A Fintech North Event
properties.description:
A room full of people who move money for a living, standing around a bar called Cash Only.
Yes. We know.
This is Fintech North's event during Toronto Tech Week. Co-hosted with ATB and Sokin. The format is simple: show up, talk to interesting people, leave knowing more people than you did before. We've done this enough times to know that nobody needs another panel. What people actually need is a room worth being in.
Who's in the room
Fintech founders and operators. Payments, lending, banking, insurance, investing. Seed to growth.
Bank executives and leaders from across Canadian financial institutions.
Builders from the broader Toronto tech community who are too curious to stay in their lane.
Why this one
It's Wednesday night during Toronto Tech Week. You've been sitting in sessions all day. You don't want another keynote. You want a drink and a conversation with someone who gets it.
The guest list is curated. The room is deliberately small. If you've been to a Fintech North event before, you know. If you haven't, this is a good place to start.
Powered by Fintech North, a community fintech practitioners.
Space is limited. Approval required.
Co-Hosted With:

ATB Financial
As the largest financial institution headquartered in Western Canada, ATB Financial offers a comprehensive suite of products and services to our clients across our Personal and Business Banking, ATB Wealth Management, and ATB Capital Markets businesses. We empower our clients with expert advice and solutions across our extensive branch network, dedicated Client Care Centre, and digital banking options.
Uniquely in Canada, ATB Financial provides comprehensive crypto and fintech financial advisory. Our experts specialize in payment and liquidity solutions, corporate and investment banking, market research, and FX & fixed income, all tailored for crypto and fintech businesses and their ecosystems. We offer industry-leading expertise, actively advising regulators and governments across Canada and in Alberta.


Sokin
Sokin was founded in 2019 with a simple vision to remove borders, barriers and burdens associated with international payments. Today it enables global businesses to send and exchange more than 70 currencies and hold balances in 26 currencies with its multi-currency IBAN and local currency accounts — all through one comprehensive platform that streamlines cross-border accounts payable, receivable, and treasury operations. Headquartered in the United Kingdom, the company has offices in the United States, Canada, United Arab Emirates, Singapore, Mexico, Norway and India. For more information, visit www.sokin.com.
Event Partners:


Central1
Trusted by more than 280 credit unions, fintechs and challenger financial institutions across Canada, Central 1 is a leading payments and banking infrastructure partner. We securely move money every day through end‑to‑end payments, clearing and settlement, and treasury services that help clients scale faster, reduce complexity and improve cost efficiency—backed by a non‑competing model that puts your priorities first.


Triyo
TRIYO enables financial institutions to modernize legacy AML infrastructure, integrating with existing core banking systems and workflows without rip-and-replace disruption. By capturing investigator workflows and structuring every case, transaction, and filing into an audit-ready data foundation, TRIYO delivers 360° visibility across the client lifecycle, automated regulatory reporting, and measurable efficiency gains across your compliance function while building the AI-ready data layer your institution needs for what comes next.

Truly Financial
Truly Financial is a financial technology company that provides an all-in-one global banking platform designed for small businesses and startups. It allows users to open local multi-currency accounts in the US, Canada, UK, and Europe, enabling them to receive payments and manage foreign exchange without traditional international wire fees. Additionally, the platform simplifies global operations by offering corporate Visa cards and integrating with tools like Stripe and Gusto to streamline team payments and payroll.

Midas Labs
Midas Labs is a forward-deployed AI engineering and product studio that accelerates zero-to-one product builds by acting as a technical co-founder rather than a traditional vendor. They utilize an AI-native delivery model where artificial intelligence handles the heavy lifting of coding, while their team of senior engineers provides the critical judgment, context, and decision-making. The company specializes in rapidly shipping AI-native infrastructure, data systems, and highly scalable platforms for heavily regulated markets such as banking and fintech.

properties.title: Powering Compass Group: North America's Largest Hospitality Provider
properties.description:
Operating one of the world's largest hospitality companies requires a powerful digital backbone.
You may not have heard our name, but you have likely dined with us. Compass Group delivers dining and support services on some of the biggest stages in the world. We were the foodservice behind Super Bowl LX at Levi's Stadium, the Oscars, the Kentucky Derby, and countless other events. Every day, we fuel 98 of the Fortune 100, leading universities, and healthcare systems.
The hospitality industry is undergoing a fundamental transformation, from how guests experience service, to how operations are managed at scale, to how AI and data are reshaping decision-making across thousands of locations. The questions this raises aren't unique to any one company; they're the defining challenges of our industry.
Through a series of keynotes, panels, and conversations, we're bringing together perspectives on where hospitality technology is heading, exploring the trends, tradeoffs, and ideas shaping the future of the industry. 

• From Curious to Hired: Cracking the AI Career Code

properties.title: Claude Cowork for Creators: Building an AI Workspace That Actually Knows You
properties.description:
If you've tried Cowork or any AI assistant and walked away thinking "this is fine, I guess," you already know the problem: the tools are powerful but the setup is empty. Every session starts cold, with no memory of who you are, what you make, or how you think.
Sean Oulashin is Stan's Senior Video Editor a Creator (500K+ across platforms). He spent the last several months building "Thinkspace", a done-for-you Cowork setup specifically for Creators.
In this 45-minute workshop, Sean will show you:
The two foundational files every Cowork workspace needs, and why most setups skip them
The exact interview process he uses to build a creator's identity file in under 20 minutes
How to wire Cowork into the tools creators already use (Notion, Granola, Slack, calendar, Drive)
Where Cowork breaks for creators specifically, and the workarounds he's tested
A live walkthrough of his own Thinkspace, so you can see what "good" looks like
Who this is for
Creators publishing content who want AI to actually help, not generate slop
Solopreneurs and operators running their own brand or business
You don't need to be technical to attend! Sean built Thinkspace specifically for non-engineers.

properties.title: How To Close Enterprise Deals in Founder-Led Sales
properties.description:
As startup founders trying to sell into the enterprise, we’ve all heard these remarks before: “Great demo” then crickets, or “this is awesome but we have some other priorities right now, can we regroup in a couple of months?” We’ve also seen pilots or proof-of-concepts (POCs) that don’t lead to closed deals, difficulty getting to the right people involved, or seemingly positive conversations that don’t move the deal forward.
The good news:  many of your lost deals were your fault.
Many of the causes of mid-funnel deals stalling or challenges with closing deals are preventable with some basic tactics for navigating complex buyer environments.  The session will provide some immediately actionable and practical tips for diagnosing problems, keeping deals moving, and maximizing value.
Note: This session is designed for startup founders. Space is limited, and preference will be given to early-stage founders. Final location details will be shared upon approval.

properties.title: AI Fundamentals: How Executives Are Cutting Through the Noise
properties.description:
The best leaders right now aren't the ones who know the most about AI. They're the ones who know the right questions to ask and how to act on the answers.
AI is already on your board's agenda, and you’re registered for this event, you're already thinking about it the right way. This session is about sharpening that thinking. The shift to SaaS didn't require executives to understand the code, it required them to know when and how to move. AI is the same call.
We'll cut through the noise on what AI actually is. The difference between machine learning, predictive AI, and generative AI isn't just technical; it changes how you evaluate investments, manage risk, and ask the right questions of your team. We'll also look honestly at where GenAI is creating friction inside organizations today and what smart leaders are doing about it.
From there, we'll get into the frameworks that make AI decisions easier: risk tiers, data governance, and a checklist you can bring back to your leadership team.
We close with a panel featuring a CFO, an advisory expert, and a technologist sharing what they're seeing on the ground. You’ll get a real perspective from people navigating the same decisions you are.
You'll leave with:
An understanding of how AI is shaping our culture
A mindset shift around embracing AI as the modern-day industrial revolution
Practical insight on where GenAI friction shows up and how to get ahead of it
Clarity on the types of AI and how to use that knowledge in boardroom conversations
Where to look in your business to find the most approachable processes to improve with the highest ROI
Most importantly, you’ll leave knowing what not to do. In a space moving this fast, the advantage isn’t in reacting; it’s in making the right calls while others are still figuring it out. This session will help you lead that conversation internally with more clarity and confidence.
Speaker

Johnny Than, MBA, is the Founder and CEO of Appficiency and AskCipher. With over 30 years of enterprise IT experience, he has guided Appficiency to become a globally recognized digital transformation consultancy, Oracle NetSuite's Canadian Alliance Partner of the Year in 2025, and launched AskCipher, an enterprise AI platform that sits as an intelligent layer across ERP, CRM, and digital ecosystems. A McMaster MBA graduate, Johnny has consulted with over 300 organizations and oversees solutions serving 1,000+ clients worldwide.
Panellists

Alex Miles, MBA, is a Partner at 180 Systems, an independent technology consulting firm based out of Toronto, ON, Canada focused on system selection and implementation for mid‑market organizations. Alex has led technology selections and implementations across industries including financial services, professional services, food & beverage, manufacturing, distribution, and construction among others. He has been a featured speaker at the National Technology Forum, presenting on implementation execution, transition to sustainment, and AI/emerging technologies; an industry expert speaker on Epicor’s In Today’s Market series, covering AI, supply chain inflation, cloud ERP trade-offs, and change management; and a recurring speaker at Project Summit BA World, where he has presented on technology project management versus project leadership.

Gareth Doherty, Ph.D., M.S.M., is an executive leader with public and private sector experience in strategy, data science, advanced analytics, artificial intelligence, and behavioural research — with a passion for leading high-performing teams that fuse science and creativity to solve complex business problems. A recipient of the Governor General's Meritorious Service Medal for his leadership developing a Total Health and Wellness Strategy for the Department of National Defence and Canadian Armed Forces, Gareth's academic research explored cognitive psychology, human reasoning, and the application of AI to human cognition. He also dabbles in cognitive security and disinformation research with industry leaders — though his wife and three daughters think he needs better hobbies.

Shayan Rastgou, MBA, is the co-founder of AskCipher and the creator of its agentic AI system, a platform that sits between enterprise users and business applications like NetSuite and Salesforce to automate complex workflows. An early adopter of agentic AI models, he has spent the last several years architecting production-grade systems on LangGraph for enterprise environments, with a particular focus on how autonomous agents can be reliably deployed inside regulated, mission-critical operations. His approach is shaped by an unusual academic foundation: an undergraduate degree in Biomedical Sciences with a minor in Psychology from the University of Ottawa, from which he draws heavily applying scientific methodology to agent evaluation and system design, and bringing principles from cognitive and behavioural psychology to how agents reason, recover from error, and interact with human operators. He also holds an MBA from McMaster University's DeGroote School of Business, where his focus was the intersection of AI capability and enterprise adoption.

properties.title: Capital Without Dilution: The Global Incentive Stack for AI & Tech Growth
properties.description:
Toronto Tech Week 2026 | Hosted by NovaForge AI Venture Lab
For AI and technology companies, growth is no longer funded from one market, one program, or one financing round.
The most strategic companies are learning how to combine local innovation incentives, global market-entry programs, export and trade support, jurisdictional partnerships, institutional capital, and non-dilutive funding into one expansion strategy.
Capital Without Dilution: The Global Incentive Stack for AI & Tech Growth is a curated Toronto Tech Week gathering for founders, operators, investors, funders, jurisdictional partners, and ecosystem leaders exploring how high-growth companies can fund R&D, commercialization, and international expansion without giving up ownership too early.
Most events separate these conversations: grants in one room, venture capital in another, export support somewhere else, and global expansion in a completely different setting. - This event brings them together.
How can AI and technology companies stack capital across markets to accelerate innovation, enter new regions, extend runway, and unlock global growth?
Built as the Toronto continuation of NovaForge AI Venture Lab’s international engagement around AFF 2026, this event convenes a focused room of up to 200 founders, operators, investors, funders, and ecosystem partners for serious fireside chats, high-value panels, and curated networking.
This is not a generic startup mixer or a grant workshop and is not an expo floor.  It is a practical, high-value room for people building, funding, advising, and attracting the next generation of global AI and technology companies.
Explore how companies can combine:
Local & global incentives to support R&D, product development, hiring, commercialization, and market entry
Non-dilutive funding pathways to extend runway and reduce unnecessary financing pressure
Jurisdictional and soft-landing programs to access new markets, customers, partners, talent, and operating environments
Stackable capital strategies that sequence grants, credits, institutional support, strategic partnerships, and equity at the right stage
Founders: This is a practical playbook for funding growth more intelligently.
Investors: A sharper view of how non-dilutive capital can support portfolio expansion and reduce financing pressure.
The companies that win the next decade will not simply be the ones that raise the most capital. They will be the ones that know how to combine capital intelligently across markets.
Capital Without Dilution is for the stakeholders who want to understand that full stack.
Presented by: NovaForge AI Venture Lab — an Ontario non-profit organization building and shaping a world-class incubator/accelerator and AI/Tech Hub within a 50,000-sq-ft building. NovaFoge is actively building and advancing efforts to shape a globally focused, Canadian Innovation Hub & Co-working Centre, that will ultimately pair mentorship, capital access, and deep resources that turn bold ideas into enduring businesses. https://novaforge.ca

properties.description:
Go from idea to prototype without a full technical team.
Designed for founders and builders at the earliest stages, you’ll learn how to use AI tools to move from concept to prototype faster, how they help to reduce development costs, and how it can support smarter product decisions from day one.
Through a mix of guided exercises, live building, and collaborative problem-solving, you’ll experience how AI can act as your co-founder across coding, design, and MVP development. Come with an idea or join a team and leave with the skills to turn an idea into a working prototype.
This workshop is offered by the Schwartz Reisman Innovation Campus Ecosystem and UTEST, in partnership with Codalio.

Aspiring and early-stage founders

Innovators interested in prototyping ideas using AI tools

How to use AI tools to accelerate product development from idea to prototype

A clearer understanding of how to scope and prioritize your first product
Confidence to start building even without a technical background

properties.title: Toronto takes on the world: Winning strategies for global growth
properties.description:
Global expansion is one of the biggest steps a company can make, but navigating legal requirements, regulatory hurdles and ongoing economic and geopolitical tensions means this journey has never been more complex.
Toronto Takes on the World brings together local founders and who have done exactly that. You’ll hear how Toronto-based ventures approached international expansion, what worked, what did not and how you can get it right — the first time.
From leveraging the city’s diverse talent and globally-connected ecosystem to identifying the correct markets to expand into, this conversation focuses on the decisions behind scaling beyond Canada. Learn how Export Development Canada and the Trade Commissioner Service can support your growth.
Join us after the discussion to connect with founders, experts and peers going global.

Speakers
Orion Buske, CEO, PhenoTips
Jason Robinson, CEO & Co-Founder, Evoco
Zinnia Jamshed, Trade Commissioner with Global Affairs Canada
Rohit Sethi, CEO, Security Compass
Lindsay Whiston-Babcock, Senior Relationship Manager, Mid-Market Business, Export Development Canada (moderator)

properties.title: Carta: Engineering the Future of Private Markets (Tech Talk and Happy Hour)
properties.description:
Join us at our brand-new Toronto office for an evening of deep-tech discussions, tactical AI applications, and a first look at how Carta is architecting the next generation of financial infrastructure.

As we plant our flag in Toronto, we’re opening our doors to the city’s engineering community to discuss the complex challenges of moving from a "cap table tool" to a full-scale Financial ERP. If you’re interested in how high-scale data infrastructure meets the cutting edge of Agentic AI, this is the room you want to be in.

The Agenda:

6:00 PM: Doors Open | Drinks, snacks, and Networking

6:30 PM: Talk 1: Architecture for the ERP Vision

Explore how we built the Customer Data Warehouse (DWH), a centerpiece of our modern data stack. We’ll dive into the technical hurdles of centralizing multi-product data and how we use Agentic AI to turn static records into dynamic, custom product experiences.

7:15 PM: Talk 2: Mastering Unstructured Data at Scale
Learn how we transform fragmented documents into a structured, queryable data repository. We’ll showcase how our platform team bridges the gap between raw LLM outputs and production-grade data products

8:00 PM: Mix & Mingle with the Carta Engineering team
Drinks and food will be provided!

properties.title: Who Decides Who Gets Funded
properties.description:
FOUNDER & FUNDER LOUNGE — APPLICATIONS NOW OPEN
A small, pre-event curated session  bringing:
20 high-potential founders preparing to raise
10 funders actively deploying capital
Designed to create direct, aligned introductions and shorten the path to capital.
4:00 PM
MNP, 1 Adelaide St E, Toronto
Application Deadline: May 14
Note for founders:
Founders registered for the event will receive an application link to the Founder & Funder Lounge through the InclusifAI platform.
PANEL EVENT & NETWORKING
AI is already influencing how companies are evaluated, how risk is interpreted, and how capital moves.
But when funding decisions continue to rely on familiar networks, repeat signals, and incomplete data, strong founders get missed. AI can make that problem faster, not fairer.
This session brings founders, investors, and ecosystem leaders into a focused room to examine how funding decisions are made, where bias enters the process, and what it takes to assess companies more accurately.

WHO THIS IS FOR
• Founders actively preparing to raise or currently in-market
• Investors and capital allocators actively deploying and reviewing deals
• Operators directly influencing funding decisions or pipeline development

WHAT YOU LEAVE WITH
For founders
Clarity on how you are being evaluated and where you are likely being filtered out before a decision is ever communicated.
For funders
A sharper lens on how decisions are being made today, where standard approaches create blind spots, and how to assess more accurately without lowering rigor.
ABOUT INCLUSIFAI
InclusifAI is a funding intelligence platform that helps founders get funding-ready and helps investors make better funding decisions.
We bring structure to how founders are evaluated and how capital is allocated.

properties.title: Women in Venture: Panel + Mentorship Mixer | Toronto Tech Week 2026
properties.description:
Venture can feel like a world with invisible gates.
So come rewire your network and your trajectory alongside powerhouse investors from top North American VCs: Inovia Capital, Drive Capital, Georgian, Panache Ventures, and Northside Ventures.
Celebrate where you've come from and where you're going next in a room where you'll actually save a number afterward, have a laugh over drinks, and ask the "embarrassing" question because we're women lifting each other up.
Whether you’re early in your career, making a pivot, or going all in on venture, you belong in this room.

Meet the panelists

Nazuk Thakkar | Canadian GM, Drive Capital
Nazuk (Naz) Thakkar is Drive Capital's Canadian general manager, a $3B AUM fund and the largest venture fund between the coasts.
She leads Drive's early-stage investing across Canada, backing founders building category-defining companies. She also spends much of her time building ecosystem initiatives to support founders and underrepresented communities in tech.


Katherine Hovdestad | Investor, Inovia Capital
Inovia Capital is a leading full-stack venture capital firm with $2.5B USD in assets under management, with operations in Canada, the Bay Area, London, and Abu Dhabi.
Prior to Inovia, Katherine's career brought her to McKinsey and EY. Katherine graduated from the University of Toronto, Victoria University in 2022 with a Bachelor of Commerce and a Minor in Economics.


Lauren Pineau | Head of Fund Ops, Georgian
Based in Toronto, Georgian is a growth equity firm investing in B2B tech, with $5.9B in assets under management and has invested in more than 80 companies.
Lauren Pineau is the Head of Fund Operations at Georgian, where she leads fund accounting, financial reporting, and other key operational processes that enable Georgian’s investment activities. She leads a team of three people and maintains strong financial oversight in a fast-paced technology environment.
Before joining Georgian, Lauren was a Senior Accountant at KPMG, where she advised clients on audit and financial reporting. Lauren is also a Chartered Professional Accountant (CPA), and holds a Graduate Diploma in Accounting and a BBA with Distinction from Wilfrid Laurier University.
By day, Lauren is a detail-driven finance professional; outside of work, she’s an avid traveler who has explored 13 countries in the past two years. Her sense of curiosity brings a unique energy to her work and informs her perspective on supporting the next generation of women in finance.


Naomi Walch | Investor, Northside Ventures
Northside Ventures is a pre-seed and seed stage venture capital firm founded in 2023 backing exceptional Canadian founders, often at ideation. Core investment categories include vertical AI, AI infrastructure and frontier tech with ~$500K checks.
Naomi works closely with founders from first meeting through diligence. She also leads the firm’s intern and scout programs and supports internal ops. Naomi graduated from the Ivey Business School at Western University in 2025.

Inshaal Qadir | Investor, Panache Ventures
Panache Ventures is a recognized as Canada's most active pre-seed venture capital firm, writing first cheques up to $1.5M from their $100M Fund II. Panache portfolio companies have collectively raised over $3.3B.
Inshaal brings a unique perspective shaped by experience across three venture capital firms, Panache Ventures, BDC Capital, and GreenSky Ventures, where she has been involved in deal sourcing, diligence, and portfolio support across the Canadian early-stage ecosystem.
Prior to her career in venture, Inshaal worked at CIBC Capital Markets, where she supported the rollout of a trading platform. She holds a BBA from the University of Toronto.

The evening
6:00 – 6:45 PM | Mingling + Food
Settle in, grab a drink, and start building relationships before we even hit the stage.
6:45 – 7:45 PM | Panel + Q&A
Hear from powerhouse women shaping Canadian VC.
How they got their start.
What they wish they knew.
What they're looking for in the next generation.
Bring ALL your questions! Yes, even the ones you think are "too basic" (they're not).
7:45 – 8:30 PM | Mentorship + Networking
Have a bite and sit down with some of the most incredible women in venture and tech. Swap numbers and ideas.

About Waterloo Venture Group
WVG is a student venture community and talent network based out of Waterloo, providing pro-bono consultancy for founders from ideation through Series A. Collectively, we’ve supported ventures in raising $8.8M+, hosted flagship programming on founder education, and curated the rooms where teams form and hires happen.
Ready to build or partner? Contact the WVG team.

properties.title: Smart Routes: Engineering Trust in the Data Journey
properties.description:
Speaker: Improving Toronto, President - Luke McGrath
Luke is a seasoned leader in technology strategy and digital transformation, with a track record of helping organizations turn complex challenges into practical, scalable solutions.
Prior to joining Improving, Luke held senior leadership roles including Director, Cloud & Data at PwC and Chief Technology Officer at Hitachi Solutions, where he guided large-scale modernization initiatives spanning data, analytics, and enterprise platforms.

Topic: Smart Routes: Engineering Trust in the Data Journey
As data becomes the backbone of decision-making, trust becomes its most critical asset. This session introduces Smart Routes, a modern approach to data engineering that applies supply chain principles to automate, streamline, and build confidence throughout the data journey. Leave with a blue-print for:
Automating the Data Journey from Source to Insight. Discover how standardized connectors, automated quality checks, and reusable ingestion pipelines reduce manual effort and improve reliability.
Building Confidence with Observability and Governance. Execute telemetry, lineage tracking, and policy-as-code for creating a control tower for your data estate—ensuring every data product is traceable, compliant, and trusted.
Focusing on Strategic Impact, Not Operational Overhead. Shift from reactive troubleshooting to proactive optimization, enabling teams to deliver trusted insights faster and with greater efficiency.
Engineer trust into your data journey to unlock smarter decisions, reduce technical debt, and create a self-improving data supply chain.
Audience: Engineers, Open To Everyone
Theme: Engineering, Open to all industries
Format: Networking, Happy Hour

properties.title: Krista Kim’s Heart Space at Toronto Tech Week 2026
properties.description:
When Worlds Converge: Art, AI & the Future of Human-Centered Innovation.
Co‑Hosted by Schulich Office of Innovation & Entrepreneurship, Pursuits Inc. and Demo Room!

Event Overview
Schulich’s Office of Innovation & Entrepreneurship, Pursuits Inc, and Demo Room present a flagship event exploring the intersection of art, artificial intelligence, biometrics, and digital trust.
The first public announcement and advance look inside the Heart Space Canadian exhibition tour (2027), a groundbreaking immersive digital artwork by internationally acclaimed artist Krista Kim, as she returns home following years of international exhibitions in Hong Kong, London, Barcelona, Seoul, Dubai, Las Vegas, New York, and more.

Heart Space is the world’s first collaborative, AI-generative immersive artwork driven by real-time biometric data. Participants’ heartbeats (their unique biometric imprint) are translated into dynamic colours and waveforms, creating a continuously evolving collective composition…
It is also an opportunity to understand the groundbreaking technology of Tenbeo (a Brussels-based biotechnology company pioneering identity authentication through advanced biometric AI) and technology provider behind the exhibition.
As AI advances, protecting digital identities and proof-of-humanity protocols are key to safeguarding human agency, creativity, and dignity…
Join us to hear Krista Kim and an amazing panel of arts and technology leaders explore these crucial concepts and delve into "proof-of-humanity" in the age of Artificial Intelligence.
Toronto Tech Week is a citywide celebration of the people building what’s next. From May 25–29, 2026, founders, investors, and builders come together for hundreds of community-led events across Toronto, connecting tens of thousands of people around Canadian tech. Torontotechweek.com.

What to expect:
Heart Space VIP Event (3 pm)
Welcome & national launch announcement
Keynote by Krista Kim (bio details below)
Cross-disciplinary panel discussion
Immersive art activation in Demo Room (Toronto's hottest new event venue)
Live demonstration of continuous authentication & AI identity security by Tenbeo
Heart Space Social/Party (7 pm)
Networking reception and startup tech demos
Event social and party, featuring 4KORNERS and DJ P
Food stations hosted byYSpace Food & Beverage
The Black Entrepreneurship Alliance

Meet the Artist

Krista Kim is a Canadian-Korean artist known for her exploration of digital consciousness. Her work investigates the impact of digital technology on human perception, communication, and social structures. Through her practice, Kim pioneers a new language of calm and clarity for the digital age, with career highlights that include:
Cultural Leader of the World Economic Forum
Recognized by UNESCO as one of '50 Minds for the Next 50’
2025 Infinity Festival Monolith Awards - Fine Art
2024 & 2025 Excellence in Digital Art Award, Createurs Design Awards, Paris
Member of the Oxford Society of Aging and Longevity
First female NFT artist collected by LACMA
Exhibited at Hermitage State Museum, Times Square, the Sphere Las Vegas, and Davos 2024 (World Economic Forum)
Metaverse Editor for Vogue Singapore
Initiated the Techism Movement (2014), a philosophical framework recognizing technological innovation as an artistic discipline

Meet the Technology Partner- Tenbeo
Tenbeo is a Brussels-based biotechnology company pioneering identity authentication through advanced biometric AI. In collaboration with Krista Kim Studio, Tenbeo brings the Heart Space experience to life by integrating its cutting-edge heart wave recognition technology.
Tenbeo’s technology uses a person’s distinct heart waveform to authenticate identity by analyzing real-time biometric data, ensuring only the true individual can access sensitive systems, prevent fraud, and secure digital transactions. Each person is made of unique electrical patterns.

Details on the 2027 Exhibition
The full Heart Space Canadian Exhibition will launch in 2027 as part of a national tour. This 2026 event offers a rare first encounter with the work, and an opportunity to experience the vision of Krista Kim, the globally celebrated Canadian artist behind the work.

properties.description:

What is the event about:
Hands-on workshop: build and deploy an on-chain app
Curious about how smart contracts actually work? In this interactive workshop, we’ll go from concept to deployment and build a simple on-chain application together.
You’ll learn the fundamentals of smart contracts and Web3 architecture, then write and deploy your own contract using Remix IDE. Participants will connect their wallets with MetaMask, deploy the contract to a test network on Ethereum, and interact with it.
This session is designed to be beginner-friendly (to web3) but hands-on — perfect for developers, founders, and anyone curious about building blockchain-powered applications.
What we’ll cover:
• How smart contracts work
• The architecture of Web3 applications
• Writing a simple contract
• Deploying and interacting with the contract
• Understanding on-chain transactions
What to bring:
A laptop and a curious mind.

properties.title: The Tech Expert to Leader Playbook
properties.description:
Most people are promoted because they are excellent technical experts, only to find that lead roles require a completely different set of skills. This transition can feel overwhelming when you are trying to balance high technical standards with the needs of a growing team.
This 4-hour workshop provides structured frameworks to handle management responsibilities with the same confidence you bring to technical projects. We avoid complicated theories in favor of practical tools that help you move from being involved in every small detail to helping your team operate independently.
Who this is for
This workshop is for technical experts or engineers who have recently moved into a lead role.
It is designed for senior team members being groomed for promotion who want to prepare for the shift in responsibilities.
It is a fit for those who have been managing a team for a while and are looking for more structure in their daily leadership.
We welcome leads who want to move away from the small details and focus on helping their team operate independently.
What We’ll Cover
We will look at how to step back from individual execution to focus on team-level oversight.
You will learn practical ways to manage your personal bandwidth and protect your mental energy.
We will bring simple frameworks to help you navigate the transition into management.
We will use exercises to help you practice moving from an effective contributor to a transformational leader.
What’s Included
A printed leadership workbook with frameworks you can apply immediately.
A catered networking lunch with other West-End tech leaders.
Tech Week Special: A shorter, high-impact format designed to fit into your busy schedule.
About Instructor
Karen Wonders is an engineer and Certified Executive Coach with over two decades of experience in technical leadership. Holding an Executive MBA, she specializes in the pivotal transition from effective contributor to transformational leader. Karen combines deep practical experience with coaching expertise to empower technical professionals to lead with confidence and impact.

properties.title: AI Fundamentals for Founders Workshop
properties.description:
AI is moving fast.
New tools are dropping every week. Everyone has an opinion on what you should be using. And somehow, despite all the hype, most founders are still buried in manual work, wondering where the time savings actually are.
If that sounds familiar, this workshop is for you.
In one room, in one session, we're going to cut through the noise together.
What we'll cover:
The latest AI innovations worth your attention and the ones you can safely ignore
The essential tools to consider for your stack, with honest takes on what works and what doesn't
What Trevor has tested across his own ventures and the real results behind it
Frameworks, mindsets, and practical skills you can apply the same week
A live build session working through real examples together, so you leave with something tangible, not just notes
You'll come away with:
A clearer picture of where AI actually fits in your business right now
A starter toolkit matched to your stage and goals
Practical skills to run and grow your business with AI without the expensive trial and error
More confidence, less overwhelm
Trevor will tailor the session based on who's in the room.
Before the event, you'll share a bit about your business and what you're working on. That way, the tools, examples, and workflows covered are relevant to where you actually are, not some hypothetical founder.
The goal isn't to add more subscriptions to your stack.
It's to leave better equipped to run and grow your business with AI. Freeing your time. Empowering you to move faster. Without running up an expensive testing bill figuring it out alone.
Hosted in partnership with George Brown Polytechnic, startGBC entrepreneur support centre and Toronto Tech Week.
Trevor Davis is a Toronto-based founder and AI marketing systems builder who runs multiple ventures almost entirely on AI-powered workflows. He'll walk you through what's actually working right now, what he's tested, and what results he's seen, so you can stop experimenting blindly and start building with intention. See more at DigiwellMarketing.com.

properties.title: Why General AI Breaks in Real Business Workflows
properties.description:
**This event has reached capacity and registration is now closed. We’re glad to see the available spots filled up quickly.
Please attend only if you have received a registration confirmation. Waitlist status does not confirm attendance. If space becomes available, we will contact waitlisted guests closer to the event date.
If you are registered but can no longer attend, please let us know so we can release your spot to someone on the waitlist. Thank you for your support.**

General-purpose AI tools can be impressive in demos, but real business workflows are a different story. Once you add industry rules, live data, customer expectations, and revenue pressure, generic AI often starts to break.
In this Toronto Tech Week session, Bryan Xu, Co-founder of DealerAI, will share what it takes to make AI work in high-stakes operating environments. Using automotive retail as a case study, this session will explore why specialized AI systems often outperform broad tools when speed, context, and execution matter.
This is a practical session for business leaders who want to understand where AI creates real operational value, where it falls short, and what it takes to apply AI effectively in customer-facing workflows.
What You’ll Learn
Why general AI often struggles in real operational workflows
What makes specialized AI more effective in complex industries
How to think about workflow integration, quality control, and ROI
What business leaders should consider before applying AI in customer-facing operations
Who Should Attend
Business owners and CEOs
General managers and auto group executives
Sales, operations, and marketing leaders
Customer experience and revenue team leaders
Anyone responsible for scaling customer communication and follow-up
Attendance
Capacity is limited, and registration is recommended.
