properties.title: Beyond AI: Building Human-Centered Businesses in a Digital-First World
properties.description:
In a week of pitches, panels, and performances, this is a room designed for something different: honest conversation between people building things that actually matter to them, and who refuse to lose the human layer in the process.
The question we're sitting with: What did you choose to keep human when you could have automated it - and what happened?
Arrive + Land: You'll walk in mid-week, probably overstimulated. We start with a reset. Tea, breathwork, a moment to actually arrive in your body before we ask anything of your mind. No name tags with company logos. Just your name and what you're building that matters to you.
Lightning Provocations:  A few builders take the floor for a few minutes each. No slides. No decks. Just a real answer to a real question: what did you keep human, and why?
Intentional Connection: Structured 1-on-1 conversations. The kind of conversations you came to Tech Week hoping to have but couldn't find in a room of 500.
Who this is for: Founders, operators, investors, designers, and builders working at the intersection of technology and human connection. People building software that strengthens relationships. Physical spaces that create belonging. Hybrid models that refuse to sacrifice depth for scale. If you're pro-technology and pro-human, you'll feel at home.
Who this is not for: People looking for a pitch event, a panel, or surface-level networking. This is an intimate, curated evening — applications are reviewed and the room is capped at 60.
What to expect: Warm lighting. Intentional sound. Elevated non-alcoholic and alcoholic drinks. Conversations that surprise you. A room full of people who actually want to be in the room. The kind of evening you'll still be thinking about on Friday.
Talk - Buy Me Love: Living Capital Amidst Collapse
Our old systems are collapsing. Let them go. We now have the ecological comprehension, cultural maturity, technological tools, and scientific insight to give life to systems and metrics that are realistic about ecological and human flourishing. More than a call to expanded awareness, this talk will describe actionable tools for devouring system shocks and spitting out abundance. By mapping your living capital beyond money, melting into luxurious cocreativity, maxxing your most luscious UX, and being met by lucid coconspirators, your organization can find long-term sustenance in chewy, real-world value flows that outlast brittle and inaccurate abstractions. Your work matters more than money can measure and for more than just this moment.
Bio: Rosebridges Starchuk Code is a large-scale systems architect interested in ecological flourishing through the liberation of emergent cocreativity, working from computer science, neuroeconomics, social neuroscience and psychosocial development. She has been realizing new, complex, and award-winning projects throughout twenty-five years in executive, creative and technical roles. Her writing has appeared in The Financial Times and she’s been a member of the Aspen Institute’s Virtually Human Working Group. She’s been featured in the Globe and Mail, BBC, Guardian, and others. She is now the founder of Oooooze Corp., Lespionage Corp., and Innerorbital Psychostructures Corp.
About Pluto:
Pluto is Toronto’s fourth space for founders, creators, and conscious operators - a curated community hub where work, wellness, and culture converge. We bring together ambitious people building meaningful things, offering a home for deep work, thoughtful connection, and transformative experiences that move ideas—and the people behind them—forward.

properties.title: Two Steps Forward: Life After The 9-5
properties.description:
🧺 This outdoor picnic is for Mid-Career Women in Business and Technology who are planning the move from a full-time role to independent consulting.
Moving from a stable 9–5 to consulting is exciting but challenging. Many successful consultants spend 1 to 2 years preparing: building relationships, financial stability and a clear plan.
We’ll be joined by women who have successfully made this transition. They’ll share their journeys, surprises, lessons learned, and practical advice for those preparing to take the leap.

THIS EVENT IF FOR YOU IF YOU ARE
👉 A mid-career woman in business and technology ready to become an independent consultant
👉 Currently working full-time and planning your transition
👉 Looking to learn from others and connect with like-minded peers
TOPICS INCLUDE
Preparing strategically without jeopardizing your current job
Developing your service offering
Building relationships and potential clients in advance
Reducing financial risk and building foundations before leaving
Navigating the emotional shift from employee to independent professional

EVENT VIBE
Casual, supportive and practical. Learn from those who have taken their next steps forward.

SPECIAL GUESTS

SCHEDULE

11:00 am - 11:15 am
Set up your blanket and introduce yourself
11:15 am - 11:30 am
Consultants tell their stories
11:30 am - 1:00 pm
Guided Q&A

Keep walking South until you see a geometric fountain/pool and a wooden hanging garden. Walk through the hanging garden and then down the hill. Our group will be there, on a hillside.

RAIN PLAN

IMPORTANT NOTES

properties.description:
Part of the Kolbe 15 Rain or Shine series.
The calm before the tech storm. Start your morning on the water as the sun rises, take a moment to be present, set the mood for the day.
We meet at Cherry Beach at 6am, get on the water, and paddle out as the sun comes up. No agenda, no pitch, no performance. Just you, the water, and the people around you.
Once we're out, we stop. For 20 minutes we close our eyes, put the week away, and just be present with the sound of the water, the air, the moment. No experience required. No instruction. Just space to breathe.
Then we have tea and coffee on the water, a small snack, and talk. The kind of conversation that happens when you're floating on a lake at sunrise with people who get it.
We paddle back by 8am. You're at your desk by 9am. But you'll feel different for the rest of the day.
Resilience Level: 1/10
Schedule
6:00 AM — Meet at Cherry Beach, get your boards
6:10 AM — Paddle out
6:40 AM — 20 min quiet presence on the water
7:00 AM — Tea, coffee and snacks on the water as you get to know fellow paddlers
8:00 AM — Paddle back
8:30 AM — Back at the beach. Done, go build something
Kolbe 15 is a hub just outside Toronto, built for founders and entrepreneurs, with events across the city. We create environments and experiences for those who crave depth over noise, and the lasting relationships that come with it.

properties.title: Boot Sequence: Contrast Therapy, Breathwork & Sauna
properties.description:
Most founders show up to Tech Week running on yesterday's cache — calendar bloat, half-finished decks, last week's stress still in the system. Boot Sequence is a 90-minute group reset to start the week sharp.
Guided contrast therapy and breathwork in a small group of founders, builders, and operators. The science is real: heat-cold cycling drops cortisol, regulates the nervous system, and pulls you out of fight-or-flight before the keynotes even start. Breathwork between rounds anchors it.
You'll leave with a clear head, a regulated nervous system, and a handful of new connections from people who started Tech Week the same way you did.
This is a paid event for $39. You can pay here or at the door.
What to expect
Group contrast therapy (large-format sauna + cold plunge tubs) guided by a world-class professional in breathwork
Breathwork between rounds to anchor the reset
Functional mocktails afterward — Lion's Mane, Ashwagandha, vitamins. Fuel, not depletion.
Bring swimwear. Towels provided. Phones go in lockers.
About the space
NRG Haus is Toronto's first social wellness club — a 5,000 sq ft Liberty Village space combining guided contrast therapy, functional beverages, IV recovery, and intentional social design under one roof. Inside: a 55-person sauna, four immersive cold-plunge tubs each paired with a 16-foot LED screen running ocean waves and icefields, a tea lounge with a water-vapour fireplace, and a zero-proof bar pouring adaptogenic drinks built around Lion's Mane, Ashwagandha, and vitamins instead of alcohol. Phones go in lockers. Be fully present. You leave grounded, energized, and clear-headed — not depleted.
[Instagram]

properties.title: From Concept to Manufacturing: Building Hardware in Canada
properties.description:
Hosted at NeuronicWorks Inc.’s Toronto facility, this half-day Toronto Tech Week event is designed for founders and innovators building hardware and connected products. The session offers a practical view of what it takes to move from early concept to manufacturable, scalable products in a Canadian context.
The event will feature sessions on concept-to-manufacturing decision-making, non-dilutive government funding pathways for hardware startups and intellectual property strategies tailored to physical product companies. A candid panel discussion with startup founders will explore the real challenges, and support systems involved in building technical products.
Attendees will also have the opportunity to tour NeuronicWorks’ manufacturing facility, covering prototyping, PCB assembly, and box build, and participate in complimentary one-on-one consultations with technical leads across hardware, firmware, software, industrial, and mechanical design.

properties.title: The Swedish Way: Capitalising on Culture and Self-Leadership
properties.description:
Over 25+ years and 5,000 clients across virtually every industry in Europe, we have learnt something that reshaped how we think about building technology: the teams that deliver the best products are not the ones with the best tools or the most rigid processes. They are the ones where people genuinely help one another succeed.

As a Swedish tech consultancy, now in Toronto, we are eager to share our thinking for Tech Week, not just what we do, but how we build products, organise teams without hierarchy and drive breakthroughs at the intersection of technology and culture.

At Netlight, we work without bosses, driven by mentorship, self-leadership and real collaboration. When our consultants join client engineering teams to design, build, and deliver technology, these principles spread: communication improves, ownership grows and teams become truly unified. Culture becomes not just words, but a genuine competitive advantage.

This event is for CTOs, engineering managers and senior tech leaders who recognise that their biggest delivery challenge isn’t always technical. Discover a different perspective, ask tough questions and connect with leaders who are thinking about the same things.

About Netlight
Netlight is an international digital consultancy, co-creating the future today since 1999. With the collective intelligence of 2,200 consultants, we help organisations transform ideas into reality across industries and borders. www.netlight.com

properties.title: Investor Readiness Bootcamp
properties.description:
If you’re planning to raise your first round in the next 3–6 months, this session is designed to get you investor-ready — by working directly on your company.
The Investor Readiness Bootcamp 2026 is a focused, one-day, in-person working session for pre-seed tech founders who already have an MVP or early traction and need clarity on how to position their company for investors.
This is not a lecture-based program.
Throughout the day, founders work directly on their own fundraising strategy using their laptops and a dedicated board space. The session is designed to help you structure decisions, refine your positioning, and identify risks before entering investor conversations.
WHAT YOU WILL WORK ON
By the end of the day, you will have:
• A structured fundraising strategy tailored to your company
• A refined investor narrative aligned with how investors think
• Clear decisions on valuation, structure, and investor targeting
• Identified key risks and gaps that could impact your raise
• A clearer understanding of how investors evaluate opportunities and make decisions
HOW THE DAY WORKS
This is a working session — not a lecture.
Each block is designed for you to actively work on your company using your laptop and a dedicated board space.
IMPORTANT
You will be working on your own company throughout the entire session — this is not a passive learning experience.
PRICING
The registration fee is per company (up to 2 founders) so teams can align on strategy together.
Thanks to the support of Global Startups, GSA Ventures, and Dentons, approximately 90% of the program cost is covered.
WHO THIS IS FOR
• Founders with an MVP or early traction
• Planning to raise capital in the next 3–6 months
• Looking for clarity before approaching investors
• Wanting to avoid common fundraising mistakes
AGENDA
09:00 – 10:30 | Structuring your raise
Define your funding strategy, investor profile, and timing.
Work through how investors will evaluate your company, including cap table implications and future rounds.

10:30 – 10:45 | Break

10:45 – 12:00 | Building investor materials
Work directly on your pitch and positioning.
Clarify how to communicate your business clearly, concisely, and in a way that aligns with investor expectations.

12:00 – 01:00 | Lunch + Legal perspective (Dentons)
Understand key legal decisions that impact your raise — applied directly to your company.

01:00 – 03:00 | Financial and deal structure
Work through valuation, funding instruments (SAFE, equity, convertible notes), and deal terms — applied to your case.

03:00 – 04:00 | Strategy review & feedback
Review your fundraising approach, identify gaps, and refine your next steps before engaging investors.
Supported by



We are part of Toronto Tech Week, a citywide celebration of the people building what’s next. From May 25–29, 2026, founders, investors, and builders come together for hundreds of community-led events across Toronto, connecting tens of thousands of people around Canadian tech. Torontotechweek.com

properties.title: How Startups Close Enterprise Deals Through Cybersecurity
properties.description:
Join OTH Security during Toronto Tech Week for a curated happy hour focused on one of the hardest parts of scaling a startup: selling into enterprise.
Cybersecurity is often treated as a hurdle late in the sales cycle, or overlooked entirely until it starts slowing deals down.
This is a relaxed, small-group gathering designed to bring together founders and senior leaders for candid conversations on how to approach security in a way that supports growth and builds trust with larger customers.
You will have the opportunity to connect directly with:
A former Fortune 500 Head of Procurement from one of the world’s most recognized brands, sharing firsthand insight into how large organizations evaluate and select emerging technology vendors.
Jeff Taylor, Co-Founder & CEO of OTH Security, who has helped early-stage companies build security programs from scratch and navigate enterprise sales
Conversations will centre around topics like:
What enterprise buyers and third-party risk management teams (TPRM) actually look for during vendor reviews
Where startups commonly get stuck in procurement, compliance, and data security requirements
How security influences deal velocity and outcomes
Practical ways to build credibility without slowing down your team
This is a small, curated gathering designed to encourage meaningful discussion and connection. It is best suited for founders and senior leaders actively selling into mid-market or enterprise customers.
Drinks and light refreshments will be provided.
Requests are reviewed and approved to help create an engaging and high-quality discussion for all attendees.

OTH Security - With over 60 years of collective cybersecurity expertise, our seasoned team provides hands-on, comprehensive guidance and services for highly-regulated industries — finance, healthcare, and beyond. Motivated by excellence, innovation and partnership, we deliver exceptional client experience, forming the cornerstone of our innovative cybersecurity solutions.

properties.title: The Human Edge: A conversation on AI and the future of work
properties.description:
Presented by Re-Work during Toronto Tech Week 2026
AI is moving faster than most people are ready for.
The productivity gains are real. But so are the consequences of that productivity. Since the industrial revolution, we’ve used productivity as a measure of the value and worth of an individual, it’s no wonder that the looming evolutionary threat of AI feels so scary for many modern workers. How do we respond to a future of work where productivity is achieved through agentic AI instead of human effort.
As a career and work wellbeing organization, we have thoughts.
In this curated and honest conversation we’ll discuss where work is heading and what that means for the people doing it. And the ways we’ll need to shift our thinking around work and productivity.
Join us for a keynote and facilitated discussion exploring the intersection of AI, automation, and human well-being. We'll get into the big picture and bring it back to what leaders and individuals can do to navigate the AI revolution in work.

About Re-Work
Re-Work is a Community First
We're a professional (and personal) development community helping people redefine work, ambition, and success on their own terms. We want you to get to a healthier, less stress-filled place with the thing we spend most of our lives doing: working. Move beyond burnout toward work that is meaningful and fulfilling as your fullest self. Through a proven process and framework, we help you craft a more sustainable and values-aligned relationship with work. Through workshops, training, storytelling, coaching and community spaces, Re-Work invites you to explore a healthier, more sustainable way of working and living.
Let’s build a future of work that works for you.

properties.title: Vertex Health Presents: The experience of raising early-stage VC fund for a health tech. A real life example and industry mixer.
properties.description:
Vertex Health presents CAPITAL AND COMMUNITY IN HEALTH TECH, a live showcase and an industry mixer for exploring the perspectives of early-stage entrepreneurs, investors and community builders on early-stage VC funding in Canada.
Hosted in collaboration with the Canada Startup Association, Toronto Tech Week, Toronto Metropolitan University and  Brampton Venture Zone this event brings together first-hand experience of founders, investors & community builders on raising capital for early-stage health tech startups in Canada.
Canada’s health-technology investment market staged a notable rebound in 2025, but the recovery was narrow with capital flowing into fewer companies, larger financings and later-stage opportunities. This reveals cautious optimism rather than a broad-based resurgence even as total disclosed funding in the industry reached approximately $1.3 billion in 2025, marking a sharp increase from the prior year.
With exit markets still constrained and early-stage financing tight, the Canadian health-tech ecosystem remains in a selective growth phase. For market watchers, the message is that opportunity exists, but it increasingly favours companies that can prove clinical relevance, economic value and a realistic path to liquidity. The sector’s next chapter will depend not just on innovation, but on execution.

properties.title: The Hidden Tradeoffs of AI: What’s Changing Inside Teams
properties.description:
Many organizations have figured out how to adopt AI. Fewer have figured out what it costs them.
Across teams moving faster with AI, the same tradeoffs keep surfacing: speed at the expense of depth, independence at the expense of connection, pace at the expense of absorption. Leaders are seeing it. Teams are feeling it. Most just aren't saying it out loud yet.
The good news is that these tradeoffs are navigable but only if you're willing to look at them honestly. The organizations getting this right aren't the ones moving fastest. They're the ones paying closest attention.
This session brings together leaders who are navigating these trade-offs in real time. We'll get specific about what's actually breaking down, what's working, and what it takes to build teams that are both AI-capable and genuinely resilient.
Panelists will be announced shortly.

properties.title: Women in STEM Leadership Accelerator Lab (Toronto Tech Week Event)
properties.description:
Women in STEM Leadership Accelerator Lab
Connect, Accelerate & Thrive

Why you want to join us?

You got the IT expertise and project management experience. You might even have the manager title✅.

Yet You are not recognized as a leader. You are spoken over at meetings. You are overlooked for a promotion...more than once. 

Or you are unsure what your next career step should be.
If you want to advance your career and influence in the male-dominated industries/ sectors but not sure what it takes?

We can help.

Understand your unique leadership voice, building visibility, and navigating complex workplace dynamics are critical.
Join Amy Au (Innovate You) and Hilda Gan (People Bright Consulting), experts in People Leadership and Confidence development, for an empowering Leadership Accelerator Lab during Toronto Tech Week 2026 designed specifically for Women in STEM leaders, professionals.

(All ticket price already include HST and processing fees.)
*******************************************************
What is a Lab?
A Lab is practical, interactive, and applied.
You will learn, explore, practice, and apply. And leave with clear, strategic actions you can use immediately.

We offer two half-day accelerator sessions, each focused on practical leadership development and real-world application.
In the Morning Accelerator Lab ( 9:00am - 12:00pm), participants will be introduced to the Fascinate® System, a research-based framework that helps individuals identify their unique communication style and strengths. Through guided exercises in real-world scenarios, participants will learn how to communicate with greater confidence, clarity, and influence. 
Pre-requisite: None
The Afternoon Accelerator Lab (1:30pm - 4:30pm) focuses on applying Fascinate Advantage on career navigation, advancement and leadership visibility.
Through interactive exercises, participants will explore how to navigate career pathways, amplify their leadership voices in real-world scenarios, build strong relationships with demanding stakeholders, bosses, and overcome challenges often faced in male-dominated industries/ sectors.
Participants who sign up for only the afternoon session are expected to have already learned about the Fascinate Advantage® System.
Participants may attend one or both sessions depending on their interests and needs.
Between sessions, attendees are invited to join a complimentary guided tour (12:00–1:00 PM) of an exhibition in the building.
Come ready to connect with other trailblazing women, accelerate your leadership growth, and thrive in your career.

All participants are invited to join Amy and Hilda for an optional social networking time after 4:30pm at a nearby restaurant.

properties.title: Backing Breakthroughs: Building and Investing in Deep Tech Companies
properties.description:
Overview
Deep tech investing requires more than capital. It demands conviction in science, sharp judgment on timing, and the ability to navigate uncertainty long before markets are obvious.
Presented by Torys LLP and the Schwartz Reisman Innovation Campus Ecosystem team, this investor lunch and fireside chat bring together leading voices across the deep tech investment spectrum—from formation-stage company builders to institutional Seed and Series A investors.
Through a candid fireside conversation, we’ll unpack how investors evaluate breakthrough technologies, where risk truly sits, and what it takes to turn scientific potential into venture-scale outcomes. This session is designed to surface how decisions are made before traction is visible—and what signals matter most along the way.

Who Should Attend
Founders building or exploring deep tech ventures
Researchers and scientists interested in commercialization
Operators working within technical or R&D-driven companies
Investors looking to better understand early-stage deep tech dynamics

Key Takeaways
You’ll leave with:
A clearer understanding of how investors assess technical risk vs. market risk
Insight into when to build a company vs. invest in one
A breakdown of what differentiates deep tech from traditional venture
Perspective on how formation rounds compare to institutional Series A
Clarity on the signals that matter most at different stages of company building
When a company should stay in university vs when it is ready to spin out
How to show customer traction when the tech isn’t ready for prime-time
The session includes a live panel and Q&A, followed by networking over lunch.

Speakers
Innovobot (IRV Fund): Neha Khera, Managing Partner
Neha Khera is the Managing Partner of IRV, Innovobot's deep tech fund, with over a decade of experience backing global success stories like ApplyBoard and Mejuri. A former co-founder of 500 Startups Canada and Partner at 2048 Ventures, she has a proven track record of scaling high-impact startups across North America. Beyond investing, Neha is a dedicated mentor and advocate for diversity in tech, holding engineering and MBA degrees from the University of Waterloo and Richard Ivey Business School.
Wittington Ventures: Zeeshan Ali, Partner
Zeeshan is a Partner at Wittington Ventures, where he leads the Innovation Fund, backing early-stage deep tech startups in healthcare and climate. Previously, he was Managing Director of the MaRS Investment Accelerator Fund (IAF), one of Canada’s most active seed-stage funds. At IAF, he led investments across enterprise software, healthcare, climate, and deep tech — working closely with founders to scale their companies, sharpen go-to-market strategies, and bring breakthrough technologies to market.
Celesta VC: Charles Lesperance, Investor
Charles Lespérance is a deep tech investor with more than 15 years of experience spanning venture capital, private equity, structured finance, and strategy. Before joining Celesta, he co-founded BDC’s $200 million Deep Tech Venture Fund, backing early-stage companies in quantum, AI, semiconductors, and robotics, with prior experience at PSP Investments and McKinsey & Company. He holds an MBA from Harvard and a mechanical engineering degree from McGill.
Multiverse Computing: Samuel Mugel, Chief Technology Officer (Moderator)
Expert in quantum computing and quantum machine learning. PhD jointly awarded by ICFO (Spain) and the University of Southampton (UK). Formerly Computational physicist at Cortirio (UK), Technical Director at The Quantum Revolution Fund, and Founder and CTO at Groundstate Consulting. Advisor for the McKinsey Tech Council and the Forbes Tech Council, and Mentor at the Creative Destruction Lab

Event Details
Date: May 25, 2026 
Time: 12:30 PM – 3:00 PM
Location: Schwartz Reisman Innovation Campus
Agenda: 
12:30 PM – 1:30 PM: Registration and networking lunch 
1:30 PM – 1:35 PM: Welcome remarks 
1:35 PM – 2:30 PM: Panel + Q&A 
2:30 PM – 3:00 PM: Networking

Register
Deep tech opportunities are shaped long before they’re obvious.
Join us for an inside look at how investors think, decide, and act at the earliest stages, and what it means for how you build or back the next generation of companies.
Spots are limited. Register now.

properties.title: The RAG Blueprint: Hands-On Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) Lab
properties.description:
Most AI tutorials teach you how to call an API.
They don’t teach you how to build systems that return reliable answers from your own data.
This workshop is different.
We’ll go beyond “chat with your docs” demos and build a production-grade Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) system—one you can actually debug, evaluate, and improve.
This is a hands-on, high-intensity session for developers and architects who want to understand how these systems really work.

🛠 What You’ll Learn (and Build)
We’ll break down RAG into the core components that actually matter:
End-to-End Architecture
From ingestion → chunking → retrieval → generation → evaluation
Smart Chunking
Why naive chunking fails—and how to structure data for better retrieval
Advanced Retrieval Strategies
Move beyond “top-k similarity” to get relevant, grounded answers
Evaluation That Isn’t Guesswork
How to measure answer quality instead of relying on vibes
Observability & Debugging
How to see where your pipeline breaks—and fix it
Hands-On Implementation
Build a working RAG pipeline step-by-step

🎯 Who This Is For
Frustrated Builders
Your RAG app works… sometimes
Engineers & Developers
You know Python/JS and LLM basics, but want real systems thinking
Solution Architects
You need to evaluate trade-offs across vector DBs, embeddings, and retrieval
Career Pivoters
Move from prompt engineering → building full AI pipelines

📦 What You’ll Walk Away With
A working RAG boilerplate you can reuse
A reference architecture you can explain and defend
A mental model for debugging poor outputs
A framework for improving answer quality and performance

Bring your laptop.
Let’s stop “chatting” with AI—and start building systems that actually work.

properties.title: What's Actually Working in Privacy-First Advertising in Canada
properties.description:
As privacy expectations evolve, many organizations are focused on compliance. But compliance alone isn't what will drive performance or build trust.
This session explores what's actually working in Canada right now, where strategies are falling short. We'll look at the growing gap between what companies say about privacy and what consumers actually understand, and why the companies that win won’t be the most compliant, but the most understandable and trustworthy.
Designed as a candid operator briefing with a focus on advertising privacy, this session will leave plenty of room for discussion and shared perspectives.

About the Digital Advertising Alliance of Canada

The Digital Advertising Alliance of Canada (DAAC) is a national non-profit that develops and administers self-regulatory programs to enhance transparency, accountability, and consumer trust in digital advertising. Learn more at https://youradchoices.ca/

properties.title: From Pilot to Clinic: Deploying Voice AI in Healthcare (Reliability, Compliance, ROI)
properties.description:
Phone calls still run healthcare, but clinics are overwhelmed, patients wait on hold, and front-desk teams burn out. This event brings together entrepreneurs, builders, and healthcare operators to explore how voice AI can agentically and measurably improve patient access.
What you'll experience
• Reliability patterns for voice agents (handoff, edge cases, fail-safes)

• Live demos of healthcare voice AI handling real call flows: booking/rescheduling, clinic FAQs, structured intake, and safe escalation to staff.
• Student entrepreneurship spotlight with NEXT Canada–style "build in public" learnings and practical advice for shipping in regulated environments.

Who should attend
• Clinic leaders, healthcare operators, and innovators exploring AI in access + operations
• Founders building applied AI products
• Investors and ecosystem partners supporting healthcare innovation

properties.title: Tested in Toronto: TTC Partnerships Driving Transit Innovation

The TTC is forging new partnerships with Toronto’s brightest innovators – start‑ups, academic institutions, and industry trailblazers. This collaboration model is a win‑win for the city, strengthening the local tech ecosystem while enabling the TTC to trial cutting‑edge transit solutions. properties.title: building an ai/ml model for beginners
properties.description:
Model you = beginner;

while (you.in(TorontoTechWeek))
  {
       you.attend(ML_Workshop);
       you.levelUp();
  }

Curious about AI and machine learning but not sure where to start? This hands-on workshop is designed specifically for non-technical and beginner audiences who want to understand how machine learning actually works and build a simple model themselves. Come hungry for both the knowledge and the dinner on us!

Hosted by Jacqline Geng, a financial engineer with experience in quant finance, fintech, consulting, AI, and hackathons, and Iris Guo, an AI Product Manager with extensive experience across big tech and startups (Zynga, Scale AI, Google X, Microsoft), this session bridges the gap between AI hype and practical understanding.

We’ll break down machine learning concepts in plain language, show you a live demo of a simple ML model, and walk through common real-world use cases, from finance and product to everyday AI tools you already use. No prior coding or math background is required.

In the second half of the workshop, you’ll get hands-on:
you’ll customize and tune model parameters yourself, experiment with inputs and outputs, and see how small changes affect model behavior. 

We’ll wrap up with a relaxed networking session so you can connect with others exploring AI from diverse backgrounds.

Agenda:
5-6:15pm - Intro & Enjoy Refreshments
6:15-7pm - ML Workshop & Demo
7-8pm - Hands-on ML Model Building Session
8-8:30pm - Mingling & Networking

What you’ll learn:
- What machine learning is (and isn’t)
- How a simple ML model works under the hood
- Common real-world AI/ML use cases
- How to interact with and tune a basic model
- How to think about AI tools as a non-technical builder

What to bring:
Just your curiosity (and a laptop, if you’d like to follow along).

Note: this workshop is open to professionals of all backgrounds (product managers, designers, consultants, founders, and startup operators) with little to no coding experience, as well as anyone curious about AI and ML.

About Rootly AI
Rootly AI is the AI-native on-call and incident response platform that provides proactive support to help Site Reliability Engineers resolve incidents faster, improve system resilience, and streamline on-call operations. It’s your always-on SRE copilot that automates root cause analysis and identifies patterns that drive continuous improvement—trusted by hundreds of companies like LinkedIn, NVIDIA, Replit, Elastic, Canva, Clay, Tripadvisor, and Shell.


Come demystify machine learning, build something real, and leave with a clearer mental model of how AI actually works, all during Toronto Tech Week.

properties.description:
A curated space dedicated to Black men in tech, aiming to connect with one another in an authentically Black male space: The Barberhood, to exist as we are and discuss topics important to us. An organic, unstructured mixer focused on community and authentic connection during Toronto Tech Week.

The tech industry moves fast, but real connection takes time.

Join us for Cut & Connect, an evening dedicated to Black men navigating the Toronto tech ecosystem. We're stepping out of the office and into The Barberhood - a space that has proven to be a pillar of community and authentic conversation - to connect, share experiences, and exist as we are.

This isn't your typical tech mixer. There are no rigid panels, no formal slide decks, and no forced networking. Instead, we’re hosting an organic, unstructured evening focused on:
Authentic Community: Meet other founders, builders, and professionals who share your journey.
Real Talk: A safe space to discuss the topics that matter most to us in a setting that feels like home.
Good Vibes: Light refreshments, music, and the best atmosphere in the city.
Whether you’re a seasoned executive, a first-time founder, or just starting your career in tech, come through to anchor your Toronto Tech Week in community.

properties.title: From Agency to In-House: Creative Conversation
properties.description:
You know that career question that lives rent-free in your head: agency, freelance, or in-house?

This talk won’t give you the answer, but it will be an honest download of what each path actually feels like from the inside. Hosted by Questrade and Friends of Figma, we’ll address the tradeoffs, the shapeshifting, the money conversations, and what it’s like to build something you're proud of inside a machine that's much bigger than you. We’ll also get into what’s changed—vibe-coding, Slack etiquette, happy hours—and what hasn’t.
After a keynote hosted by Caroline Leong (writer & strategist) and Roger Dario (designer, art director) of Questrade (formerly of Jam3/MediaMonks & Tendril), we’ll continue the conversation with both agency and in-house creatives in an open panel discussion. We’ll close out Toronto Tech Week with dinner and refreshments, and great conversation with talent from across the city. If you’re a creative, this event is for you.
The fine print: This is a 19+ event! To register, you must be 19 years of age or older.

Questrade is one of Canada’s most established fintechs, and home to a Creative Shop that thinks a little more like an agency than a traditional in-house team. Based in Toronto, the team works across brand, product, motion, and UX to make finance feel like it was made for real people.
🖤 About Friends of Figma Toronto

properties.title: AI-Native GTM Systems: How DoorDash is Building the Next Stack
properties.description:
What does it take to rebuild a go-to-market system from the ground up at DoorDash scale?

Join us during Toronto Tech Week for an inside look at how DoorDash’s GTM Engineering team is rethinking the foundation of sales, growth, and merchant experiences. We’re tackling a deeply complex problem space shaped by fragmented tools, acquisitions, and manual workflows—and transforming it into a unified, AI-native platform.

In this session, you’ll hear directly from engineering leaders and builders working on systems that power go-to-market across every segment we serve. We’ll go beyond high-level vision and dive into real technical challenges, live demos, and early explorations in AI-driven workflows—from intelligent merchant discovery to automating pre-sales processes.

This isn’t just about what we’ve built, it’s about what comes next. We’ll share how we’re embedding AI into core systems, standardizing experiences across segments, and moving toward a fully unified, AI-first GTM stack.

If you’re excited by solving ambiguous, high-impact problems, building 0→1 systems, and turning AI experimentation into production-grade products, we’d love to meet you.

properties.title: Build a Multi-Agent AI App on AWS - Hands On Workshop
properties.description:
Build a multi-agent AI app on Amazon Bedrock AgentCore, from first line of code to production deployment, in a single afternoon.
Hosted by the AWS Startup Team Canada at YYZ18 (18 York St, Toronto). Sponsored by Caylent.
Agenda
1:30 PM / Welcome
2:15 PM / Introduction (AWS + Caylent)
2:30 PM / Hands-on workshop (Nima Arki & Victor Fonne, AWS)
4:30 PM / Workshop wrap-up
4:45 PM / Production use case walkthrough (Caylent)
5:00 PM / Happy hour (light food and drinks)
7:00 PM / End
What you'll build
Three specialist agents (Brand Intelligence, Content Generation, Campaign Planning) coordinated via A2A protocol, each on its own AgentCore Runtime
Agents connected to external tools and analytics through MCP Gateway with Lambda-backed tools
Cedar policies enforcing guardrails at the gateway: live demos of policies blocking oversized recipient lists and quiet-hour sends that agents cannot bypass
Brand preferences and campaign history persisted across sessions with AgentCore Memory, plus sandboxed analytics via Code Interpreter
Cognito JWT for human users (Auth Code + PKCE) and M2M JWT via Token Vault for agent-to-agent calls
Agent quality evaluation with built-in evaluators, on-demand or continuous
Agent Registry for discovery and lifecycle governance
CDK deployment of the entire stack with an Amplify-hosted React frontend
What you leave with A reference architecture for production agentic apps on AWS: orchestration, tools, policy, memory, auth, and IaC in one stack.
What to bring Your laptop. Everything else is provided.
Who should attend Engineers, architects, and technical founders building or evaluating production AI agent systems.
Hosts AWS (Amazon Bedrock AgentCore) + Caylent (AWS Premier Tier Services Partner, multi-time AWS Partner of the Year)

properties.title: Bike from Toronto to Kolbe 15
properties.description:
Part of the Kolbe 15 Rain or Shine series. A ride that earns its finish line.
For those who know resilience is not built in a boardroom.
Before we spend a week indoors, this is how we kick off Tech Week. We meet at Finch Station at 7 am, clip in, and ride out of the city, ending at the Kolbe 15 founder hub.
A support vehicle rides alongside the whole way with snacks and water. It's there if you need it, but you won't want to use it.
When you arrive, jump in for a swim, then unwind in the sauna and hot tub. If you've still got energy in those legs, shoot pool, take a canoe out, or paddleboard on the water.
We eat lunch together, gather around the fire, and let the conversation go where it goes, the kind that happens naturally with people who get why we push ourselves.
We depart at 5pm, everyone gets a ride back to Toronto in a vehicle. You'll be back in the city by 7pm.
What's included: snacks and water along the ride, lunch, drinks and refreshments at the property, and full use of the property and its amenities.
The best way to start Tech Week.

Schedule
7:00 AM — Meet at Finch Station ready.
7:10 AM — Depart
~12:45 PM — Arrive at Kolbe 15
1:00 PM — Swim, lunch, unwind
3:30 PM — Fireside conversation
5:00 PM — Depart Kolbe 15
~7:00 PM — Back in Toronto
Ride Details
Distance: 110km
Format: Ride at your own pace 20–25 km/h
Safety
Call out hazards (holes, bumps, debris)
Obey all traffic signals and stop signs
Hold your line and ride predictably
Requirements
Road bike required
No city bikes or rentals
Comfortable riding long distance
Helmet required
Bring your own spare tube
Resilience Level: 6/10

properties.title: KindLogic Presents: Shadow AI vs. The Secure Stack - Grounding the 98% Reality of Unsanctioned AI
properties.description:
While leadership teams debate AI policy, their employees have already moved ahead. Recent data shows that 98% of organizations face "Shadow AI" which is unauthorized usage of public LLMs and unvetted tools that boost individual productivity but create massive governance, security, and data leakage risks. In this executive working session, Kind Logic’s co-facilitators Jordan Loewen-Colón, PhD (Queen's University) and Alonso Gutierrez (Systems Engineer, ex-Twitter/Huawei) strip away the theatrical jargon to address the reality of the modern workforce. What we will cover:  
- The Signal in the Shadow: Why your team is bypassing IT and what those "Shadow" tools tell you about your organization’s actual needs.  
- The Systems Reality: A technical look at how personal accounts and unvetted APIs interact with proprietary data, and why "blocking" is rarely the solution.  
- Building the Secure Stack: Practical frameworks for transitioning from "Ghost" usage to an institutional AI infrastructure that is both empowering and governed. 
- Beyond the Vibe Check: How to replace vague AI principles with rigorous, programmatic assessments that hold up in a real institution.   

This is a practical session for leaders who need to manage the AI that is already inside their building today. Hosted by KindLogic AI Labs: Bringing practical signal to executive decision-making.

https://kindlogic.ai/

properties.title: Built for Her: Women's Health, AI & the Future of Care
properties.description:
89% of women feel dismissed by their healthcare providers. Half the population. Centuries of data gaps. One question: what happens when AI finally listens?
Join HerSay for an evening of bold conversation, community, and connection at the intersection of women's health and technology. Featuring a keynote from HerSay Founder & CEO Rebecca Lyons, a curated panel of femtech founders, clinicians, and investors, and an open networking hour with a live app demo — this is the room where the women building the future of health come together.
This is not a panel about women. It's a panel by them.
Drinks, dialogue, and a demo. Come ready to connect.

properties.title: Cursor / Toronto Tech Week (Workshops + Hackathon)
properties.description:
Join Canada's First Largest Cursor Event in Canada
(Everyone gets free Cursor, Lovable, ElevenLabs & Composio credits 🥳)
AI isn't coming. It's already building. And Toronto is showing up.
We're bringing together 400+ individually vetted developers, designers, product managers, and founders for a full day during Toronto Tech Week.
The event is about building with AI using Cursor. Whether you write code, push pixels, ship product, or run a startup, there's a track with your name on it.

Agenda
9AM - Doors open
9 - 9:30AM - Coffee + Keynote
9:30AM - 12PM: Workshops for PMs, Engs, Founders and Designers (Full schedule here)
12-12:30PM: Lunch
12:30-4PM: Hackathon with prizes (Register here) (Only open to 120 participants)
This is not a sit-and-watch event. We've got six rooms running at the same time with hands-on workshops, spotlight talks, fireside panels, speed networking, a multi-track hackathon, co-founder matching, and sponsor office hours.
We welcome software developers, AI/ML engineers, data scientists, and designers. Technical founders and startup leaders based in Toronto. AI-curious PMs, professors, sales pros, and operators. Students working on real technical projects.
